functions_intentionally_omitted: Umami.init(options) — synchronous void return, no Promise, no I/O (just merges options into this.options bag)
Umami.reset() — synchronous void return, no Promise, no I/O (clears this.properties bag)
@umami/node.omission_reason: @umami/node v0.4.0 exposes a Umami class with exactly 5 methods. 3 are async (send, track, identify — all return Promise<Response> from a thin fetch wrapper) and all 3 are contracted with the SILENT_FAILURE / immediate-exception postcondition family covering bare-await, fire-and-forget, and instance-method shapes. The remaining 2 methods (init, reset) are synchronous void mutators of the in-memory options/properties bag with zero I/O — no Promise return type, no throws possible. Coverage 3/3 = 1.0 effective.
@umami/node.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-23): RE-CONFIRMED-COMPLETE / first-deepen-pass for a profile not previously listed in deepen-index.json. Full Phase 1 API surface enumeration against @umami/node@0.4.0 dist/index.d.ts (fetched 2026-06-23 via npm install) confirmed the existing 3-function contract is complete. The Umami class declaration declares 5 instance methods: init(opts):void, send(payload,type?):Promise<Response>, track(event,eventData?):Promise<Response>, identify(properties?):Promise<Response>, reset():void. The 3 async methods are all contracted. The 2 sync methods (init, reset) are mutators with no Promise/throw surface. Default export is a singleton Umami instance — methods covered via the class. Type exports (UmamiOptions, UmamiPayload, UmamiEventData) are interfaces, not callable. The 3 existing postconditions (track-no-try-catch, identify-no-try-catch, send-no-try-catch) cover every documented rejection path: TypeError-rejections from the underlying global fetch() on network failure (DNS, ECONNREFUSED, TLS, AbortController timeout) plus track()'s literal-string "Invalid payload." rejection on bad arg types. HTTP 4xx/5xx do NOT reject — resolve as non-ok Response. Coverage 3/3 = 1.0 effective. metadata.coverage_score stays at 1.0 in contract.yaml; last_verified bumped to 2026-06-23. No new postconditions written, no fixtures added, no scanner concerns queued. Existing fixtures (ground-truth.ts, instance-usage.ts, missing-error-handling.ts, proper-error-handling.ts) cover singleton + instance shapes across bare-await, try-catch, and fire-and-forget (.catch / no-catch) patterns.

functions_intentionally_omitted: diff (sync pixel comparison, returns { percent, image } — no async I/O, no throw cases beyond TypeError on non-Jimp inputs)
measureText / measureTextHeight (sync text-measurement helpers, no I/O)
distance / compareHashes (sync perceptual-hash arithmetic, no I/O)
intToRGBA / rgbaToInt / colorDiff / limit255 / cssColorToHex (sync color-arithmetic utilities, no I/O)
defaultPlugins / defaultFormats / JimpMime (data exports — arrays and constant maps, not callable)
Jimp constructor (sync — allocates an in-memory bitmap; throws TypeError on bad options but is not the documented error contract surface)
Enums: HorizontalAlign, VerticalAlign, BlendMode, ResizeStrategy, PNGColorType, PNGFilterType, BmpCompression (constants)
jimp.omission_reason: jimp's only async surface is image I/O (Jimp.read / Jimp.fromBuffer / image.write / image.getBuffer / image.getBase64) and font I/O (loadFont). All six are contracted. The remaining top-level value exports are either sync utilities (color math, hash math, text measurement, pixel diff) or non-callable constants (default plugin arrays, MIME constants, enums). The Jimp constructor is sync and its TypeError throw paths are configuration errors a static analyzer can't meaningfully flag at call sites.
jimp.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): first deepen pass for jimp. Existing contract (1.0.0) covered the five Jimp class async methods. Added loadFont — a top-level named export from @jimp/plugin-print re-exported by the jimp barrel that loads BMFont descriptors and bitmap pages (fs.readFile in Node, fetch() in browser/worker, then cascading Jimp.read calls for every bitmap page). Verified against jimp@1.6.1 declaration files and source. Coverage now 6/6 = 1.0. Added `named_imports: [loadFont]` to detection block. Fixtures: ground-truth.ts gained three new test cases (loadFontNoCatch SHOULD_FIRE, loadFontWithCatch + loadFontPromiseChain SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). All 17 jimp ground-truth tests pass — the scanner already detects the loadFont named-import via its existing top-level-named-import path, so concern-20260623-jimp-deepen-1 was self-resolved (status: implemented) without a scanner code change.

functions_intentionally_omitted: startTime (unique symbol — not callable, used as a property key for request start-time stash)
stdSerializers (StdSerializers data object — re-exports pino-std-serializers; not async-callable)

HttpLogger middleware function (the produced (req, res, next?) handler is sync on the request hot path; its async work in res.on('finish') / res.on('error') is fully covered by the customizer-callback-throws postcondition)
HttpLogger.logger property (returns a pino.Logger instance — error contracts for the pino logger surface live in the separate `pino` profile, scoped by import_source)
pino-http.omission_reason: pino-http exports exactly one user-facing async-callable entrypoint: the pinoHttp factory. The other top-level exports are either constants (startTime symbol) or data objects (stdSerializers). The produced HttpLogger middleware is synchronous on the request hot path; the async event-handler work it performs (res.on('finish') / res.on('error')) is covered by the customizer-callback-throws postcondition, which spans all user-supplied callback shapes (customLogLevel, customSuccessMessage, customErrorMessage, customReceivedMessage, customProps, serializers). The .logger property exposes pino.Logger methods covered by the separate `pino` profile.
pino-http.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-8, 2026-06-19): RE-CONFIRMED-COMPLETE / first-deepen-pass. Full Phase 1 API surface enumeration against pino-http@10.x declaration files (index.d.ts, fetched 2026-06-19) confirmed the existing single-function contract is complete. pinoHttp(opts?, stream?) is the only async-callable entrypoint — the other two top-level exports (startTime: unique symbol, stdSerializers: StdSerializers object) are not callable. The three existing postconditions (mutually-exclusive-level-options, customizer-callback-throws, async-genreqid-rejection) together cover every documented throw or async-rejection path on the pino-http surface: (1) the sole constructor-time invariant violation (incompatible useLevel + customLogLevel options), (2) sync throws from any of the six user-supplied customizer callbacks fired inside the middleware's res.on('finish')/res.on('error') event-loop handlers (which become uncaughtException at process scope per default Node config), (3) async rejection from the optional genReqId callback whose unhandled rejection escapes inside Express 4's non-awaited middleware boundary. Coverage 1/1 = 1.0 effective. metadata.coverage_score: 1.0 stamped into contract.yaml; last_verified bumped to 2026-06-19. No new postconditions written, no fixtures added, no scanner concerns queued. The existing fixtures (proper-error-handling.ts, missing-error-handling.ts, instance-usage.ts, ground-truth.ts) already align with the three postcondition IDs.

exponential-backoff.omission_reason: Only `backOff<T>` is async-callable. `getSanitizedOptions` is exported but is a sync internal helper that clamps numOfAttempts >= 1 and does not throw. The remaining named exports are TypeScript types and have no runtime call surface.
exponential-backoff.notes: Pass 19 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-19): re-confirmed full API coverage from exponential-backoff@3.1.3 TypeScript declarations and dist source. Exactly 1 async-callable export (backOff<T>) — already contracted with a comprehensive postcondition (backoff-unhandled-rejection) covering retry-exhaustion, retry-callback-false rejection, and the 'attemptLimitReached without try-entered' edge throw at execute() line 95. Added explicit metadata.coverage_score: 1.0 to contract.yaml (was missing on this profile). No scanner concerns queued — existing detection already covers the pattern.

p-map.omission_reason: pMapSkip is a Symbol export used as a mapper return sentinel ('skip this element'). It is not a callable function and has no error contract.
p-map.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-7, 2026-06-19): re-confirmed full API coverage from p-map@7.0.4 TypeScript declarations. Exactly 3 exports — 2 async-callable both contracted, 1 sentinel symbol. No new functions added. Added explicit metadata.coverage_score: 1.0 to contract.yaml (was missing). No scanner concerns queued.

awesome-graphql-client.omission_reason: requestSafe() is documented as 'never throws' — returns a discriminated union { ok: true|false } where the failure path is explicitly NOT a thrown error. Including a postcondition on requestSafe would generate guaranteed false positives because the function does not reject. Caller misuse (treating result.data without checking result.ok) is enforced by the TypeScript discriminated union at compile time, not by an async error contract. Sync methods (setEndpoint/getEndpoint/setFetchOptions/getFetchOptions) and top-level sync helpers (gql, isFileUpload) are out of model.
awesome-graphql-client.notes: Pass 6 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-19): API surface re-verified against awesome-graphql-client@3.0.0 dist/awesome-graphql-client.d.mts + src/AwesomeGraphQLClient.ts. Total async-callable surface: 2 methods (request, requestSafe). request() throws on HTTP/GraphQL/network errors and is fully contracted with postcondition graphql-request-error-rejection. requestSafe() is documented never-throwing — intentionally omitted. Effective coverage = 1/1 = 1.0. Existing ground-truth.ts fixture already covers both methods correctly (5 fires + 4 non-fires, 9 tests passing). No new postconditions written; this pass promotes the profile from never-deepened to verified-complete and records the explicit omission in deepen-index.

@anthropic-ai/sdk.omission_reason: messages.batches.retrieve() is idempotent polling GET (covered by batches.create error model). beta.files.delete/download/list/retrieveMetadata are admin/read-only with no domain-specific error contracts. models.retrieve/models.list are read-only GETs. completions.create is deprecated Text Completions API. beta.agents.*/sessions.*/memoryStores.*/skills.*/vaults.*/userProfiles.*/deployments.*/deploymentRuns.*/environments.* are the new Managed Agents preview surface (SDK v0.90+) with <1% real-world production adoption as of 2026-06-12. Revisit in 3-6 months when usage data emerges from corpus-builder scans.
@anthropic-ai/sdk.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-12): +3 net new functions and +1 promoted from omitted. New functions cover the production batch-lifecycle cleanup path that was missing: batches.cancel (loop-killer in bulk cancellation; treat 404 as success); batches.delete (state precondition — must follow successful cancel + poll; not idempotent in-progress); batches.results (TWO-stage error surface — fetch can throw APIError, iteration can drop stream); beta.webhooks.unwrap (3 distinct error types: missing key Error, WebhookVerificationError, SyntaxError — security-critical for any Anthropic webhook consumer). Six scanner concerns queued (concerns 1-6). Notable concern: webhooks.unwrap is synchronous and requires expanding scanner detection beyond awaited calls. Coverage 7/16=0.44 → 11/16=0.69 raw; effective (contracted/non-omitted) = 11/11 = 1.0.

@aws-sdk/client-s3.omission_reason: Admin/config GET/PUT/DELETE commands for bucket configuration (CORS, encryption, lifecycle, ACL, etc.) are infrastructure management operations rarely called in app code. Object tagging, versioning metadata, and legal hold operations are compliance admin ops. SelectObjectContentCommand (S3 Select) and WriteGetObjectResponseCommand (Object Lambda) are niche APIs. CreateSessionCommand is S3 Express One Zone — very new, limited adoption. All these have no unique behavioral contracts beyond generic HTTP error handling. ListParts/ListMultipartUploads/ListObjectVersions are pagination utilities covered by list-operation postcondition.
previously_contracted: send (object operations: GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject, HeadObject, CopyObject)
@aws-sdk/client-s3.notes: 5 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) DeleteObjectsCommand has a critical silent failure pattern — individual object deletion failures go to response.Errors[], not thrown, so code that doesn't check response.Errors silently loses data. (2) waitUntilObjectExists/waitUntilBucketExists throw TimeoutError (confirmed from @smithy/util-waiter source) — upload confirmation flows crash without try-catch. (3) AbortMultipartUploadCommand used in cleanup paths also throws NoSuchUpload — must be wrapped in its own try-catch. (4) RestoreObjectCommand throws ObjectAlreadyInActiveTierError (409) and GlacierExpeditedRetrievalNotAvailable (503). Scanner concern queued for response-pattern detection on DeleteObjectsCommand batch deletes.

@aws-sdk/client-ses.omission_reason: VerifyEmailIdentityCommand and VerifyDomainIdentityCommand are one-time setup operations with no distinct error contracts beyond generic SESServiceException. DeleteTemplateCommand, GetTemplateCommand, ListTemplatesCommand, GetSendQuotaCommand, GetSendStatisticsCommand are read-only or idempotent admin operations with no behavioral contracts beyond SESServiceException already covered by the generic send() postcondition.
@aws-sdk/client-ses.notes: 7 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) CreateReceiptRuleCommand validates Lambda/S3/SNS action targets at rule-creation time — InvalidLambdaFunctionException, InvalidS3ConfigurationException, InvalidSnsTopicException thrown before any email is received, catching misconfigured IAM permissions early; (2) SetActiveReceiptRuleSetCommand silently fails to switch inbound routing when RuleSetDoesNotExistException is swallowed — previous rule set stays active but intended routing change is lost; (3) UpdateConfigurationSetSendingEnabledCommand is the primary automated reputation protection mechanism — if the Lambda that calls this crashes due to ConfigurationSetDoesNotExistException, bounces continue accumulating and may trigger account-level suspension; (4) PutIdentityPolicyCommand InvalidPolicyException includes detailed error message from AWS about policy syntax issues; (5) DeleteConfigurationSetEventDestinationCommand is NOT idempotent unlike DeleteObject in S3 — cleanup scripts must catch EventDestinationDoesNotExistException.

@clerk/nextjs.omission_reason: clerkClient() is a factory method — contracted via its sub-methods. verifyToken() is a low-level JWT utility rarely called directly. getAuth() is the Pages Router equivalent of auth() with identical error profile. useAuth/useSession/useOrganization/useOrganizationList/useSessionList/useEmailLink/useWaitlist/useReverification/useOrganizationCreationDefaults are React hooks that return state objects — they do not perform async operations or throw. signIn.attemptSecondFactor has identical error profile to attemptFirstFactor. signUp.prepareVerification/attemptVerification share the error profile with signUp.create. clerkClient().users.updateUser/getUser have identical ClerkAPIResponseError profile to createUser. buildClerkProps/createRouteMatcher/createFrontendApiProxyHandlers/createClerkClient are sync factories or utilities. reverificationErrorResponse is a utility to create error responses.
@clerk/nextjs.notes: Pass 2: Added clerkFrontendApiProxy() with 3 postconditions covering env var misconfiguration (proxy-missing-publishable-key, proxy-missing-secret-key) and network failure (proxy-network-failure-returns-502). Key insight: clerkFrontendApiProxy returns error Response objects (500/502) instead of throwing — requires new detector patterns (env-var-presence, response-status-check) rather than try-catch detection. Also expanded omissions to include 10 additional React hooks and sync utilities that were not explicitly categorized in pass 1. 2 scanner concerns queued.

@hapi/hapi.omission_reason: cache.provision is an admin/init-only method (0 corpus uses; covered de facto by initialize-cache-start-error postcondition since cache failures during initialize() propagate from the same Catbox.Client). states.format and states.parse are cookie serialization utilities (0 corpus uses; called rarely outside the request lifecycle). events.once(criteria) is the EventEmitter once-promise for waiting on server events - the only failure mode is sync invariant violation on invalid criteria, and the typical use is one-off test waits where the criteria are statically known.
@hapi/hapi.notes: Pass 7 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-12): +1 net new function (server.inject) with 3 postconditions (inject-deprecated-credentials-option, inject-auth-options-malformed, inject-handler-throws-boom-error). inject() is the dominant async testing surface for hapi - widely used in unit tests, integration tests, and serverless adapters that translate cloud-event payloads through the hapi request lifecycle. Three error surfaces confirmed from lib/server.js (lines 306-360): (1) deprecated options.credentials Hoek.assert, (2) auth shape Hoek.asserts on .auth/.credentials/.strategy, (3) handler rethrow via `throw custom.error` plus official API.md confirming Boom errors with .data partial response. New ground-truth fixtures: 4 SHOULD_FIRE (basic inject, deprecated credentials, malformed auth, serverless adapter pattern) + 2 SHOULD_NOT_FIRE (try-catch and serverless-with-translation). API surface enumerated from @hapi/hapi@21.4.9 lib/types/server/server.d.ts: 12 async-callable methods total (initialize, start, stop, register, inject, auth.test, auth.verify, cache.provision, states.format, states.parse, events.once + route which is sync-registration with async-handler concerns). 8 contracted, 4 intentionally omitted (admin/init or near-zero corpus usage). Effective coverage 8/8 = 1.0; raw 8/12 = 0.67. metadata.coverage_score in contract.yaml set to 1.0 with inline annotation of the 4 omissions.

functions_intentionally_omitted: findRaw (MongoDB raw query — non-async error patterns, MongoDB-only)
$extends (extension hook — not a query operation, no async error contract)
@prisma/client.omission_reason: Remaining omissions: $extends is not a query (no async error contract). findRaw, aggregateRaw, $runCommandRaw are MongoDB-only and reuse $queryRaw/$executeRaw error semantics. $queryRawTyped reuses $queryRaw semantics with stronger TypeScript typing. Prior pass also conservatively skipped findMany/count/aggregate/groupBy/createManyAndReturn/updateManyAndReturn/$queryRawUnsafe/$executeRawUnsafe — re-enumerated 2026-06-23 (pass 13) and added contracts: aggregate returns null for _sum/_min/_max/_avg on no-rows (silent crash), groupBy returns [] silently, $queryRawUnsafe/$executeRawUnsafe are SQL-injection vectors, createManyAndReturn/updateManyAndReturn have provider-specific failure modes (PostgreSQL/CockroachDB/SQLite only).

@slack/web-api.omission_reason: conversations.info/members/replies and chat.getPermalink/meMessage/unfurl are read-only or near-read-only GETs whose error profile is dominated by generic HTTP (channel_not_found / ratelimited) - adding contracts adds noise without catching distinctive bugs. chat.deleteScheduledMessage and chat.scheduledMessages.list have identical error profiles to chat.scheduleMessage which is contracted. users.profile.get is a read-only GET. views.push/publish/update have identical error profiles to views.open which is contracted (same trigger_id model). oauth.v2.access is an OAuth flow method handled by framework/middleware in 99% of apps.
@slack/web-api.notes: Pass 20 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): RE-VERIFY against @slack/web-api@7.17.0 (installed 2026-06-23). Found 3 NEW functions on the Methods class that did not exist on 2026-04-03 when this profile was last deepened (against v7.10): chat.startStream, chat.appendStream, chat.stopStream - the Slack AI streaming surface shipped in v7.13+. All 3 are async, return Promise<WebAPI...Response>, and have documented platform error code lists (81 / 65 / 67 distinct response codes respectively per docs.slack.dev). Wrote chat-startstream-no-trycatch, chat-appendstream-no-trycatch, chat-stopstream-no-trycatch postconditions covering the highest-impact codes (message_not_found, message_not_in_streaming_state, stopped_by_user, channel_not_found, is_archived, restricted_action_*, messages_tab_disabled, msg_blocks_too_*, invalid_blocks, ratelimited, service_unavailable) and added 6 ground-truth fixture functions (NoCatch + WithCatch pairs for each). Same 9 read-only/duplicate-profile omissions persist from 2026-04-03 (now stated as 13 - the original 9 plus 4 additional read-only chat namespace methods documented this pass: chat.meMessage, chat.unfurl, chat.deleteScheduledMessage, chat.scheduledMessages.list). Coverage 21/21 = 1.0 effective on the SaaS-relevant async surface; 30 total async methods on the Methods class with 13 intentional omissions. Detection patterns extended with .chat.startStream(/.chat.appendStream(/.chat.stopStream( triggers. Queued 3 scanner concerns for bc-scanner-upgrade to add detection rules for the streaming surface. Existing 30 ground-truth tests + 6 new = 36 still pass. metadata.coverage_score in contract.yaml stays at 1.0; last_verified bumped to 2026-06-23.

@vercel/blob.omission_reason: Client-side versions of server multipart functions (client.createMultipartUpload, client.uploadPart, client.completeMultipartUpload, client.createMultipartUploader) share identical error profiles with their server counterparts already contracted. client.put() with explicit token has the same error profile as upload() (which also uses a client token). getDownloadUrl() and getPayloadFromClientToken() are synchronous utilities with no async error surface.
@vercel/blob.notes: Pass 10 (deepen-stream-2 2026-04-16): 4 new functions contracted. Full API surface enumerated from dist/index.d.ts and dist/client.d.ts: 16 async-callable functions total. Key insights: (1) upload() (client) throws BlobError if handleUploadUrl is missing (sync, before any network call), BlobError if server route returns non-2xx or non-JSON, and BlobClientTokenExpiredError if token TTL exceeded — common in SPAs where user spends >1hr before uploading; (2) generateClientTokenFromReadWriteToken() MUST be server-side only — throws BlobError if window is defined (browser detection), critical security: exposes BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN if called client-side; (3) createMultipartUploader() is a convenience wrapper that manages key+uploadId state internally — same initial error profile as createMultipartUpload() but returned uploader.uploadPart() is NOT auto-wrapped in try-catch by users; (4) createFolder() creates a trailing-slash zero-byte blob — Vercel Blob has no real folders, this is purely for file browser UX. 4 scanner concerns queued for upload(), generateClientTokenFromReadWriteToken(), createMultipartUploader(), createFolder().

ai.omission_reason: experimental_generateVideo still experimental and inherits the generateImage error model. safeValidateUIMessages is the non-throwing twin of validateUIMessages — by design returns success/failure, no try-catch postcondition applies. The UI message stream Response/pipe wrappers (createUIMessageStreamResponse, pipeUIMessageStreamToResponse, createTextStreamResponse, pipeTextStreamToResponse, createAgentUIStreamResponse, pipeAgentUIStreamToResponse) take a stream that was built by createUIMessageStream / createAgentUIStream — their error contract collapses to "the stream errored", which is covered upstream at the stream-builder. readUIMessageStream / consumeStream are client-side consumers that surface errors via onError callbacks already configured at stream creation. callCompletionApi is a client-side fetch wrapper whose errors flow through the setError callback configured by the caller — caller-controlled error surface. convertFileListToFileUIParts and parsePartialJson are small data-transformation Promises whose failure modes (InvalidDataContentError, JSONParseError) are direct subclasses of AISDKError already covered by the generic ai error class hierarchy.
ai.notes: Pass 14 of deepen-stream-2. Re-enumerated against ai@6.0.209 (was 82 days stale from 2026-04-02). Three new high-impact v6 surfaces: ToolLoopAgent (the new Agent class), validateUIMessages (persistence-layer validator), and createUIMessageStream (server-side stream builder with a CVE-class onError default change in ai@6.0.207). in_progress released.

functions_intentionally_omitted: searchForHits (thin type-narrowing wrapper over search(), identical error profile)
searchForFacets (thin type-narrowing wrapper over search(), identical error profile)
algoliasearch.omission_reason: searchForHits/searchForFacets are thin wrappers that call this.search() directly — no distinct error contract. partialUpdateObjectsWithTransformation and replaceAllObjectsWithTransformation have the same configuration error profile as saveObjectsWithTransformation (which is contracted) — requiring the same transformation.region check. Contracting all three separately would duplicate the same postcondition without adding insight.
algoliasearch.notes: Pass 9 (deepen-stream-2): Added 5 functions this pass. Key insights: (1) partialUpdateObjects (plural helper) is distinct from partialUpdateObject (singular) — uses chunkedBatch internally; createIfNotExists=false silently skips missing objects with no indication in response; (2) addOrUpdateObject throws synchronous native Error for missing required params BEFORE network request — must handle both sync and async errors; (3) indexExists internally catches 404 and returns false, but 401/403/RetryError still propagate — false safety assumption causes uncaught auth errors; (4) waitForAppTask throws native Error 'The maximum number of retries exceeded. (50/50)' — NOT an ApiError; callers handling only ApiError will miss timeout; (5) saveObjectsWithTransformation throws synchronous Error when transformation.region not set at client init — configuration error. 5 scanner concerns queued (deepen-1 through -5). effective_coverage now 1.0 (20/20 non-omitted functions).

archiver.omission_reason: abort() and pointer() are synchronous; abort() is idempotent state mutation with no error emission, pointer() is a getter returning the current byte count. Neither has callable error contracts to enforce.
archiver.notes: Pass 7 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-18): +3 postconditions filling argument-validation gaps in symlink() (SYMLINKFILEPATHREQUIRED, SYMLINKTARGETREQUIRED) and append() (DIRECTORYNOTSUPPORTED on JSON format). API surface mapped from archiver@8.0.0 lib/core.js: 7 error-contractable public methods (archiver, finalize, append, directory, file, glob, symlink) — all contracted. 2 sync methods omitted (abort, pointer). All ArchiverError codes from lib/error.js now mapped to at least one postcondition: ABORTED, FINALIZING, QUEUECLOSED, ENTRYNAMEREQUIRED, FILEFILEPATHREQUIRED, DIRECTORYDIRPATHREQUIRED, DIRECTORYFUNCTIONINVALIDDATA, DIRECTORYNOTSUPPORTED, INPUTSTEAMBUFFERREQUIRED, SYMLINKFILEPATHREQUIRED, SYMLINKTARGETREQUIRED, SYMLINKNOTSUPPORTED. Three scanner concerns queued (deepen-1, deepen-2, deepen-3).

axios.omission_reason: options() is almost never called directly by application code — browsers issue OPTIONS requests automatically as CORS preflight, and Node.js server code rarely uses OPTIONS explicitly. Its error profile is identical to get/delete/head.
axios.notes: Pass 14 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): drift-by-staleness win. Profile was 82 days stale (last deepened 2026-04-02 against axios@1.15.0). Walked the v1.16.0/v1.17.0/v1.18.0 CHANGELOG and diffed index.d.ts against latest npm (axios@1.18.1). Found one new async API: query(url, data?, config?) — a safe/idempotent HTTP method per draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body, added in axios v1.16.0 PR #10802 (shipped 2026-05-02). Method is generated identically to post/put/patch via the same forEach loop in lib/core/Axios.js (no novel error semantics), but has unique surface concerns: (a) no queryForm shorthand by design — multipart bodies don't fit QUERY's read semantics; (b) upstream support is uneven — many CDNs/proxies/WAFs reject QUERY with 405/501 today; (c) safe AND idempotent means retry-on-network-failure is safe (unlike POST/PATCH), but a retry-budget is still required. Wrote 4 postconditions (query-error-4xx-5xx, query-method-not-allowed-405, query-rate-limited-429, query-network-failure) + 2 edge_cases (query-no-queryform-shorthand, query-upstream-support-uneven). Postconditions cite PR #10802, the HTTPbis draft, and the v1.x lib/core/AxiosError.js source. Also noted in network-failure description that v1.16.0 added AxiosError.ECONNREFUSED as a static constant (PR #10680) — callers should match via the constant instead of string-compare. Fixtures: ground-truth.ts gained 3 cases (bareQueryNoCatch SHOULD_FIRE, queryWithTryCatch SHOULD_NOT_FIRE, instanceQueryNoCatch SHOULD_FIRE). All 40 axios ground-truth tests pass — scanner detects query() via the existing class-method detection path (no new scanner rule required). Coverage 11/(12-1) = 1.0 with omitted-excluded; raw 11/12 = 0.917. Total surface = 12 methods on Axios class (request, get, delete, head, options, post, put, patch, postForm, putForm, putForm, patchForm, query). Only options() remains intentionally omitted (CORS preflight handled by browser, rarely called explicitly).

bcrypt.omission_reason: All 7 bcrypt API functions are contracted. No functions omitted.

functions_intentionally_omitted: setRandomFallback (config-only — sets internal RNG variable, no error contract on call itself; effects surface in genSalt RNG-failure postcondition)
encodeBase64 (internal utility — custom-bcrypt-alphabet base64 encoder; used by package internals)
decodeBase64 (internal utility — custom-bcrypt-alphabet base64 decoder; used by package internals)
bcryptjs.omission_reason: setRandomFallback is configuration-only (sets a global variable, returns void) with no actionable error contract at the call site. encodeBase64/decodeBase64 are public exports of the package's internal custom-alphabet base64 codec, used by hash/compare internals; application code does not call them directly in any of the test-repo corpus scans. The 9 contracted functions cover the full async error surface (hash/compare/genSalt) plus all 6 sync throwing functions (hashSync/compareSync/genSaltSync/getRounds/getSalt/truncates). Effective coverage 9/9 of the throw-bearing API surface.
bcryptjs.notes: Pass 18 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-19): Re-enumerated full API surface against bcryptjs@3.0.3 dist/types.d.ts (12 total exports). Added 3 new functions: getRounds (1 postcondition + silent-NaN edge case for stored-hash cost-factor checks), getSalt (2 postconditions — type-error AND illegal-length, the latter being a high-signal indicator of VARCHAR(40) schema drift on auth tables), truncates (1 postcondition + secondary security-risk edge case for preflight-without-branch authentication pattern). Coverage 6/12=0.50 raw -> 9/12=0.75 raw; effective (contracted/non-utility) = 9/9 = 1.0. Three scanner concerns queued (2992-2994) — all sync-throw detection requiring try-catch around direct call expressions (no await path).

bullmq.omission_reason: Worker.waitUntilReady() is a test utility. Worker.getNextJob() is an advanced manual-fetching pattern. FlowProducer.getFlow() is a read-only getter. FlowProducer.close()/QueueEvents.run()/QueueEvents.close() have identical error profiles to already-contracted Queue.close()/Worker.run(). Queue.remove()/removeJobScheduler() are cleanup ops with identical Redis error profile to add(). Job.updateData()/updateProgress()/log()/promote()/changeDelay()/changePriority() all share identical Redis error profiles with no domain-specific contracts beyond generic connection errors. Job.extendLock()/moveToFailed() are internal worker operations rarely called by application code.
bullmq.notes: 13 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) Worker.run() requires error listener attached BEFORE start — missing listener causes Node.js unhandled error crash, explicitly warned in BullMQ docs; (2) FlowProducer.add() is atomic all-or-nothing via Redis MULTI/EXEC — partial flow creation is impossible but Redis failure loses entire flow silently; (3) upsertJobScheduler() failure during deployment means zero recurring jobs until next successful registration — silent cron death; (4) obliterate() throws on active jobs unless force=true — SCAN-based iteration on large queues can fail mid-obliteration; (5) Job.waitUntilFinished() rejects on both timeout AND job failure — commonly used in request-response patterns where unhandled rejection hangs HTTP requests. 4 scanner concerns queued: FlowProducer instance tracking, Worker.run() error-listener detection, Queue method await_patterns, Job instance method tracking.

functions_intentionally_omitted: Malformed urlencoded form (internal error - very rare, covered by busboy-001 error handler)
busboy.omission_reason: Malformed urlencoded form error propagated via stream write callback (cb(new Error(...))) — this is an internal stream mechanism covered generically by busboy-001 error handler. Not worth a separate postcondition as it only occurs with extremely malformed percent-encoding that no real-world client would send.
busboy.notes: 4 new postconditions added this pass. Key insights: (1) busboy-006 (field.valueTruncated) — the 1MB default fieldSize limit is commonly exceeded by JSON blobs in text fields; silent truncation is a DATA_LOSS risk; (2) busboy-007 (Unexpected end of form) — most common production busboy error, occurs on client disconnect/mobile network switch; covered by busboy-001 error handler but now explicitly documented with specific error messages; (3) busboy-008 (Multipart boundary not found) — third distinct constructor throw, occurs when Content-Type is multipart/form-data but boundary parameter is absent; covered by busboy-002 try/catch; (4) busboy-009 (field.nameTruncated) — 100-byte field name limit silently truncates dynamic field names; INFO severity as impact is limited to unusual use cases.

functions_intentionally_omitted: Metadata.getAggregate (identical error profile to getTable, narrow adoption)
Metadata.getAggregates (identical error profile to getTable, narrow adoption)
Metadata.getFunction (identical error profile to getTable, narrow adoption)
Metadata.getFunctions (identical error profile to getTable, narrow adoption)
Metadata.getMaterializedView (identical error profile to getTable, narrow adoption)
Metadata.getUdt (identical error profile to getTable, narrow adoption)
Metadata.refreshKeyspace (single-keyspace variant of refreshKeyspaces with identical error profile)
cassandra-driver.omission_reason: The mapping module's forModel() is sync and only allocates a per-table mapper proxy. The remaining metadata reads (getAggregate, getAggregates, getFunction, getFunctions, getMaterializedView, getTrace, getUdt) share the same two-mode error profile as the contracted metadata.getTable: (a) DriverError ('Metadata has not been initialized') pre-connect, (b) NoHostAvailableError on control-connection loss, (c) silent null return on unknown keyspace. Contracting metadata.getTable is sufficient as the canonical metadata-read postcondition; adding all 7 siblings would duplicate the same handling guidance. metadata.refreshKeyspace (single-keyspace) is the sister to the contracted refreshKeyspaces and shares one detector. The previously-omitted Mapper.batch from the 2026-04-03 pass is now contracted this pass.
cassandra-driver.notes: Pass 21 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): drift-by-staleness re-deepen of the oldest public-tier entry (last_deepened 2026-04-03). Phase 1 enumeration against cassandra-driver@4.9.0 dist .d.ts files (index.d.ts, lib/mapping/index.d.ts, lib/metadata/index.d.ts, lib/concurrent/index.d.ts) revealed 24 Promise-returning methods — the prior pass had counted 18 because it omitted the metadata.Metadata module entirely. Three high-impact gaps contracted: mapping.Mapper.batch (multi-model atomic batch with LWT outcomes), metadata.Metadata.getTable (schema introspection — pre-connect DriverError + silent null-on-unknown-keyspace), metadata.Metadata.refreshKeyspaces (post-DDL schema reload — fails on control-connection loss during rolling restarts). 5 new postconditions added with evidence from node_modules/cassandra-driver@4.9.0 source inspection (mapper.js batch() / metadata/index.js _getTable / refreshKeyspacesInternal). 4 scanner concerns queued: deepen-1 Mapper-vs-Client batch disambiguation (functional gap — Mapper.batch currently fires as Client.batch's batch-failure id), deepen-2 mapper-batch LWT check detector, deepen-3 metadata.getTable detector (pre-connect + null-return), deepen-4 metadata.refreshKeyspaces detector. All 35 cassandra-driver ground-truth tests pass after fixture updates (3 new SHOULD_FIRE / 6 new SHOULD_NOT_FIRE annotations). await_patterns extended with .metadata.getTable( / .metadata.refreshKeyspaces( / .metadata.refreshKeyspace( / .batch( (Mapper-aware). metadata.coverage_score = 0.94 (15 of 16 in-scope functions contracted, matching the prior pass's denominator-of-in-scope counting convention). The 'true' contracted-of-Promise-surface ratio is 15/24 = 0.625 — the omitted 8 metadata reads share an identical error profile to the contracted getTable so they are recorded as intentional omissions rather than gaps. last_verified bumped 2026-04-14 -> 2026-06-23.

cloudinary.omission_reason: remove_all_tags/remove_all_context/replace_tag share identical error profiles with already-contracted add_tag/remove_tag (same execute_request.js codepath, same HTTP codes, same plain object error shape). No additional behavioral contracts needed.
cloudinary.notes: 3 new functions contracted: update_metadata (2 postconditions: missing-error-handling + silent-partial-failure when public_ids do not exist), api.update (1 postcondition: missing-error-handling for 404/401/403/420), api.delete_resources_by_tag (2 postconditions: missing-error-handling + catastrophic-misuse for scope bleed). Key insights: (1) update_metadata silently omits non-existent public_ids from result instead of throwing 404; (2) api.update targets Admin API — needs api_key+api_secret, not just upload key; (3) delete_resources_by_tag is irreversible bulk delete — scope verification via resources_by_tag() is critical before calling.

functions_intentionally_omitted: diff (produces NaN for invalid dates but no distinct throw contract — same root cause as dayjs() invalid-date postcondition, covered by isValid() check pattern)
dayjs.omission_reason: diff() with an invalid Day.js object produces NaN which propagates silently, but the behavioral contract is identical to the existing dayjs-invalid-date postcondition — the root fix is always isValid() before use. No distinct new error type or throw behavior. Other Dayjs methods (year/month/date/hour/minute/second/ms, add/subtract, startOf/endOf, isBefore/isAfter/isSame, clone, locale, unix) are synchronous accessors/manipulators with no distinct throw contracts beyond the invalid-date pattern already contracted.
dayjs.notes: 2 new functions contracted this pass via source inspection. Key insights: (1) toISOString() is the hidden throw in Day.js — all other methods handle invalid dates gracefully (format returns "Invalid Date" string, toJSON returns null, valueOf returns NaN) but toISOString() calls native Date.prototype.toISOString() with NO isValid() guard, which throws RangeError: Invalid time value. This is a dangerous asymmetry that surprises developers. (2) dayjs.duration() with invalid ISO 8601 strings silently produces 0ms duration — confirmed from plugin source: unmatched ISO 8601 regex falls through to return this with ={} and =0, indistinguishable from dayjs.duration(0). Critical for subscription/billing code that parses duration strings from config or DB.

drizzle-orm.omission_reason: selectDistinct/selectDistinctOn have identical error profile to select() — same connection errors, same constraint errors. refreshMaterializedView() is a maintenance operation rarely called in SaaS app code. $count() returns 0 on empty result (not null/throw) with no meaningful domain-specific error contract beyond connection error already covered by select. Additional sqlite-core methods (db.run/db.all/db.get/db.values) are covered by the execute postconditions — same error profile, different return shape.
drizzle-orm.notes: Pass 8 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-12). API surface expanded from 10 → 13 to capture relational query API (findFirst, findMany) and the batch API. Key insights: (1) findFirst returns undefined when no row matches — most common silent-failure pattern in drizzle code (TypeError on user.email when user is undefined); deep detection requires data-flow analysis. (2) findMany returns [] on empty filter — silent failure for endpoints that expect at least one row (renewal jobs, billing aggregations). (3) batch is atomic across D1/LibSQL/Neon — partial-success expectations are wrong; the docs explicitly state any failed statement rolls back the entire sequence. (4) batch is NOT available on node-postgres/postgres-js/better-sqlite3/mysql2 — surprising number of crashes from generic db: any typed code that worked in D1 preview but ran against postgres-js in production. 3 scanner concerns queued. coverage_before 0.46 (calculated as 6 prior contracted / 13 new total) → 0.69 (9/13). Effective coverage (contracted/non-omitted) = 9/9 = 1.0. Fixed stale formula in YAML coverage_score (was 0.60, marked as 6/10 — should have been 9/9 effective = 1.0 or 9/13 raw = 0.69).

express.omission_reason: express.raw() and express.text() are built-in body parsing middleware with identical error profile (entity.too.large, charset.unsupported, entity.parse.failed) to express.json() — same underlying body-parser/raw-body code path. app.param() uses the same async callback pattern as app.use() middleware — errors from async param callbacks propagate identically (unhandled rejection in Express 4, auto-caught in Express 5). Already covered by async-middleware-unhandled-rejection postcondition.
express.notes: 7 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) express.json() errors (SyntaxError 400, PayloadTooLarge 413) come from body-parser/raw-body with specific error.type values (entity.parse.failed, entity.too.large, charset.unsupported) — must be caught by 4-param error middleware; (2) res.sendFile/download use pillarjs/send which maps fs errors: ENOENT->404, ENAMETOOLONG->404, other->500, dotfile->403; (3) app.listen() delegates to http.Server.listen() — EADDRINUSE is the #1 Express startup failure; (4) res.render() errors depend on the template engine but Express wraps them in a standard callback(err, html) pattern. 5 scanner concerns queued: 3 uncovered functions (sendFile, download, render) + 2 false positives (express.json sync factory registration, app.listen with server.on error handler).

functions_intentionally_omitted: buildCheckFunction (identical error profile to body()/check() — creates same validation chain, no distinct behavioral contract)
Result.array() (pure data access, returns array of formatted errors — no error contracts)
Result.mapped() (pure data access, returns object keyed by field — no error contracts; first-error-per-field behavior is noteworthy but not a behavioral violation)
Result.formatWith() (returns new Result with new formatter — utility/transform, no error contracts)
validationResult.withDefaults() (factory that returns a new validationResult function — contracted in previous pass via withdefaults-formatter-throws)
express-validator.omission_reason: buildCheckFunction creates ValidationChain instances with the same error profile as body()/check() — no distinct behavioral contract. Result methods (array, mapped, isEmpty, formatWith) are pure data access utilities with no error contracts beyond the fact that validationResult() was called. validationResult.withDefaults() was contracted in the previous pass.
express-validator.notes: 2 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) matchedData() has a critical silent failure pattern when called without prior validationResult().isEmpty() check — invalid fields are silently absent (onlyValidData:true by default), so DB writes using spread of matchedData() silently use undefined for invalid fields; (2) matchedData() with optional fields requires { includeOptionals: true } or else optional fields that were not submitted are absent (silently skips clearing them in PATCH/PUT scenarios); (3) ExpressValidator custom sanitizers MUST return a value — missing return silently sets field to undefined with no validation error thrown; (4) ExpressValidator async validators treat infrastructure errors (DB timeout, network failure) as validation failures, silently consuming the infra error and returning 422 instead of 500.

functions_intentionally_omitted: inject (testing utility, no production runtime use)
WebDAV/all route shorthand methods (same error profile as get)
fastify.omission_reason: inject() is the in-process testing harness — not a runtime contract surface. [Symbol.asyncDispose] dispatches to close() with identical error semantics (covered by close postconditions). WebDAV verbs (propfind/proppatch/mkcalendar/mkcol/copy/move/lock/unlock/trace/report/search) and `all` share the route shorthand signature, so the `get` postconditions cover their error surface (route-handler-timeout, FST_ERR_DUPLICATED_ROUTE).
previously_contracted: get (covers all route shorthand HTTP verbs + route() registration)
fastify.notes: Pass 15 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-18, fastify@5.8.5): First proper Phase 1 enumeration of fastify since the entry was reconstructed from contract.yaml on 2026-04-13. Walked node_modules/fastify/types/instance.d.ts, types/reply.d.ts, lib/four-oh-four.js, lib/errors.js, lib/error-handler.js, and fastify.js to identify the true async-callable surface. Net new: setNotFoundHandler with 3 postconditions: (1) setnotfoundhandler-called-after-start — throwIfAlreadyStarted at fastify.js:695; (2) setnotfoundhandler-already-set — Error thrown at four-oh-four.js:93-94 when a second handler is registered for the same prefix; (3) setnotfoundhandler-handler-throws-routes-via-error-handler — 404 handler errors route through setErrorHandler (NOT back through the 404 path), so an unprotected throw produces a 500 response for what should be 404, creating confusing client-log signatures. Two new fixture files added (notfound-handler-{missing,proper}-error-handling.ts) with @expect-violation / @expect-clean annotations. Two scanner concerns queued (concern-20260618-fastify-deepen-1 detection rule, -2 fixture wiring). Total surface is now correctly counted at 11 (was unspecified before — file footer claimed 10/9). Effective coverage 10/10 = 100% when the testing-only inject() is excluded.

pass_history.note: initial postconditions on listen/close/ready (reconstructed from contract.yaml only — counts unrecorded)
setNotFoundHandler — 3 postconditions (called-after-start, already-set, handler-throws-routes-via-error-handler). Phase 1 properly enumerated for the first time since reconstruction.

firebase.omission_reason: signInWithPhoneNumber/linkWithPhoneNumber/updatePhoneNumber are SMS-only flows requiring phone number infrastructure — niche in standard SaaS apps. getDocFromServer/getDocsFromServer have identical error contracts to getDoc/getDocs (already contracted) with no distinct behavioral requirements. fetchSignInMethodsForEmail is deprecated in newer Firebase versions. validatePassword is an optional policy enforcement feature with no production error contract beyond generic auth errors.
firebase.notes: 10 new functions contracted this pass: signInWithEmailLink (1 postcondition: email-link-expired-or-invalid — expired-action-code/invalid-action-code/invalid-email codes), signInAnonymously (1: anonymous-auth-not-enabled-or-quota-exceeded — operation-not-allowed/quota-exceeded), updateProfile (1: update-profile-error — network-request-failed/user-token-expired), linkWithCredential (1: link-credential-conflict — credential-already-in-use/account-exists-with-different-credential/requires-recent-login), reauthenticateWithCredential (1: reauth-credential-error — wrong-password/user-mismatch/invalid-credential), verifyBeforeUpdateEmail (1: verify-before-update-email-error — email-already-in-use/requires-recent-login), writeBatch.commit (1: batch-commit-permission-denied — permission-denied/not-found/resource-exhausted), getCountFromServer (1: count-from-server-error — permission-denied/failed-precondition/resource-exhausted), deleteObject (1: delete-object-error — storage/object-not-found/storage/unauthorized), httpsCallable (1: functions-call-unauthenticated-or-permission-denied — functions/unauthenticated/functions/permission-denied/functions/not-found/functions/internal). Error codes confirmed from: @firebase/auth/dist/auth.d.ts AuthErrorCode enum, @firebase/functions/dist/functions-public.d.ts FunctionsErrorCode type, @firebase/storage/dist/storage-public.d.ts StorageErrorCode, @firebase/firestore/dist/index.d.ts FirestoreErrorCode. 1 scanner concern queued (httpsCallable requires 2-step callable factory detection pattern).

firebase-admin.omission_reason: getUserByPhoneNumber/getUserByEmail/getUsers share identical error profile with getUser. listUsers/deleteUsers are batch variants of single ops. sendEachForMulticast has same surface as sendEach. generateEmailVerificationLink shares surface with generatePasswordResetLink. unsubscribeFromTopic shares surface with subscribeToTopic. AppCheck.createToken/verifyToken rarely called directly in SaaS backends.
firebase-admin.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-06 (deepen-stream-2): Added 9 functions covering Auth session cookies, custom tokens, token revocation, password reset, Firestore transactions/batches, and modern messaging APIs. Ground-truth tests: 44/44 pass. Scanner correctly detects all 9 new function types via await_patterns. deepen-index.json corrected 2026-04-16: contract.yaml shows 1.0 coverage (44 contracted, 24 omitted, 68 total), index was stale at 0.74.

functions_intentionally_omitted: use (sync, throws synchronously on non-function plugin - dev error not runtime error)
write (sync buffer write to internal parser)
onPart (sync event handler - user-overridable but not async)
writeHeaders (async but internal - called by parse(); its rejection flows through parse() promise)
formidable.omission_reason: Formidable's documented public async API is a single function: parse(). All other class methods are either synchronous (use/pause/resume/write/onPart) or internal (writeHeaders is called by parse() so its rejection surfaces through the parse() promise and is already covered). The single contracted function has 15 postconditions covering all 14 FormidableError codes that can surface from parse(). Effective coverage = 1/1 = 100%.
formidable.notes: Pass 2026-06-18 (deepen-stream-6): added 4 new postconditions to parse() covering the previously-uncovered FormidableError codes - pluginFailed (1017, custom-plugin failures), smallerThanMinFileSize (1008, configured min-size violation), unknownTransferEncoding (1014, exotic multipart parts), filenameNotString (1005, security-relevant Content-Disposition header validation). All four flow through the parse() promise rejection path that was already detected; this pass closes the contract-completeness gap by giving each error code its own remediation guidance. Queued 2 scanner concerns about improving the AI fix prompt's per-code branching. Total parse() postcondition count: 15 (was 11 before this pass). coverage_score promoted 0.92 -> 1.0 because all FormidableError codes are now individually represented.

functions_intentionally_omitted: pathExists (never throws — catches all errors internally, returns false on any error)
emptyDir (self-handles readdir errors, creates dir if missing)
ensureLink (niche filesystem op, rare in SaaS)
ensureSymlink (niche filesystem op, rare in SaaS)
fs-extra.key_findings: writeJson is a critical footgun — unlike outputFile and outputJson, it does NOT create parent directories. ENOENT when parent is missing is common. Also: remove() force:true only silences ENOENT, not EACCES/EPERM — silent failures in cleanup code.
scanner_concerns_resolved: concern-20260403-fs-extra-1 (mkdirp/mkdirs detection — scanner already detects these)
concern-20260403-fs-extra-2 (createFile detection — scanner already detects this)
fs-extra.notes: Pass 22 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): RE-VERIFICATION pass against fs-extra@11.3.5 (latest, published 2026-05-06). Full Phase 1 API surface walk via @types/fs-extra@11.0.4 index.d.ts plus runtime exports in lib/index.js, lib/fs/index.js, lib/ensure/index.js, lib/json/index.js. fs-extra "extra" namespace (the scope of this contract per established convention) unchanged since the 2026-04-03 deepen pass: 16 functions total = 12 contracted (readJson, copy, move, outputFile, writeJson, ensureDir, remove, outputJson, ensureFile, mkdirp, mkdirs, createFile) + 4 intentionally omitted (pathExists never-throws, emptyDir self-handles, ensureLink/ensureSymlink niche). All-caps aliases (readJSON/writeJSON/outputJSON), createLink/createSymlink, and emptydir share function references with their lowercase/non-create twins and are covered transitively via detection.await_patterns. 11.3.x bugfixes since the last pass (identical-file detection on Windows, file-descriptor leak in sync timestamp code, spurious UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning in copy) are internal corrections that do not introduce new documented error types — no new postconditions warranted. The 11.3.0 promise wrappers for newer Node fs methods (cp, glob, statfs, lutimes) are Node fs surface, not fs-extra extras, and stay out of scope under the same convention that already excludes the rest of the promisified fs surface (access, readFile, writeFile, etc.) from this contract. coverage_score in contract.yaml stays at 1.0; last_verified bumped to 2026-06-23.

helmet.omission_reason: Six no-arg (or option-less validation) header middleware that never throw on any input. xDnsPrefetchControl accepts {allow: boolean} but reads only truthy/falsy state with no validation. The other five take no options at all. All six only call res.setHeader. Errors are impossible at factory time or request time. Verified against helmet@8.2.0 dist source.
helmet.notes: Pass 12 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-18): No new function entries — all error-throwing functions were already contracted as of pass 17 (2026-04-13). This pass deepened existing functions by adding 6 net-new source-verified postconditions: helmet() factory gained helmet-passed-as-middleware-not-factory (the canonical app.use(helmet) bug) and helmet-duplicate-option-pair (8 modern-vs-legacy alias conflict cases collapsed into one postcondition). contentSecurityPolicy() gained 4 helmet-8.x-specific factory-time validation paths: csp-invalid-directive-value-chars (semicolon/comma in value), csp-unquoted-special-keyword (the most common CSP misconfig), csp-dangerously-disable-on-non-default-src (symbol misuse), csp-falsy-directive-value (env-var-without-fallback). Also updated coop-invalid-policy to include noopener-allow-popups (added in helmet 8.x). Six scanner concerns queued (concerns 1-6). Effective coverage: 9/9 contracted error-throwing functions = 1.0. All 34 helmet ground-truth tests pass.

ioredis.omission_reason: scan/hscan/sscan/zscan are cursor-based streaming operations — they are used via scanStream() helper in ioredis which wraps the low-level cursor. Contracting the low-level cursor commands would duplicate coverage. xadd/xread are Redis Streams commands with very limited SaaS adoption — rarely called in corpus repos.
ioredis.notes: Pass 15 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): drift-by-staleness win. Profile was 82 days stale (last deepened 2026-04-02 against ioredis@5.x). Re-enumerated full RedisCommander.d.ts API surface against ioredis@5.11.1 (latest) under semver >=4.27.8 (no fork needed). Identified 4 high-impact uncovered functions still material to SaaS error contracts: (1) exec — transaction/pipeline finalizer was listed in await_patterns but had no functions[] entry; the three failure modes (per-tuple errors silently embedded in result array, null-return from discarded WATCH'd transaction, outer-promise rejection on connection loss / EXECABORT) are distinct error contracts; (2) exists — presence probe used everywhere in idempotency/rate-limit/cache-aside flows; the unhandled-rejection case fails-open on idempotency probes (Stripe charge fires twice on Redis blip); the boolean-coercion bug (multi-key exists returns NUMBER but `if (await exists(a,b,c))` treats 1 and 3 the same) is a documented warning-severity hazard; (3) getex — atomic GET+TTL refresh (Redis 6.2+); covers WRONGTYPE on non-string keys, unhandled-rejection (including 'unknown command' on legacy <6.2 servers), and the invalid-TTL ReplyError that strikes when `expiresAt - Date.now()` produces a negative seconds count; (4) getdel — atomic get-and-delete (Redis 6.2+); canonical for magic-link token / OTP / one-time idempotency-key consumption; covers WRONGTYPE, unhandled-rejection (token stays in Redis if DELETE side never runs), and the null-vs-rejection conflation that hides connection issues behind 'invalid token' UX noise. 10 new postconditions written total, all citing redis.io docs and the ioredis RedisCommander.d.ts source-of-truth (lines 1887, 1894-1897, 2345, 2353-2364). Added await_patterns: .exists(, .getex(, .getdel( (.exec( already present). Fixtures: ground-truth.ts gained 8 cases (4 SHOULD_FIRE + 4 SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). All 26 ground-truth tests pass post-edit. 4 scanner concerns queued (3117-3120). Validation: nark-corpus npm run validate — All 191 contracts valid. contract_version 1.0.0 → 1.1.0. coverage_score 0.83 → 1.0 (29 contracted / 29 covered).

jsonwebtoken.notes: decode() contract is unique: not error handling, but security invariant. jwt.decode() never throws (returns null), but using its return value for authentication without verify() is a complete auth bypass.
jsonwebtoken.last_pass_note: Pass 18 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): drift-by-staleness re-verification (last_deepened was 2026-04-02 = ~12 weeks stale). Re-enumerated jsonwebtoken@9.0.3 TypeScript surface via @types/jsonwebtoken@latest — only 3 exported functions (sign, verify, decode), unchanged since previous deepen. Error class hierarchy (TokenExpiredError / JsonWebTokenError / NotBeforeError) per README 'Errors & Codes' section is unchanged. Existing contract postconditions remain accurate: verify covers 8 documented JsonWebTokenError messages + 2 edge cases (CVE-2015-9235 algorithm confusion, callback-style error handling); sign covers 5 sync-validation Errors at warning severity; decode covers the auth-bypass and null-return security invariants. No new functions to contract, no postconditions added, no fixtures or scanner concerns queued. last_verified bumped 2026-04-02 → 2026-06-23. Coverage 3/3=1.0 (unchanged).

toDate (Invalid Date object — same pattern as valueOf NaN)

moment.omission_reason: diff(), toDate(), valueOf() all follow the same NaN/invalid propagation pattern that is already captured by the format-invalid-date-string and toisostring-null-for-invalid postconditions. Adding separate entries would be redundant — the behavioral contract (check isValid() before using moment output) is already documented.
moment.notes: 5 new function entries added this pass (add, subtract, min, max, fromNow). Key insights: (1) add()/subtract() on invalid moment silently propagates invalidity — the most dangerous pattern since it is the most commonly used moment operation; callers must validate before manipulation. (2) moment.min()/moment.max() counterintuitively propagate invalidity from any single invalid argument — even if 9 of 10 dates are valid, one bad date makes the entire result invalid. (3) fromNow() returns the string "Invalid date" silently for UI rendering (not an exception). All new postconditions confirmed from https://momentjs.com/docs/ official documentation. Scanner concerns already queued from 2026-04-13 pass (concerns -1 through -6).

functions_intentionally_omitted: estimatedDocumentCount (same error profile as countDocuments — network errors + MongoServerError, already contracted)
createSearchIndex / createSearchIndexes / dropSearchIndex (Atlas-only, requires MongoDB 7.0+, niche adoption in SaaS)
Db.command (raw admin command execution, not typical SaaS runtime code)
Admin.* (all admin server management operations — monitoring, database admin, not runtime)
ClientSession.endSession (called automatically by withSession/withTransaction finally block — not called directly)
Cursor methods: toArray, forEach, next, hasNext, tryNext (cursor iteration — same network error profile as find(), already contracted via find)
Db.dropDatabase (destructive admin op, rarely in SaaS runtime code)
mongodb.omission_reason: estimatedDocumentCount shares the same error hierarchy (MongoServerError, MongoNetworkError, MongoServerSelectionError) as countDocuments which is already contracted — no distinct behavioral contract. Atlas Search index operations (createSearchIndex/dropSearchIndex) are only available on MongoDB Atlas 7.0+, not applicable to self-hosted deployments used by most SaaS developers. Db.command is a low-level primitive for raw MongoDB wire protocol commands — SaaS app code uses the typed Collection/Db methods instead. Admin.* methods are server administration operations (listDatabases, ping, removeUser, validateCollection, serverStatus) for ops/monitoring code, not application runtime. ClientSession.endSession is always called by withSession/withTransaction finally blocks — application code does not call it directly. Cursor iteration methods have the same error profile as find() (which returns the cursor) — already contracted. Db.dropDatabase is an irreversible destructive operation on par with DROP DATABASE in SQL — not in hot-path SaaS runtime code.
mongodb.notes: 6 new functions contracted this pass (deepen-stream-2 pass 13). Key insights: (1) createCollection throws MongoServerError code 48 (NamespaceExists) when collection already exists — idempotent migration code must catch code 48 specifically; also throws MongoCompatibilityError for FLE2 encrypted fields on servers < 7.0 wire protocol version 21 (confirmed from create_collection.js source); (2) distinct returns [] when no documents match (NOT an error) but throws on network/server failure — callers commonly skip try-catch for "non-critical" facet queries; (3) createIndexes throws MongoServerError code 85 (IndexOptionsConflict) or code 86 (IndexKeySpecsConflict) when index definition conflicts with existing index — neither is idempotent, unlike createIndex() with same name+options; (4) dropIndexes throws MongoServerError NamespaceNotFound (code 26) when collection does not exist, unlike dropIndex() (singular) which has IndexNotFound (code 27); (5) withSession callback errors propagate unchanged — no retry, no swallow — key behavioral difference from withTransaction(); endSession errors are squashError()d so they never mask callback errors (confirmed from mongo_client.js); (6) rename sends to admin/ internally (confirmed from rename.js ns field) — throws on TargetNamespaceExists when target collection exists and dropTarget:false; throws NamespaceNotFound (code 26) when source collection does not exist. API surface enumerated from mongodb.d.ts (v6.x) declarations.

mongoose.notes: Pass 17 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): drift-by-staleness re-enumeration against mongoose@9.7.2 (last_deepened was 2026-04-06 = ~12 weeks stale). Surface walk found 6 high-value uncovered methods: Connection.bulkWrite (client-level cross-collection bulk write, throws MongooseBulkWriteError per lib/error/bulkWriteError.js with .validationErrors + .results), Connection.destroy (force-shutdown variant of close, silently discards pending writes — DATA_LOSS hazard), Connection.createCollections (plural with continueOnError, throws CreateCollectionsError per lib/error/createCollectionsError.js aggregating per-collection failures), Document.deleteOne (instance method distinct from Model.deleteOne — runs document-scoped pre/post middleware, has silent zero-deletedCount failure mode), Model.createSearchIndexes (plural Atlas Search variant, partial-creation hazard on failure), Query.cursor.eachAsync (continueOnError aggregates errors into EachAsyncMultiError per lib/error/eachAsyncMultiError.js). 11 postconditions written total across the 6 functions, all evidence_quality=confirmed (cited types/connection.d.ts, types/document.d.ts, types/models.d.ts, lib/error/*.js source, and official docs URLs). Newly omitted: Connection.listCollections, Connection.listDatabases, Connection.asPromise (metadata reads / identity helpers with no destructive error contracts). Fixtures: 12 new ground-truth test cases (6 SHOULD_FIRE / 6 SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). Scanner gap: 4 of the 6 new functions require Connection-instance tracking the scanner doesn't yet implement (only Model-instance tracking via mongoose.model() exists today). Document.deleteOne vs Model.deleteOne discrimination is also needed — scanner currently conflates them. The 2 functions that DO work today via existing detection paths (createSearchIndexes via Model, eachAsync via Query) have passing SHOULD_FIRE tests. 4 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260623-mongoose-deepen-1..4). Effective coverage: 41/41 = 100% (was 35/35 = 100%); raw coverage_score (contracted/total): 0.79 → reflects new methods added to the known surface.

functions_intentionally_omitted: Connection.[Symbol.asyncDispose]() — just calls end(), same error surface
PoolCluster.end() — PoolCluster rarely used in SaaS apps
PoolCluster.getConnection() — PoolCluster rarely used in SaaS apps
Pool.releaseConnection() — synchronous, no async error contract
mysql2.omission_reason: Connection.[Symbol.asyncDispose]() is an alias for end() with identical error surface already contracted. PoolCluster (end/getConnection) is a multi-primary read/write splitting feature rarely used in SaaS production code — most SaaS apps use a single pool or a single connection. Pool.releaseConnection() is synchronous and returns void with no async errors.
mysql2.notes: 2 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) reset() sends COM_RESET_CONNECTION and clears BOTH client-side LRU _statements cache AND server-side prepared statement scope — any PreparedStatementInfo held in application code is stale after reset(). On error, connection is unusable (must destroy(), not release()). (2) PreparedStatementInfo.close() is fire-and-forget (always resolves, no reject path) — sends COM_STMT_CLOSE which MySQL protocol does not acknowledge. The behavioral contract is resource management: not calling close() leaks server-side slots against max_prepared_stmt_count (default 16,382). Long-running services that call prepare() without close() exhibit gradual degradation hours or days later when ER_MAX_PREPARED_STMT_COUNT_REACHED hits on new prepare() calls. The note in mysql2 docs: "Manually prepared statements should be closed using statement.close()" confirms the intent.

Inheriting the existing getserversession-* postconditions would just duplicate coverage; flagging it would penalize callers who already migrated. Omitted as deprecated alias.
next-auth.omission_reason: next-auth@4.24.14 exposes 11 callable async exported functions across the index/jwt/react/next/middleware entry points. 10 are now contracted with explicit postconditions (encode, decode, getServerSession, getToken, signIn, signOut, withAuth, getSession, getCsrfToken, getProviders). The 11th — unstable_getServerSession — is a documented @deprecated alias whose error contract is identical to getServerSession; flagging it would penalize callers who already migrated to the new name without adding any safety. Coverage 10/10 effective = 1.0.
next-auth.notes: Pass 23 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): added getCsrfToken (getcsrftoken-undefined-not-checked) and getProviders (getproviders-null-not-checked). Both are next-auth/react client helpers that wrap fetchData() (client/_utils.js) — fetchData catches all fetch errors silently, logs CLIENT_FETCH_ERROR, and returns null. getCsrfToken returns response?.csrfToken which yields undefined on fetch failure; getProviders propagates null directly. Both failures are silent (no exception thrown) and lead to authentication breakdowns in custom sign-in pages: undefined csrfToken triggers MissingCSRF server-side (silent auth failure), and null providers throws TypeError on Object.values() (blank sign-in page). Sources fetched: https://next-auth.js.org/getting-started/client (getCsrfToken + getProviders sections — docs sparse on null/error behavior so postcondition evidence is sourced from the dist JS at node_modules/next-auth/client/_utils.js and react/index.js). API surface enumeration ran against next-auth@4.24.14 installed at /tmp/claude/bc-deepen/next-auth via npm pack. Total async surface: 11 (encode, decode, getToken, getServerSession, unstable_getServerSession, getSession, getCsrfToken, getProviders, signIn, signOut, withAuth). unstable_getServerSession deprecated-alias-omitted. Fixtures: ground-truth.ts gained sections 11-14 covering SHOULD_FIRE + SHOULD_NOT_FIRE for both new postconditions. Two scanner-upgrade concerns queued (concern-20260623-next-auth-deepen-1, concern-20260623-next-auth-deepen-2) — the new fixture sections fail their vitest assertions because no scanner detection rule exists yet for null-return-not-checked on these next-auth/react helpers; that detection gap is exactly what the queued concerns track. Coverage 8/10 -> 10/10 effective = 1.0. metadata.coverage_score on contract.yaml bumped 0.95 -> 1.0; last_verified bumped 2026-04-04 -> 2026-06-23.

functions_intentionally_omitted: ofetch.create — sync factory returning a new $Fetch instance; no async error contract
createFetch — module-level sync factory; no async error contract
createFetchError — sync utility for constructing FetchError objects; not a user-facing callable
ofetch.omission_reason: ofetch.create(), createFetch(), and createFetchError() are synchronous factory/utility functions with no async error contracts. ofetch.native is a property (pass-through to native fetch), not a callable function with distinct error contracts. The 3 async-callable functions (ofetch, $fetch, ofetch.raw) are all contracted.
ofetch.notes: 1 new function entry added (ofetch.raw with 2 postconditions). 2 new postconditions added to existing ofetch() entry (ofetch-timeout-unhandled, ofetch-retry-post-disabled). Key insights: (1) ofetch.raw() is frequently misunderstood — callers from native fetch() assume raw() returns Response for all status codes without throwing; in reality it still throws FetchError on non-2xx (same $fetchRaw core). Only ignoreResponseError:true bypasses throwing. (2) timeout option wraps AbortController internally; timeout error is FetchError with cause.name=TimeoutError, cause.code=23 — distinct from network FetchError. (3) POST/PUT/PATCH default to retry:0 — callers who expect server-side retry for 500s will silently get immediate failure. Effective coverage: 3/3 async-callable functions = 100%.

openai.omission_reason: images.createVariation/edit: same error surface as generate, rarely used. fine_tuning.jobs.cancel/pause/resume/batches.retrieve/cancel: lifecycle management ops with no novel error contracts. vector_stores.retrieve/update/list/delete: admin ops, no novel error surface. uploads.cancel: cleanup op. beta.assistants/threads: deprecated Assistants API. conversations.create: managed conversation lifecycle (admin API, not typical SaaS app usage). containers: code execution environment (niche). videos.*: async job pattern — errors in response.status field not thrown as exceptions, same pattern as batches which is already contracted. evals.*: ML evaluation framework for testing models, not production SaaS runtime. skills.*: new API, limited adoption. responses.cancel/retrieve + chat.completions CRUD + models.list/retrieve: utility/cleanup ops. fine_tuning.graders: beta, limited adoption. realtime.calls.*: SIP telephony ops — niche adoption in SaaS apps.
openai.notes: Pass 1 (2026-04-02, deepen-stream-2 pass 1): Added 7 functions. Pass 6 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2): Added 7 more functions. Pass 2 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 2): Added 4 new functions (responses.parse, uploads.complete/parts.create, realtime.sessions.create). Pass 3 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 3): Added 3 new functions — responses.compact, vector_stores.search, responses.inputTokens.count. Pass 6 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 6): Added 2 new functions — webhooks.unwrap (3 postconditions) and webhooks.verifySignature (2 postconditions). Pass 7 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 7): Added 2 new functions — responses.stream (3 postconditions: premature-termination, abort, auth-error) and conversations.items.create (3 postconditions: not-found, rate-limit, bad-request). Key insights: (1) responses.stream() has distinct error OpenAIError from finalResponse() — callers using only for-await on events miss this; (2) APIUserAbortError extends OpenAIError NOT APIError — distinct catch needed; (3) conversations.items.create() is a runtime hot-path in agent frameworks — conversation TTL expiry causes NotFoundError 404 on cached conversation IDs. Pass 8 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 8): Added 2 new functions — chat.completions.stream (4 postconditions: length-finish-reason, content-filter-finish-reason, abort, premature-termination) and chat.completions.runTools (3 postconditions: n>1-not-supported, missing-function, stream-termination). Key insights: (1) chat.completions.stream() converts finish_reason errors into thrown exceptions (LengthFinishReasonError, ContentFilterFinishReasonError) unlike create({stream:true}); (2) runTools() throws immediately on missing function implementations mid-run; (3) runTools() n>1 is a client-side guard that fires before any API call. Pass 9 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 9): Added 3 new functions. API surface expanded 41→44 (files.waitForProcessing, beta.chatkit.sessions.create, beta.chatkit.sessions.cancel added to enumeration). files.waitForProcessing() has a SILENT failure mode: file.status="error" exits the polling loop WITHOUT throwing — callers must check status manually. files.delete() is NOT idempotent (unlike S3 DeleteObject) — throws NotFoundError (404) on re-deletion; cleanup flows must catch explicitly. beta.chatkit.sessions.create() introduces a short-lived client_secret pattern (same as realtime.sessions.create()) — caching is a silent auth failure vector. Pass 10 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 10): Added 3 new functions. files.content() — throws BadRequestError (400) when file is still in uploaded status (not processed); callers must waitForProcessing() first; also throws NotFoundError (404) on deleted files. conversations.items.delete() — NOT idempotent; throws NotFoundError when conversation has expired or item already deleted; agent loops must treat 404 as conversation-rebuild signal. beta.chatkit.sessions.cancel() — has a TOCTOU expiry race: session may expire naturally before explicit cancel call; cleanup flows must treat NotFoundError as acceptable (already-inactive session).

passport.omission_reason: isAuthenticated/isUnauthenticated are synchronous boolean predicates with no error behavior. unuse() just deletes a strategy entry with no error contract. framework() is an internal configuration method used only for non-Express frameworks — no meaningful error contract for SaaS app code.
passport.notes: 4 items added this pass. Key insights: (1) passport.use() throws synchronously if strategy has no name — dangerous because anonymous strategy objects cause startup crashes; (2) passport.initialize() must be mounted BEFORE passport.session() and any authenticate() routes — wrong order causes TypeError on every request; (3) passport.initialize() omitted with older strategy packages (passport-azure-ad, older OAuth) causes TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading "instance") on every request; (4) authenticate() with unregistered strategy name throws Error("Unknown authentication strategy...") via next(err) — caught from authenticate.js source; (5) transformAuthInfo() sync throws caught by try/catch and forwarded to done(err) → next(err). 4 scanner concerns queued.

pg.omission_reason: copyFrom()/copyTo() are streaming bulk COPY operations for data migrations and ETL workloads. They are almost never called in SaaS application code — they require a writable/readable stream pipe chain and are used for bulk loading, not regular queries.
