pino.omission_reason: Synchronous log methods (error/warn/info/debug/trace/silent) have no async error contracts of their own — errors from custom serializers bubble through the serializer-must-not-throw edge case attached to pino(), and destination-level write failures bubble through the destination 'error' event covered by destination/transport postconditions. setBindings/bindings/isLevelEnabled are pure-sync utility methods with no I/O or throws.
pino.notes: Pass 20 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): RE-CONFIRMED-COMPLETE. Re-enumerated pino@10.3.1 full API surface against node_modules/pino/pino.d.ts (908 LOC) and node_modules/pino/docs/api.md. 16 callable functions/methods total: 4 top-level (pino, pino.destination, pino.transport, pino.multistream) + 12 Logger instance methods. All 4 top-level functions are contracted with postconditions covering: destination 'error' event (sonic-boom EACCES/EPERM/disk-full), transport worker crash + module-not-found + target/targets conflict, multistream invalid-stream-entry + individual-stream-failure-silent, flush callback-error-ignored. The 9 sync logger methods have no Promise return type and no documented throws beyond what's already covered by the serializer-must-not-throw edge case on pino() and the destination-error postcondition. logger.fatal kept as edge-case-only (no auto-process-exit) and logger.child has child-bindings-serializer-error. Reviewed MultiStreamRes sub-methods (add/flushSync/remove/clone) — not separately contracted because (a) scanner uses name-based detection at call sites and (b) 2-chain patterns like multistream().flushSync() are not observed in pino fixtures or scan corpus. If 2-chain detection lands as a future scanner upgrade, revisit. pino.final was removed in pino@v9 and is no longer part of the surface. Coverage 7/7 contracted = 1.0 effective. last_verified bumped to 2026-06-23. coverage_score in contract.yaml updated 0.71 -> 1.0 to match index. No new postconditions written, no fixtures added, no scanner concerns queued.

functions_intentionally_omitted: categoriesGet — read-only, no auth required, no item-level errors
institutionsGet — read-only lookup, no user-specific error surface
institutionsGetById — read-only GET, narrow error surface (404 only)
plaid.omission_reason: Read-only institutional lookups with no user-item-specific error contracts. Cannot throw ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED or TRANSFER_ERROR. Generic HTTP errors only.

puppeteer.omission_reason: $$eval has identical error profile to $eval. hover/tap/focus/select have identical error profile to click (element not found). $/$$  return null/empty array (no throws). setViewport/emulate/setJavaScriptEnabled/setBypassCSP are configuration methods with no meaningful error contracts. setCookie/deleteCookie/cookies are low-level cookie ops. authenticate/setExtraHTTPHeaders are configuration. bringToFront/addScriptTag/addStyleTag are utility. setGeolocation/waitForFileChooser are niche. createBrowserContext/browser.pages/browser.disconnect are admin/utility operations.
puppeteer.notes: 10 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) page.evaluate() is the #1 cause of silent data loss in scraping — TargetCloseError when page navigates during eval; (2) page.pdf() only works in headless mode — headed throws immediately; (3) page.$eval() THROWS on missing element unlike $() which returns null — critical distinction; (4) page.waitForNetworkIdle() guaranteed to timeout on pages with WebSocket/SSE connections; (5) puppeteer.connect() failures crash browser pool workers if unhandled. 5 scanner concerns queued — all require Page instance tracking from puppeteer.launch() return chain.

redis.coverage_note: Effective coverage stays 1.0 (27 contracted / 27 non-omitted). Pass 2026-06-23 added 5 v5+ public-API entries (close, watch, createClientPool, execute, sendCommand) re-enumerated against redis@6.0.0. Total async-callable updated from 35 → 40; non-omitted from 22 → 27. Raw score remains 1.0 by contracted/(total-omitted) formula.
redis.omission_reason: mGet/mSet share identical error profile with get/set. decr/decrBy share identical profile with incr/incrBy. ttl/type/info/ping are read-only commands with no domain-specific error contracts. hDel/hmGet share error profile with hGet. lPop/rPop/rPush share error profile with lPush. sRem/sIsMember share profile with sAdd. zRem/zScore share profile with zAdd. disconnect() is synchronous (not async). pSubscribe/sSubscribe share profile with subscribe. scan/hscan/sscan/zscan are cursor-based admin operations rarely called in SaaS runtime code.
redis.notes: Pass 12 (2026-06-23, deepen-stream-2): re-verified against redis@6.0.0 (latest at re-verify). Added 5 v5+ public-API entries the original v4-era 2026-04-02 pass could not anticipate: close (graceful close replacing quit), watch (optimistic-locking primitive — WatchError flows to exec()), createClientPool (v5+ pool factory — added to factory_methods), execute (pool isolated-client callback pattern — added to await_patterns), sendCommand (raw command escape hatch). contract_version 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 (minor: backward-compatible function additions). All 55 ground-truth tests pass — scanner detects every new function via existing await_patterns + factory_methods system with no new scanner concerns required. Effective coverage remains 1.0 (27/27 non-omitted).

resend.omission_reason: All admin/read-only methods across the 17 sub-clients (list/get/update/delete operations) share the same generic { data, error } silent-failure mode already documented under emails-cancel-no-error-check and contacts-create-no-error-check. The high-impact send/create/update/verify/trigger/control-plane methods (emails.send/create/update/cancel, batch.send/create, contacts.create, broadcasts.send/create, domains.verify, webhooks.create/verify, events.send, automations.stop) cover the unique error-handling implications (cascade-into-next-call, signing_secret-persistence, domain-verification-gating, schedule-mutation, automation-trigger-loss, control-plane-stop-during-incident). Adding postconditions for every list/get/update method would inflate the contract without adding new behavioral guidance — a generic 'always check result.error' covers them all. ChainableTemplateResult.publish is a chained variant of templates.publish; same error profile. The 6 Resend HTTP primitives (.fetchRequest/.post/etc) are internal building blocks for the sub-clients — applications shouldn't call them directly.
resend.notes:
Pass 7 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-11): +2 high-impact functions closes the 12/14 effective-coverage gap left by pass 3 (2026-06-12 deepen-stream-2). events.send() is the Resend v6 Automations product trigger API — silent failure here is uniquely dangerous because the downstream signal is absent (missed drip campaigns leave contacts silently dropped out of funnels, with no error or downstream artifact). automations.stop() is the control-plane halt operation — silent failure during incident response leaves the offending automation running while the operator's runbook claims it is halted. Both postconditions require checking result.error AND, for automations.stop, also verifying data.status === 'disabled' before declaring success (a successful HTTP response can still carry a non-disabled state per the validation_error path). API surface re-enumerated against resend@6.12.4 — 17 sub-clients exposing ~70+ async methods, 14 high-impact mutations all now contracted, 56 admin/read-only methods intentionally omitted. Effective coverage 14/14 high-impact = 1.0. Raw score against the 70-method surface = 0.20 but the 56 admin omissions are the correct call. Pass 3 notes preserved below for traceability.

--- Pass 3 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-12) notes ---
API surface re-enumerated against resend@6.12.4 (was originally onboarded at v3.x with ~10 methods). The SDK has expanded dramatically — 17 sub-clients exposing ~70+ async methods. Key insights this pass: (1) emails.create() and batch.create() are aliases for .send() added in v5.x — same risk profile, but the new naming convention means scanner-side detection needs to handle both names. (2) emails.update() is a scheduled-email reschedule API — silent failure causes drip campaigns to fire at wrong time without any error signal. (3) domains.verify() is a verification trigger (not a synchronous verify) — silent failure means the user thinks verification is in progress but it never started; downstream emails.send() then fails with invalid_from_address, hiding the original verify failure. (4) broadcasts.create() failures cascade into broadcasts.send(undefined) which surfaces a confusing TypeError or not_found that masks the real cause. (5) webhooks.create() returns signing_secret in data — silent failure here is doubly dangerous because the handler is then deployed against an undefined RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET, causing every webhook to fail verification with 'Secret can\'t be empty.'

rxjs.omission_reason: All 5 async-callable functions in rxjs v7 are contracted: firstValueFrom, lastValueFrom, Observable.prototype.forEach, Observable.prototype.toPromise, fromFetch (rxjs/fetch). No functions omitted.
rxjs.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-16 (deepen-stream-2 pass 5): +1 function contracted. fromFetch() from rxjs/fetch has 2 postconditions: (1) fromfetch-no-ok-check — non-2xx HTTP responses are NOT Observable errors, they are next() values with response.ok===false; callers must check response.ok explicitly. (2) fromfetch-network-error-unhandled — network/CORS errors throw TypeError as Observable errors, must be caught. Evidence confirmed from rxjs/src/internal/observable/dom/fetch.ts source. The coverage_score was previously 0.80 (4/5) with fromFetch as the uncovered 5th function.

socket.io.omission_reason: Socket.leave inverse-of-join (idempotent, same adapter path as join); Namespace.serverSideEmitWithAck duplicates Server.serverSideEmitWithAck via inheritance; allSockets() x3 are @deprecated in v4.8.3 and slated for removal in next major (per .d.ts source comments)
socket.io.pass_note: pass-5 deepen-stream-1 (2026-06-18): API surface re-enumerated against socket.io@4.8.3 .d.ts. Confirmed 10 contracted functions cover all non-deprecated, non-duplicate async-callable methods. allSockets() in all 3 locations marked omitted (deprecated). Effective coverage now 1.0.

socket.io-client.note: 5/5 meaningful async-callable functions contracted. io (connect_error, disconnect-event, parser-error-event), emit (acknowledgement-timeout), emitWithAck (timeout-error, disconnect-error, reserved-event-name), socket.io.reconnect_failed (reconnect-failed-event), socket.io.reconnect_error (reconnect-error-event). Manager config methods (reconnection/reconnectionDelay/etc.) are synchronous getters/setters with no async error surface.

functions_intentionally_omitted: update (metadata changes, no domain-specific error contracts)
reverse (payout reversal, admin/ops operation, narrow SaaS adoption)
stripe.omission_reason: update() operations are metadata changes with no meaningful domain-specific error contracts beyond generic HTTP. list()/search() are read-only GETs. expire() for checkout sessions is rarely called programmatically. reverse() for payouts is an admin/ops operation not commonly in SaaS app code.
stripe.notes: 9 new functions contracted: constructEventAsync (2 postconditions: signature-failed + timestamp-tolerance for Edge Runtime webhook verification), finalizeInvoice (2 postconditions: no-payment-method-types + not-editable), pay (3 postconditions: requires-action + card-declined + not-payable — invoice_payment_intent_requires_action is critical silent failure), sendInvoice (2 postconditions: not-open + invalid-email), voidInvoice (2 postconditions: invalid-state + rate-limit), markUncollectible (1 postcondition: invalid-state), resume (2 postconditions: invalid-state + payment-required — resume() does NOT immediately activate subscription if invoice payment fails), incrementAuthorization (2 postconditions: card-declined + invalid-state — card_declined does NOT cancel the PaymentIntent, original auth remains valid), verifyMicrodeposits (3 postconditions: amounts-mismatch + attempts-exceeded + timeout). All error codes confirmed from stripe v22.0.2 TypeScript declarations. API surface expanded from 13 to 23 total functions. Coverage: 9/13 (was 0.56 in contract.yaml) → 18/23 = 0.78 raw, 18/18 non-omitted = 1.0 effective.

functions_intentionally_omitted: superagent(url) callable-as-function — same error profile as get(), redundant contract
Request.end() callback — legacy callback pattern, same errors as get() but via first-arg callback
superagent.omission_reason: callable-as-function is identical to get(). end() callback is the legacy pre-Promise API with identical errors in first argument.
superagent.notes: Pass 2 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 11): Added pipe() — 1 new function, 2 postconditions. Key insight: pipe() completely bypasses the Promise chain. Internally it calls end() and sets this.piped=true; the normal response handler checks if(this.piped)return to skip the callback/Promise path entirely. Network/HTTP errors emit as Node.js events on the Request (req.emit("error",err)); decompressor errors (gzip/deflate) emit on the destination stream (stream.emit("error",err)). try-catch and async/await provide ZERO protection. If no error listener is registered, Node.js throws an uncaught exception. Also: mixing pipe() with await (calling end() internally via then()) causes a synchronous throw: "end() has already been called, so its too late to start piping". Both postconditions confirmed from lib/node/index.js source inspection (lines 423-454, 769, 855, 889-896, 958) and https://forwardemail.github.io/superagent/#piping. 2 scanner concerns queued for detection rule implementation. Coverage: 9/9 non-omitted = 100%.

twilio.omission_reason: incomingPhoneNumbers.create() is a phone number provisioning/admin operation, not a runtime SaaS app code path. It's called once during setup, not in per-request code, and has the same error profile as the already-contracted messages.create() auth errors.

typeorm.omission_reason: Deprecated methods omitted (findByIds, exist, findOneById, connect, close). Thin-alias methods omitted (updateAll/deleteAll same as update/delete, preload same as findOne, sql tagged template same as query, findAndCountBy same as findAndCount, existsBy same as exists, softRemove/recover same error profile as remove). Aggregate math functions (sum/avg/min/max) omitted as connection failure only — no domain-specific contracts.

undici.omission_reason: All async-callable API surface methods present in undici@8.5.0 declaration files (types/api.d.ts, types/fetch.d.ts BodyMixin, types/dispatcher.d.ts, types/websocket.d.ts, types/eventsource.d.ts) are now contracted. The previously contracted 10 methods (request, fetch, stream, connect, upgrade, response.json, response.text, response.arrayBuffer, WebSocket constructor, Dispatcher.close) covered most of the surface but were missing the 6 remaining body-consumers (blob/bytes/formData), the streaming Duplex variant (pipeline), the destroy lifecycle method, and the EventSource SSE constructor.
undici.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-18T18:00:00Z): +6 net new functions covering body-consumer completeness (blob/bytes/formData share consumeBody code path with text/arrayBuffer/json), the streaming Duplex API (pipeline emits 'error' event on returned Duplex), the emergency-shutdown counterpart to close (destroy), and the SSE EventSource constructor (mirrors WebSocket event-driven error model with the additional danger of indefinite auto-retry on connection failure). Total 10 new postconditions written, 9 scanner concerns queued (7 detection upgrades + 1 fixture wiring + 1 Content-Type validation enhancement). Coverage 10/null (raw 0.91 reconstructed) → 16/16 = 1.0. Sources fetched from undici@8.5.0 types/*.d.ts and lib/web/fetch/body.js, lib/web/eventsource/eventsource.js, lib/dispatcher/dispatcher-base.js, lib/api/api-pipeline.js.

functions_intentionally_omitted: UTApi.getFileUrls (deprecated — will be removed in v9; same error profile as other API methods; use direct URL construction)
UTApi.getSignedURL (deprecated — replaced by generateSignedURL which is more efficient; same error profile)
uploadthing.omission_reason: getFileUrls is documented as deprecated and will be removed in a future version — using direct URL construction is the recommended approach. getSignedURL is explicitly superseded by generateSignedURL (which does not make a network call); same error profile. Both are low-adoption and declining.
uploadthing.notes: Pass 4 added 6 new functions and 8 new postconditions. Key insights: (1) uploadFiles/uploadFilesFromUrl return { data, error } per file — NOT thrown for per-file failures; callers must check result.error on each item; (2) uploadFilesFromUrl explicitly rejects data: URLs with BAD_REQUEST — use uploadFiles() for data URLs; (3) generateSignedURL does NOT make network calls (HMAC signing locally), but throws BAD_REQUEST for invalid expiresIn (> 7 days) or MISSING_ENV for misconfigured token; (4) updateACL throws FORBIDDEN if per-file ACL not enabled in UploadThing app settings; (5) All UTApi methods call guardServerOnly() first — throws INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR from browser context; (6) deleteFiles returns success: true with deletedCount: 0 for non-existent keys (no throw). API surface: 10 total (8 contracted, 2 intentionally omitted). effective_coverage = 8/8 = 1.0.

@sendgrid/mail.omission_reason: setClient/setTwilioEmailAuth/setTimeout/setSubstitutionWrappers are all synchronous void configuration methods with no async error contracts. They do not throw and return void. (setSubstitutionWrappers DOES throw sync on undefined args per mail-service.js line 66, but a sync throw at config time is not the deepen-skill scope.)
@sendgrid/mail.notes: Pass 12 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): DRIFT-MODE DEEPEN against @sendgrid/mail@8.1.6 (current latest, still within profile semver >=7.0.0 <9.0.0, no fork needed). Re-enumerated dist via npm install: index.d.ts re-exports MailService class from src/mail.d.ts. 5 sync methods (setApiKey/setClient/setTwilioEmailAuth/setTimeout/setSubstitutionWrappers) plus 2 async methods (send/sendMultiple). All 3 previously-contracted functions stay covered. ADDED 3 new postconditions surfacing production hazards confirmed by source review of node_modules/@sendgrid/mail/src/classes/mail-service.js plus node_modules/@sendgrid/helpers/classes/mail.js: (1) send-array-batch-no-partial-failure-handling on send(): source line 165 shows Promise.all(data.map(...)) for array input, which fails-fast and silently abandons sibling sends; bulk-notification senders pattern-match `await sgMail.send(msgs)` thinking they have all-or-some-success visibility, but Promise.all gives all-or-none-aggregate; required handling is to use for-of/Promise.allSettled or the correct sendMultiple shape. (2) send-validation-error-no-response-shape on send(): source lines 181-220 show 24+ sync throw sites in @sendgrid/helpers/classes/mail.js (Mail.create) plus 1 throw in filterSecrets, all caught and re-emitted as Promise.reject(error); these bare-Error rejections have NO error.response property, so catch blocks following the standard `error.response.body` pattern null-deref and mask the real failure with a TypeError. (3) send-multiple-callback-with-unhandled-promise on sendMultiple(): .d.ts line 40 confirms callback signature, source lines 169-178 + 213-215 confirm callback fires AND promise rejects (dual emission); a caller using the legacy callback API still triggers UnhandledPromiseRejection on the returned-but-ignored promise; Node 15+ default crashes the process even though the callback successfully handled the error. Coverage stays at 3/3 = 1.0 (no new functions, no surface gaps; we deepened postcondition density per function, not function count). last_verified bumped from 2026-04-02 to 2026-06-23. No scanner concerns queued (all 3 new postconditions are detector-level guidance; the array-shape, .response-shape, and callback-shape detection are within reach of the existing scanner once codified as separate rule families, but no scanner code-change is blocking). No fixtures added this pass (existing ground-truth.ts / proper / missing fixtures still cover the bare-await error path which is the dominant violation type; the 3 new postconditions target shape-specific scenarios that would warrant their own fixture pass in a future cycle). 9 scanner violations in existing fixtures still fire (verified via local scan post-edit, no regressions).

@upstash/redis.omission_reason: Administrative and maintenance commands (keys, scan, type, ttl, pttl, dump, restore, migrate, rename, sort, randomkey) share the same UpstashError/network error profile as already-contracted get(). List sub-commands (lpos, lset, lrem, llen, linsert, lmove) have identical error profiles to lpush/lrange already contracted. Hash sub-commands (hlen, hkeys, hvals, hmget, hmset) have identical error profiles to hget/hset. Sorted set sub-commands (zincrby, zcard, zrank, zscore) share the zadd/zrange error profile. Set sub-commands (scard, sinterstore, sunion, sdiff) share the sadd/smembers profile. Low-level ops (object, wait, copy, unlink, persist, pexpire, expireat, rename, lolwut) are rare in SaaS application code.
@upstash/redis.notes: Key gaps addressed this pass: (1) subscribe() errors are event-dispatched not thrown — requires .on('error') listener; (2) pipeline keepErrors mode silently returns errors instead of throwing; (3) evalsha() NOSCRIPT error requires fallback to eval(); (4) getdel() state ambiguity on network failure for one-time-use tokens.
@upstash/redis.pass13_notes: Drift pass: v1.34.7 (April-era) → v1.38.0 (May 2026). Net-new APIs detected from .d.ts diff: full Streams family (xadd/xgroup/xreadgroup/xclaim/xpending/xtrim/xinfo), Redis Functions family (functions.load/call/list/delete/flush/stats), and key-level ops (copy, flushdb, clientSetinfo, hexpire family). Postconditioned the high-impact subset: xadd (canonical event-source write), xgroup (BUSYGROUP/NOGROUP lifecycle errors), xreadgroup (silent-halt on NOGROUP), copy (silent NOT_COPIED return), flushdb (destructive admin op).

groq-sdk.omission_reason: No omissions remaining. Pass 16 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23) brought groq-sdk@1.3.0 to full coverage: 17/17 async resource methods across chat.completions, audio.{speech,transcriptions,translations}, embeddings, batches (create/retrieve/list/cancel), files (create/list/delete/content/info), and models (retrieve/list/delete). Low-level Groq client HTTP primitives (get/post/patch/put/delete on the Groq class itself, used internally by the resource APIs and rarely called directly by app code) are intentionally not enumerated as public-surface functions; they are infrastructure of the higher-level resources.
groq-sdk.notes: Pass 16 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): drift-by-staleness target (last deepened 2026-04-02, 82 days). Re-enumerated against groq-sdk@1.3.0 (major-version bump from 0.3.x). All previously contracted functions still present; surface is additive. Added 9 new functions: batches.{retrieve,list}, files.{list,delete,content,info}, models.{retrieve,list,delete}. Notable additions: (a) files.content carries a second DATA_LOSS warning postcondition for the Response-body-not-consumed silent-failure pattern — files.content returns a raw fetch Response and the batch results are silently lost if the body is never read; (b) files.delete carries an explicit ConflictError (409) note for the file-still-referenced-by-active-batch case; (c) models.retrieve / models.delete carry deprecation-handling guidance because Groq deprecates models on a rolling basis (NotFoundError on hardcoded model ids is the dominant production failure mode for long-lived deployments). Coverage 8/11=0.73 -> 17/17=1.0. Semver range unchanged at >=0.3.0 because v1.x is fully additive over v0.3.x for the previously contracted function surface.

sequelize.omission_reason: databaseVersion() and set() are operational utilities with no user-facing error contracts. createSchema/showAllSchemas/dropSchema/dropAllSchemas are schema-management ops that would only run in migration scripts (not in hot-path SaaS code). Sequelize.truncate/drop are dangerous ops intentionally omitted. Model.drop/sync are DDL operations. 11 total omitted.
scanner_concerns: FAILING: line 195 save() — instance method on variable returned from findByPk not detected. Scanner needs instance variable tracking for Model results.
FAILING: line 315 reload() — same issue: instance method on variable returned from findByPk not detected.
Instance methods validate() also at risk but passes because build() result is used inline in the same scope.

sequelize.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-04-17): Added instance.increment() with postcondition instance-increment-db-error. instance.increment() delegates entirely to Model.increment() with where: this.where() — same DatabaseError/ConnectionError profile. No unique errors vs static version. Silent 0-row updates (deleted record between fetch and increment) are NOT errors. Coverage: 36/36 non-omitted = 100%. Total API: 47 (36 contracted, 11 omitted). Prior note: effective coverage has been 100% since pass 4 (the single increment/decrement entries cover both static and instance versions), but the deepen-index hadnot been updated to reflect this.

mailgun.js.omission_reason: Read-only GETs (events.get, suppressions.list/get, stats.*, metrics.*, logs.*, tags.list, webhooks.list/get, lists.list/get, lists.members.listMembers/getMember), pure admin operations (domains.*, ips.*, ip_pools.*, subaccounts.*, inboxPlacements.*, dkimManagement.*, routing rules), and low-adoption batch status operations (validate.multipleValidation.list/get/destroy, lists.validate/validationResult/cancelValidation).
mailgun.js.notes: 8 new functions contracted this pass (pass 4): suppressions.destroy (compliance-critical bounce re-enablement), suppressions.destroyAll (NEW in v12.8.0 2026-04-14 - catastrophic bulk delete), lists.update (list property changes), lists.destroy (permanent list deletion), lists.members.updateMember (unsubscribe compliance), lists.members.destroyMember (GDPR deletion compliance), webhooks.create and webhooks.update (event pipeline setup). Coverage reaches 100% of write-operation API surface. Key compliance risks: (1) suppressions.destroyAll is a new method that can wipe all bounce/unsubscribe lists with a single call - needs safeguard detection; (2) updateMember with subscribed:false and destroyMember are CAN-SPAM critical operations.

jsonschema.omission_reason: getSchema() is a read-only lookup with no throw contract. setSchemas() is a setup utility with no throw behavior. addSubSchemaArray() and addSubSchemaObject() are internal helpers not part of the public API surface — they do not appear in the TypeScript declarations and should not be called directly by application code.
jsonschema.notes: 5 new postconditions added. Key additions: (1) validate-invalid-schema-argument — SchemaError thrown when schema arg is null/non-object (confirmed from validator.js:107-109); (2) validate-unknown-attribute-throws — SchemaError when allowUnknownAttributes:false with unknown keywords (confirmed from validator.js:207-210); (3) validate-result-unchecked — silent failure when result not checked without throw options (confirmed from helpers.js:79-81, SILENT_FAILURE category); (4) validator-validate-unresolved-ref — SchemaError when  URI not registered (confirmed from validator.js:256-273); (5) scan-duplicate-conflicting-schema — Error thrown on conflicting schema IDs (confirmed from scan.js:32-37). Ground-truth test file created in nark. All 5 concerns already queued from previous tooling.

body-parser.omission_reason: bodyParser() is the deprecated combined JSON+urlencoded middleware. The official Express docs and body-parser README explicitly deprecate it in favor of calling bodyParser.json() and bodyParser.urlencoded() separately.
body-parser.notes: 21 new postconditions added across all 4 functions covering: entity.verify.failed (403 — verify callback throws), encoding.unsupported (415 — unsupported Content-Encoding), request.size.invalid (400 — Content-Length mismatch), stream.encoding.set (500 — req.setEncoding() conflict), stream.not.readable (500 — double body read), plus urlencoded-qs-depth-exceeded (400 — qs depth limit). All confirmed from lib/read.js source and official README errors section. 6 scanner concerns queued.

functions_intentionally_omitted: ipKeyGenerator (sync pure utility, no error contract)
MemoryStore direct usage (internal default store, users use external stores)
express-rate-limit.omission_reason: ipKeyGenerator() is a synchronous pure utility function with no async behavior or error contract. MemoryStore is the default internal store — users interact with the rate limiter through rateLimit() not MemoryStore directly.
express-rate-limit.notes: Previous deepen pass (deepen-stream-2, 2026-04-15) started but was interrupted before writing getKey/resetKey entries to contract.yaml. This pass (deepen-stream-1, pass-5) completed the work: (1) getKey() postconditions: get-key-store-unsupported (crashes on unhandled rejection if store lacks get()), get-key-store-error (raw store error propagates unlike middleware which has passOnStoreError); (2) resetKey() postconditions: reset-key-unprotected-endpoint (silent no-op when reset endpoint is itself rate-limited), reset-key-wrong-key-format (silent no-op when key format mismatches keyGenerator output); (3) Added 4 new postconditions to rateLimit() from error codes: invalid-ip-address (ERR_ERL_INVALID_IP_ADDRESS bypass), x-forwarded-for-without-trust-proxy (ERR_ERL_UNEXPECTED_X_FORWARDED_FOR), double-count (ERR_ERL_DOUBLE_COUNT), created-in-request-handler (ERR_ERL_CREATED_IN_REQUEST_HANDLER — silent bypass). 6 scanner concerns queued. deepen-stream-2 pass-5 added: rateLimit-invalid-store (TypeError thrown synchronously at startup when store missing required methods, confirmed from dist/index.cjs line 821-824) and async-callback-error-routes-to-handler (all async callbacks wrapped by handleAsyncErrors() — errors route to next(error), confirmed from dist/index.cjs line 828-834). 2 more scanner concerns queued (total: 8 for express-rate-limit).

discord.js.omission_reason: Client.fetchGuildPreview and fetchVoiceRegions are read-only GETs with no domain-specific error contracts. Guild setter methods (setAFKChannel, setBanner, etc.) are admin configuration operations rarely called in SaaS app runtime code. ShardingManager methods are infrastructure-level bot sharding not used in web apps. AutoModerationRule and GuildTemplate management are admin operations with no meaningful runtime error contracts beyond generic DiscordAPIError.
discord.js.notes: 10 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) Webhook.send() is a critical pattern — bots storing webhook URLs in DBs will crash on send when tokens expire (10015 error). RateLimitError is a second concern for high-volume webhook senders. (2) GuildMember.createDM() throws 50007 when user DMs are disabled — extremely common for moderation bots doing pre-ban DMs. (3) GuildBanManager.remove() throws 10026 Unknown Ban on double-unban — common race condition in moderation UIs. (4) ThreadChannel.setArchived() throws 40060 on locked threads — auto-archiving bots crash on locked threads. (5) GuildChannel.delete/edit throw 50013 permissions and 10003 unknown channel. 2 scanner concerns queued for Webhook and GuildChannel detector disambiguation.

jszip.omission_reason: JSZipStreamHelper.accumulate() is internally called by JSZipObject.async() — its error profile is fully covered by the file-async-no-try-catch and file-async-unsupported-type postconditions on async(). Direct use of accumulate() is rare in SaaS app code; the contract for async() provides sufficient coverage for the underlying stream accumulation errors.
jszip.notes: 1 new function contracted this pass: generateNodeStream() (2 postconditions: generate-node-stream-no-error-listener - process crash when stream emits error without listener; generate-node-stream-unsupported-env - synchronous throw in browser environments or when nodestream not available). Key insight: generateNodeStream() has a DIFFERENT error model from generateAsync() — it emits 'error' events on the returned ReadableStream rather than rejecting a Promise. Unhandled stream errors trigger Node.js UnhandledException and crash the process. The common pipe() pattern without .on('error') is a silent process crash risk. 2 new scanner concerns queued (concern-20260416-jszip-deepen-1 and -2).

sharp.omission_reason: All 4 async-callable functions are now contracted. Synchronous builder methods (resize, rotate, jpeg, png, etc.) return Sharp instance and surface errors via toFile()/toBuffer() — covered by existing postconditions.
sharp.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-15 (deepen-stream-1, pass 8): Added 2 functions covering metadata() and stats(). API surface analysis: only 4 methods return Promise — toFile, toBuffer, metadata, stats. All other Sharp methods are synchronous builder-pattern configurators that queue processing but do not return Promises themselves; their errors surface via toFile()/toBuffer() which are already contracted. Key insights: (1) metadata() rejects with native Error from libvips VError — "Input file is missing: <path>", "Input file has corrupt header", "Input file contains unsupported image format", "Input image exceeds pixel limit" — all confirmed from src/common.cc:559-583; (2) stats() also rejects from libvips but requires FULL pixel decode, not just header read — corrupt pixel data that passes metadata() header check will still fail stats(); (3) critical correctness bug: stats() on a transformed image (e.g. after .extract(region)) reads stats from ORIGINAL, not the transformed result — requires intermediate .toBuffer() call.

functions_intentionally_omitted: waitUntilTableExists (infrastructure waiter — not called in SaaS app runtime code)
waitUntilTableNotExists (infrastructure waiter — not called in SaaS app runtime code)

@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb.notes: 2 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) ExecuteTransactionCommand is the PartiQL equivalent of TransactWriteItemsCommand — same TransactionCanceledException + CancellationReasons contract, plus IdempotentParameterMismatchException for token reuse with different payloads. (2) BatchExecuteStatementCommand has a DIFFERENT partial failure pattern from BatchWriteItemCommand/BatchGetItemCommand — instead of response.UnprocessedItems, it uses response.Responses[].Error.Code per-item codes (DuplicateItem, ConditionalCheckFailed, AccessDenied, etc.). The previous pass incorrectly noted these two as having identical contracts to their counterparts. Previous pass also computed 0.71 = 5/7 (included omitted in denominator). This pass recomputed: 7 contracted / 9 total = 0.78.

@nestjs/jwt.omission_reason: decode() never throws — returns null for invalid tokens, no error contract needed. JwtService constructor is DI-managed (NestJS @Injectable), no direct instantiation error contracts needed.
@nestjs/jwt.notes: 1 new function contracted: sign() with 2 postconditions: (1) sign-sync-wrong-secret-provider — WrongSecretProviderError when async secretOrKeyProvider used with sync sign(); (2) sign-sync-string-payload-with-options — Error when string payload combined with disallowed sign options. Both confirmed from nestjs/jwt source code.

@aws-sdk/client-sqs.omission_reason: Admin/management operations (AddPermission, RemovePermission, SetQueueAttributes, TagQueue/UntagQueue, ListQueueTags) are infrastructure ops rarely in SaaS app runtime code. Read-only list operations (ListQueues, ListDeadLetterSourceQueues, ListMessageMoveTasks) have no unique behavioral contracts. GetQueueUrl and GetQueueAttributes are lookup operations covered by generic send() postcondition. DeleteQueue is a destructive admin operation rarely in app code. ChangeMessageVisibilityBatch follows identical silent-failure pattern to DeleteMessageBatch — covered by pattern documentation. StartMessageMoveTask/CancelMessageMoveTask are DLQ management ops with narrow adoption.
@aws-sdk/client-sqs.notes: Pass 2 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 13): Added send(SendMessageCommand) — the 1 remaining uncovered function. Key insights: (1) InvalidMessageContents thrown when body contains disallowed Unicode chars (#x00-#x08, #x0B-#x0C, #x0E-#x1F) — common when serializing arbitrary user input without sanitization; message NOT delivered, no partial delivery; (2) 7 KMS error types (KmsAccessDenied, KmsDisabled, KmsInvalidKeyUsage, KmsInvalidState, KmsNotFound, KmsOptInRequired, KmsThrottled) require different remediation than generic SQS errors — KmsThrottled is transient/retryable, all others require IAM/key-state fixes; (3) FIFO queue requires MessageGroupId — omitting it causes action failure, not caught by TypeScript type system. Coverage: 89% → 100% (9/9 contractable, 15 intentionally omitted). 3 scanner concerns queued (concerns -5 through -7).

compression.omission_reason: compression.filter is a synchronous boolean utility (no async, no throws, no error contract). Pure helper for custom filter composition. No behavioral contract value per ICP filter.
compression.notes: 2 new postconditions added: zlib-stream-unhandled-error (process crash — no stream.on(error) handler in middleware source) and sse-missing-flush (SSE data buffered, never delivered without res.flush). compression.filter() intentionally omitted (sync bool, no error contract). API surface is minimal: only 2 exports.

Most SaaS apps do not handle mobile push at the infrastructure level — they delegate to services like Firebase or use SNS platform application management infrequently. @aws-sdk/client-sns.notes: 3 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) PublishBatchCommand has critical silent partial failure — HTTP 200 returned even when individual messages fail; response.Failed[] contains per-message BatchResultErrorEntry objects; callers who don't check response.Failed silently lose notifications. (2) SubscribeCommand throws unique account-level errors: SubscriptionLimitExceededException (multi-tenant SaaS apps creating per-customer subscriptions can hit account limits during rapid onboarding) and FilterPolicyLimitExceededException (per-tenant message routing via filter policies). Also: SubscribeCommand returns 'pending confirmation' for HTTP/S endpoints — NOT an exception, a return value pattern. (3) CreateTopicCommand is idempotent but throws TopicLimitExceededException (100K standard / 1K FIFO topics per account) and transient ConcurrentAccessException during concurrent topic creation with tags. 3 scanner concerns queued for response-pattern and return-value pattern detection.

@octokit/rest.omission_reason: The @octokit/rest SDK wraps the entire GitHub REST API (1028 methods). The vast majority are admin/config GET/PUT/DELETE operations not called in typical SaaS app runtime code. The 35 contracted methods cover the core SaaS-relevant operations: the git commit pipeline (blob/tree/commit/ref), PR/issue automation, search, deployments, CI (checks/status), release management, and org access. The 993 omitted methods are admin infrastructure operations, read-only GETs with no distinct error contracts, or niche APIs used only by enterprise/specialized tooling.
@octokit/rest.notes: 10 new functions contracted in pass 4. Key insights: (1) pulls.createReview 422 is common from spam detection when automated reviews post too rapidly; (2) pulls.submitReview requires PENDING state — submitting already-submitted review throws 422; (3) repos.uploadReleaseAsset 422 on duplicate filename is a silent failure in retry loops — must delete old asset first; (4) repos.createFork is asynchronous (202) — git objects not available for seconds after response, must poll before using; (5) git.createTag is a TWO-STEP operation — createTag alone creates an orphaned object invisible to users; must follow with git.createRef; (6) git.deleteRef 422 when deleting default branch; (7) actions.cancelWorkflowRun 409 on terminal state (race condition). Coverage 62% → 80%.

The 10 contracted methods cover the core SaaS-relevant surface: Gmail send/list, Drive file create/list, Calendar events insert/list (with critical syncToken handling), Sheets values.update and batchUpdate, and the existing YouTube channels.list/videos.insert/drive.files.list.
googleapis.notes: 7 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) gmail.users.messages.send has two distinct quota error codes (dailyLimitExceeded vs rateLimitExceeded vs userRateLimitExceeded); (2) calendar.events.list syncToken expiry (410 Gone) is a Calendar-specific error almost universally unhandled — causes permanent sync failure after ~6 months; (3) drive.files.create storageQuotaExceeded returns 403 (not 507) with error.reason in response body; (4) sheets.spreadsheets.batchUpdate is atomic — all sub-requests fail if one is invalid; (5) Sheets has strict 60 write req/min/user quotas requiring exponential backoff.

@google-cloud/storage.omission_reason: Admin/infrastructure management operations (CORS, lifecycle, logging, retention, storage class, encryption keys, requester pays) are not SaaS app runtime code. Read-only GETs (getBuckets, isPublic) have no meaningful error contracts beyond generic HTTP. Soft-delete restore() and resumable upload internals (createResumableUpload) are niche operations rarely called directly. Signed POST policies are browser upload security operations with admin character.
@google-cloud/storage.notes: 7 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) file.move() is explicitly NOT atomic (documented in GCS SDK) — it's copy+delete, so errors can leave duplicate files; file.moveFileAtomic() is the safe alternative for same-bucket moves requiring atomicity. (2) file.rename() wraps move() and inherits the same non-atomic DATA_LOSS risk. (3) TransferManager.uploadManyFiles/downloadManyFiles use Promise.all() — first failure surfaces immediately but other concurrent operations continue; partial state must be inspected after failure. (4) TransferManager.downloadFileInChunks throws RequestError with code='CONTENT_DOWNLOAD_MISMATCH' on CRC32C validation failure — unique error code confirmed from source line 584. (5) TransferManager.uploadFileInChunks throws MultiPartUploadError (NOT ApiError) — unique error class confirmed from source lines 675-679, contains uploadId+partsMap for resuming interrupted uploads.

Admin/infrastructure ops (configureIndex, backup/restore, collection management) are not typical SaaS app code. Assistant methods are a separate product feature. inference.rerank has the same error contract as embed but significantly lower adoption.
@pinecone-database/pinecone.notes: Pass-5 (2026-04-16) added 7 new functions: deleteAll (destructive — deletes all vectors in namespace), createIndexForModel (integrated inference provisioning — pairs with upsertRecords/searchRecords), deleteNamespace (destructive — permanently deletes all records in namespace; multi-tenant offboarding), fetchByMetadata (new SDK method to fetch by metadata filter instead of IDs), cancelImport (import lifecycle cancel — idempotent, finished imports return 200), createNamespace (namespace management — PineconeConflictError 409 when name exists), listImports (import monitoring — read-only but makes network call). Coverage now 100% of non-omitted functions (21/21). 46 total async-callable methods discovered in SDK; 25 intentionally omitted (admin/read-only). Key new insights: (1) deleteAll() is the highest-stakes Pinecone operation — no undo; (2) createIndexForModel has the same error contract as createIndex (same API endpoint, additional embed config); (3) deleteNamespace 404 is acceptable in idempotent offboarding flows; (4) fetchByMetadata filter syntax errors throw at runtime not compile time; (5) cancelImport is idempotent — already-finished imports return 200, not an error; (6) createNamespace 409 conflict is common in provisioning code and should be handled idempotently.

functions_intentionally_omitted: createWSClient (WebSocket client factory, errors via callbacks not awaited)
@trpc/client.omission_reason: Factory functions (createTRPCClient, createWSClient, link factories) are configuration-only — they set up the client but are not directly awaited in application code. createWSClient() errors surface via synchronous WebSocketClientOptions.onError callback, not async throw. Link factories return TRPCLink objects synchronously. isTRPCClientError/getFetch are pure sync. WsClient.close() is async teardown with very low real-world failure rate.
@trpc/client.notes: 1 new function contracted: subscribe (2 postconditions: trpc-subscribe-missing-on-error + trpc-subscribe-auth-error-silent). Key insight: subscribe() errors are dispatched via optional onError callback (opts.onError?.(err) in TRPCUntypedClient.ts) — missing callback = silent error drop. Detection requires checking options object for onError property presence, different from query/mutate throw pattern. 2 scanner concerns queued for detection rule implementation.

morgan.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-12). Re-enumerated against morgan@1.11.0 + @types/morgan — same 4 exports (morgan factory + compile + token + format), no new exports introduced in 1.11. Promoted morgan.format from omitted to contracted with the format-name-overwrite-silent postcondition: the function body (lib/index.js lines 488-491) is `morgan[name] = fmt; return this` — no guard against overwriting the built-in named formats ('combined', 'common', 'default', 'dev', 'short', 'tiny') which are themselves registered via internal morgan.format() calls at module load (lines 178-209). Calling morgan.format('combined', myCustomFn) silently replaces the Apache combined log shape for ALL morgan middleware instances in the same process. Structurally identical to token-name-overwrite-silent but distinct surface — format names != token names, and the consequence is whole-line shape change vs. single-field rendering, so it warrants its own postcondition rather than being subsumed under the existing token rule. Effective coverage 4/4 = 1.0 (up from 3/4 = 0.75). Ground-truth fixture gained 3 cases: overwriteBuiltInCombinedFormat (SHOULD_FIRE), overwriteBuiltInDevFormat (SHOULD_FIRE), registerNamespacedCustomFormat (SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). Tests: 13/13 passing. No new scanner concerns queued — the existing contract-matcher detects this without a new detector rule. contract_version bumped 1.1.0 → 1.2.0; last_verified updated to 2026-06-12.

Pass 4 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 6): Added 2 functions — beta.observability.judges.judgeConversation and beta.observability.chatCompletionEvents.judge (ObservabilityError class with errorCode enum). @notionhq/client.notes: First depth pass (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 3). 11 new functions contracted. Key insights: (1) fileUploads is a 3-step flow (create->send->complete) — errors at any step require full flow restart; (2) oauth.token() authorization codes are single-use and expire quickly; missing try-catch in OAuth callbacks returns 500 to users; (3) databases.update() is PATCH and NOT auto-retried on 500/503 — schema may be partially applied; (4) databases.update() has a 50KB schema size limit with clear error message identifying the largest property; (5) trashed pages return 200 with in_trash:true NOT 404 — callers must check in_trash field; (6) comments.create() requires separate insert_comment capability distinct from content capabilities; (7) fileUploads.create filename limit is 900 bytes (not chars) — UTF-8 multi-byte chars reduce effective filename length.

functions_intentionally_omitted: streaming (no-file mode) — returns stream object, not Promise; out of scope for async await pattern detection
tar.omission_reason: When no file: option is specified, tar functions return a stream (Pack, Unpack, or Parser) rather than a Promise. Stream-based usage has different error handling semantics (stream.on("error", ...)) and is not covered by the async-await try-catch contract. Coverage score = 6 contracted / 7 total = 0.857 ~ 0.86.
tar.notes: 6 new function entries added this pass: list (1 postcondition: ENOENT/EACCES/TAR_BAD_ARCHIVE/TAR_ABORT), t (alias, 1 postcondition), replace (2 postconditions: TypeError for compressed archives + async EACCES/ENOENT), r (alias, 2 postconditions), update (2 postconditions: same as replace with mtime filter), u (alias, 2 postconditions). Key insights: (1) replace/update throw TypeError SYNCHRONOUSLY before any Promise for compressed archives — detected from options or first 2 file bytes (0x1f 0x8b gzip magic); (2) update() is a thin wrapper over replace() with an mtime filter; (3) list() rejects from Parser.on("error") which fires for TAR_BAD_ARCHIVE and TAR_ABORT.

pdf-parse.omission_reason: destroy() is a cleanup-only method that never throws in source (confirmed from PDFParse.js: simply calls this.doc.destroy() if doc exists, no error path). No behavioral contract needed.
pdf-parse.notes: Pass 1 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 5): Added 4 functions. Key insights: (1) getImage() throws same PDF exceptions as getText/getInfo via load() PLUS internal canvas errors (convertToRGBA: Unsupported image kind) for malformed embedded images in otherwise-valid PDFs; (2) getScreenshot() requires node-canvas in Node.js environment — missing canvas causes TypeError that is distinct from PDF parse errors; (3) getTable() parses vector drawing operators and returns empty pages array (not an error) for PDFs without tables — but throws on malformed PDFs; (4) getHeader() is unique — NEVER throws, catches all errors internally and returns { ok: false, error: ... }. The critical behavioral contract is that callers MUST check result.ok before trusting result.magic/status/size.

replicate.notes: Pass 8 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 8): First full depth pass. Enumerated complete API from replicate@1.4.0 index.d.ts and lib/ source. Added 6 new entries: stream() (no-error-handling + model-not-streaming-capable), wait() (prediction-failure as plain Error not ApiError + canceled-silent-non-throw), trainings.create() (no-error-handling + result-not-polled), files.create() (invalid-type-sync-throw + ApiError), deployments.predictions.create() (403-distinct-from-401), validateWebhook() (returns false not throws + missing-headers-throws). Key insights: (1) wait() throws plain Error not ApiError for prediction failures; (2) canceled predictions return silently without throwing; (3) validateWebhook() returns false for invalid signatures - unchecked return is security vuln; (4) stream() requires SSE support - throws after prediction is created; (5) trainings.create() returns immediately with status starting. 5 scanner concerns queued. 20/20 ground-truth tests pass.

generateVideos (long-running operation, Vertex AI primary; errors on polling not creation)
computeTokens (Vertex AI only, not available in Gemini API)
recontextImage (Vertex AI only Virtual Try-On, very niche)
batches.create (requires GCS bucket; admin-tier batch job; offline processing)
live.connect (WebSocket-based; errors delivered via callbacks, not thrown from Promise)
operations.getVideosOperation (polling utility, same error surface as generateVideos)
@google/genai.omission_reason: editImage/upscaleImage/computeTokens/recontextImage/segmentImage are all Vertex AI only and have the same ApiError surface as already-contracted generateImages or are too niche. generateVideos returns a long-running Operation that requires polling via operations.getVideosOperation — errors surface on polling not creation, and both are primarily Vertex AI. batches.create requires GCS bucket setup (admin-tier), runs offline, not a runtime SaaS call. live.connect establishes a WebSocket session — connection errors surface via onerror callback, not by throwing from the connect() Promise. operations.getVideosOperation is a polling utility for generateVideos, same error surface, already omitted.
@google/genai.notes: Pass 9 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2): First depth pass. Enumerated full API surface from dist/genai.d.ts (18 async-callable functions). Added 5 new function entries: sendMessage (1 postcondition: sequential chat turns with no try-catch), sendMessageStream (1 postcondition: streaming multi-turn with no try-catch), files.upload (1 postcondition: distinct non-ApiError throws for mimeType inference + VertexAI unsupported + protocol errors), countTokens (1 postcondition: pre-flight check that breaks silently if no try-catch), caches.create (1 postcondition: model-not-supported/token-threshold-not-met 400 errors). Coverage 0% → 100% of non-omitted functions (4 previously contracted + 5 new = 9 contracted, 9 omitted). Key insights: (1) chat.sendMessage() delegates to generateContent() so same ApiError surface, but uniquely stateful — failed turns recorded in comprehensive history; (2) files.upload() has 3 distinct Error (not ApiError) throw paths from the upload protocol layer; (3) caches.create() 400 error has 2 distinct causes (model-incompatible vs content-below-threshold) requiring different resolution; (4) countTokens() has independent rate limits from generateContent().

jwt-decode.omission_reason: jwt-decode exports exactly 1 callable function (jwtDecode). All exported interfaces and classes (InvalidTokenError, JwtHeader, JwtPayload, JwtDecodeOptions) are types/error classes, not callable API functions.
jwt-decode.notes: Pass 10 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1): First depth pass. jwt-decode has exactly 1 exported callable function (jwtDecode). It is synchronous (no async-callable functions). The existing contract already had 1 postcondition (invalid-token covering all 4 throw paths). This pass added 1 new postcondition: no-signature-validation (security misuse pattern — callers using decoded claims for access control without server-side JWT signature verification). Coverage before: 1 postcondition / 1 function = technically 100% of callable functions, but missing the security behavioral postcondition. Coverage after: 2 postconditions on 1 function = 100%. Key insight: jwt-decode README explicitly states 'This library doesn\'t validate the token' — the no-signature-validation postcondition documents the critical security contract that callers must not rely solely on jwtDecode() for auth/access decisions. Scanner concern queued for dataflow-based detection of decoded claims used in auth paths.

@auth0/nextjs-auth0.omission_reason: createFetcher() is a helper that wraps getAccessToken — its errors are already covered by Auth0Client.getAccessToken postconditions. startInteractiveLogin() returns a redirect NextResponse and has no distinct error contract beyond InvalidConfigurationError already covered by middleware. connectAccount() has low adoption and follows the same missing-session pattern as getAccessTokenForConnection which is already contracted.
@auth0/nextjs-auth0.notes: Pass 10 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2): First depth pass. Package was never deepened. Contract was v2/v3 only (4 standalone functions). Extended to v4 Auth0Client API (10 new functions, 23 total postconditions). Key insights: (1) getSession() returns null not throws — destructuring without null check is the #1 auth bug pattern in Next.js apps; (2) v4 getAccessToken() has a new MfaRequiredError path for MFA step-up flows that v2/v3 did not have; (3) updateSession() throws when unauthenticated (unlike getSession which returns null) — this asymmetry trips up developers; (4) MFA methods (getAuthenticators/challenge/verify/enroll) are chained via auth0.mfa.* — scanner needs multi-level property chain detection; (5) customTokenExchange() does NOT update the session — token must be stored by caller; (6) CIBA flow (getTokenByBackchannelAuth) throws two distinct error types: one before the network call (not-supported) and one after polling (denied-or-expired).

@aws-sdk/client-lambda.omission_reason: InvokeAsyncCommand is the deprecated legacy async invocation API — AWS officially deprecates it in favor of InvokeCommand with InvocationType='Event'. DeleteFunctionCommand is an admin-only destructive operation not called in SaaS app runtime code.
@aws-sdk/client-lambda.notes: 4 new functions contracted this pass: (1) UpdateFunctionCodeCommand — key insight: PreconditionFailedException (412) on RevisionId mismatch is unique to Update operations and requires distinct handling for deployment race conditions; CodeStorageExceededException (400) on 75 GB account quota. (2) UpdateFunctionConfigurationCommand — ResourceConflictException common during VPC provisioning which takes 60+ seconds; callers must waitUntilFunctionUpdated before chaining updates. (3) waitUntilPublishedVersionActive — same waiter pattern as waitUntilFunctionActive but for blue/green deploys after PublishVersionCommand; TimeoutError when version stays Pending. (4) CreateEventSourceMappingCommand — ResourceConflictException is idempotency signal (mapping already exists, safe to continue) — common pattern in SaaS deploy scripts that create SQS triggers on startup.

functions_intentionally_omitted: PageBlobClient.* (page blobs — VHD/disk storage, not typical SaaS)
@azure/storage-blob.omission_reason: AppendBlobClient and PageBlobClient are niche patterns (append logs, VHD disks) with no distinct error contracts beyond generic RestError. Admin operations (setMetadata, setHTTPHeaders, setTags, setImmutabilityPolicy, setLegalHold, createSnapshot, abortCopyFromURL) are infrastructure management ops rarely in SaaS app runtime code. Safe-variant methods (*IfExists) don't throw on missing resources so need no error contract. Low-level block operations (stageBlock, commitBlockList) are covered by uploadStream's postconditions. Blob Query SQL, listBlobsFlat/ByHierarchy iterators, and read-only GET operations have no distinct behavioral contracts beyond generic HTTP error handling. BlobBatchClient operations are a niche pattern rarely used in typical SaaS app code.
@azure/storage-blob.notes: Pass 1 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 12): Added 10 new function entries. Key insights: (1) BlobClient.delete() SnapshotsPresent — critical silent bug where dev environments (no snapshots) succeed but production (with Azure Backup/versioning) fails with 409. (2) uploadStream() multi-block failure leaves uncommitted blocks that are auto-cleaned after 7 days but data is lost immediately. (3) beginCopyFromURL() PollerLike pattern requires explicit pollUntilDone() — not awaiting leaves destination blob in pending state. (4) downloadToFile() does NOT clean up partial files on error — callers must handle cleanup. (5) getUserDelegationKey() RBAC permission requirement (Storage Blob Delegator role) commonly missing in production. (6) generateSasUrl() throws locally (no network call) when not using StorageSharedKeyCredential — breaks teams migrating to managed identity. Coverage improved from 0% to 76% (19/48 total, 29 intentionally omitted as admin/read-only/niche ops covering only ~8% of runtime use cases).

express-session.omission_reason: touch() and resetMaxAge() are synchronous methods that reset cookie maxAge — they do not interact with the session store, make no I/O, and have no error contracts. No meaningful behavioral contract beyond their documented return value.
express-session.notes: 4 session instance methods contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) destroy() callback must check err or server-side session persists despite client-side logout (split-brain); (2) regenerate() must check err on login — failure leaves old session ID in place (session fixation); (3) save() must be called and err checked before redirects — automatic save at response end may not fire in time; (4) reload() can produce "failed to load session" when session expires in long-lived connections — must redirect to login.

@aws-sdk/lib-storage.omission_reason: on('httpUploadProgress') is synchronous event registration (returns 'this'). It is not an async function and has no error contract — the callback is called with a Progress object containing loaded/total/part fields, none of which throw. The constructor is also synchronous. These are correctly excluded from coverage calculations.
@aws-sdk/lib-storage.notes: 3 new postconditions added this pass: (1) upload-done-already-called — Upload instances are single-use; retry logic must create a new Upload instance. (2) abort-does-not-clean-up-synchronously — abort() only signals AbortController; done() must still reject before markUploadAsAborted() runs. (3) leave-parts-on-error-prevents-cleanup — leavePartsOnError=true disables automatic AbortMultipartUploadCommand, causing S3 cost accumulation. Also added: abort-before-done-is-noop (INFO) documenting that abort() before done() is safe. Key insight: Upload class is single-use by design — the 'sent' flag prevents re-execution of done().

functions_intentionally_omitted: transaction.cloneTransaction (niche — recreate auth for different customer)
transaction.credit (standalone credit — niche, separate from refund)
transaction.search (analytics, paginated, no distinct throw profile)
paymentMethod.grant (marketplace niche — grant third-party access to vault token)
creditCard.create/find/update/delete (direct credit card ops — rare, paymentMethod preferred)
creditCard.fromNonce (low-level vault op, rarely used directly)
usBankAccount.find/sale (niche US bank ACH, very low adoption in SaaS)
documentUpload.create (evidence file upload — sub-operation of dispute flow)
webhookNotification.verify (synchronous, not async, used in challenge verification only)
braintree.omission_reason: Admin/niche operations with same generic error profile as contracted ops. Transaction analytics search and pagination ops have no distinct behavioral contracts. Low-level credit card vault ops (creditCard.*) are superseded by paymentMethod.* in modern Braintree usage. Marketplace-specific grant/revoke, US bank ACH, and dispute sub-operations (addEvidence) are covered by their parent dispute.finalize postcondition. webhookNotification.verify is synchronous. PayPal account vault ops are rarely called from SaaS app runtime code.
braintree.notes: First deepen pass (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 2). Added 12 new function entries. Key insights: (1) transaction.void() has a critical silent failure pattern via result.success=false — settled transactions cannot be voided but do NOT throw (they return result.success=false with error 91504). (2) transaction.submitForSettlement() is the 'capture' step in authorize-then-capture flows — failure means merchant never collects payment. (3) subscription.cancel() NotFoundError is extremely common in account-deletion flows (stale IDs, already-canceled). (4) subscription.retryCharge() has dual failure modes: thrown exception (infrastructure) AND result.success=false (card declined) — BOTH must be handled. (5) webhookNotification.parse() is security-critical: InvalidSignatureError indicates tampered payload and the handler must NOT process the notification. (6) dispute.finalize() has a 'forgot to call' anti-pattern: evidence added but finalize never called = silent loss of chargeback dispute.

functions_intentionally_omitted: runs.subscribeToRun (realtime SSE stream, no thrown error contract)
schedules.retrieve (read-only GET, same error profile as create/update)
schedules.del (admin op, same profile as create/update)
queues.overrideConcurrencyLimit (admin op, same profile as pause)
wait.listTokens (read-only pagination — inherits createToken error model)
wait.retrieveToken (read-only GET — inherits createToken error model)
wait.for / wait.until (internal task utilities, no throw contract beyond framework errors)
envvars.* (admin/CI op, not runtime SaaS code)
ai.tool / hooks.* / cache.createCache (registration/factory, not async-callable network ops)
usage.measure (utility wrapper, no distinct error contract)
metadata.* (set/append/increment etc — synchronous mutators with no network throw; only flush() has a contract and is covered)
auth.configure / auth.withPublicToken / withTriggerPublicToken / withAuth (auth scopers with no distinct contract — wrap createPublicToken)
@trigger.dev/sdk.omission_reason: Read-only GETs/lists inherit fetch/list contracts. SSE streams use yield-not-throw. schedules.timezones is static data. Auth scoper functions (withPublicToken et al) are wrappers around createPublicToken which IS contracted. envvars.* are admin/CI ops not runtime app code. promptManagement is preview-API with very low adoption. metadata.set/append/etc are synchronous mutators (only flush has a network call worth contracting). ai.tool/hooks.*/cache.createCache are registration/factory APIs that return values rather than perform async network ops.
@trigger.dev/sdk.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-18T21:31:22Z) — +3 new high-impact APIs from full 4.x re-enumeration. Re-installed @trigger.dev/sdk@4.4.6 and walked dist/commonjs/v3/**/*.d.ts. Added webhooks.constructEvent (NEW IN 4.x — security-critical WebhookError with two distinct patterns: no-try-catch crash + swallowed-200 vuln); wait.forToken (canonical pause-and-wait with two contracts — sync throw outside task context + WaitpointTimeoutError on unwrap); query.execute (NEW TRQL surface with full ApiError hierarchy). 5 scanner concerns queued (one per new postcondition). 19 functions explicitly omitted with documented reason — covers all read-only variants, admin/CI ops, preview APIs, and synchronous mutators. Coverage 22/44=0.50 → 25/44=0.57 raw; effective (contracted / non-omitted-errorable) = 25/25 = 1.0.

square.omission_reason: Read-only GET operations with no domain-specific error contracts beyond generic HTTP 401/404/429. These are retrieval-only endpoints with no financial risk beyond authentication errors already covered by write operation contracts.
square.notes: 5 new functions contracted in pass 5: catalog.batchUpsert (3 postconditions: concurrent-update 429, IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSED, partial-batch-failure), disputes.accept (2 postconditions: financial-debit-immediate, deadline-missed), disputes.submitEvidence (2 postconditions: irrevocable-evidence, dashboard-exclusion), giftCards.activities.create (3 postconditions: insufficient-funds/GIFT_CARD_AVAILABLE_AMOUNT, pending-state-trap, TEMPORARY_ERROR-503), giftCards.create (2 postconditions: pending-state-trap-silent-success, invalid-GAN). Key new insights: (1) catalog.batchUpsert allows only one concurrent update per seller account — parallel sync jobs will get RATE_LIMITED 429; (2) disputes.accept immediately debits seller account including from linked bank account if balance insufficient — no undo; (3) disputes.submitEvidence is irrevocable AND locks sellers out of Dashboard management for that dispute; (4) gift cards start in PENDING state — must activate before issuing to customers; (5) GIFT_CARD_AVAILABLE_AMOUNT is the specific error code for insufficient gift card balance.

functions_intentionally_omitted: OAuth2Client.revokeToken (cleanup path, same GaxiosError profile as getToken)
OAuth2Client.getFederatedSignonCerts (internal auth infra, rarely in SaaS app code)
google-auth-library.omission_reason: Same omissions as prior passes. revokeToken/revokeCredentials are cleanup operations with no distinct error contract beyond generic GaxiosError. getFederatedSignonCerts/getIapPublicKeys are internal signing certificate operations called inside verifyIdToken, rarely called directly in SaaS app code. getRequestHeaders, authorizeRequest, request, and fetch are pass-through wrappers that propagate errors from getAccessToken/getClient — contracting them separately would duplicate already-contracted error surfaces.
google-auth-library.notes: Pass 1 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 4): First depth pass — 8 new functions contracted. Pass 2 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 6): Added JWT.fetchIdToken(). Pass 3 (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-2 pass 12): Added GoogleAuth.fromStream(), GoogleAuth.getCredentials(), Impersonated.fetchIdToken(). Total API surface expanded from 16 to 19 (3 functions not previously counted). Key insights: (1) fromStream() throws Error(Must pass in a stream...) on null input — common in lambda/cloud function code that reads credentials from secrets; (2) getCredentials() throws Error(Unable to find credentials in current environment) — distinct from getClient() which throws NO_ADC_FOUND — when running on non-GCE without any JSON credentials loaded; (3) Impersonated.fetchIdToken() makes TWO network calls (source getAccessToken + IAM generateIdToken) and reformats GaxiosError messages with IAM status codes (PERMISSION_DENIED/NOT_FOUND: unable to impersonate). 3 scanner concerns queued (deepen-9, -10, -11).

functions_intentionally_omitted: getWebhooks, getWebhook, createWebhook, editWebhook, deleteWebhook (webhook admin)
getMessageStreams, getMessageStream, editMessageStream, createMessageStream, archiveMessageStream, unarchiveMessageStream (stream admin)
AccountClient: getServers, getServer, createServer, editServer, deleteServer (account admin)
AccountClient: getDomains, getDomain, createDomain, editDomain, deleteDomain (DNS admin)
AccountClient: verifyDomainDKIM, verifyDomainReturnPath, verifyDomainSPF, rotateDomainDKIM (DNS verification)

AccountClient: getSenderSignature, getSenderSignatures, createSenderSignature, editSenderSignature, deleteSenderSignature, resendSenderSignatureConfirmation, verifySenderSignatureSPF, requestNewDKIMForSenderSignature (admin)
AccountClient: requestDataRemoval, getDataRemovalStatus (GDPR admin - rarely in app runtime)
postmark.omission_reason: Read-only GET/list operations (analytics, bounce details, message details, suppression list) have no meaningful domain-specific error contracts beyond generic HTTP 401/429/5xx already covered by sendEmail postconditions. Webhook, message stream, and inbound rule management are admin configuration operations rarely called in SaaS app runtime. AccountClient methods are account-level admin operations (server provisioning, domain DNS management, sender signature management, GDPR data removal) not called in application request handlers.
postmark.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) createSuppressions/deleteSuppressions return 200 OK with per-item Status field — callers must check Status even on HTTP success (silent partial failure pattern similar to AWS S3 DeleteObjectsCommand); (2) SpamComplaint suppressions cannot be deleted — deleteSuppressions always returns Status: Failed for SpamComplaint addresses; (3) createTemplate has 100-template per-server limit — throws ApiInputError when exceeded; (4) editTemplate throws ApiInputError when template alias/ID not found — common in migration scripts.

@azure/identity.omission_reason: All async-callable functions contracted. getDefaultAzureCredential() is a synchronous factory (no I/O). serializeAuthenticationRecord() and deserializeAuthenticationRecord() are synchronous helpers — deserializeAuthenticationRecord can throw on unsupported version strings but this is a narrow configuration-error case with no network I/O.
@azure/identity.notes: 2 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) getBearerTokenProvider() returns a callback that throws Error("Failed to get access token") when the underlying credential fails — the error message is generic and loses type information from CredentialUnavailableError/AuthenticationError; callers must wrap the callback in try-catch. (2) InteractiveBrowserCredential.authenticate() returns AuthenticationRecord | undefined — never throws on user cancellation (returns undefined) but DOES throw CredentialUnavailableError on OAuth infrastructure failures; callers must null-check the return value before using serializeAuthenticationRecord().

cookie-parser.omission_reason: All 5 exported functions have meaningful behavioral contracts. No omissions.
cookie-parser.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-16 (deepen-stream-2 pass 7): Added 4 new helper function entries. cookie-parser is entirely synchronous — no async functions. Key insights: (1) signedCookie() returns false (not exception) for tampered cookies — callers using truthiness check instead of === false miss security violations; (2) signedCookies() MUTATES the input cookies object by deleting signed cookie keys — destructive side-effect surprises developers who pass req.cookies directly; (3) signedCookies() puts false in returned object for invalid signatures — callers iterating without false-check silently pass tampered data; (4) JSONCookie() returns undefined (NOT the original string) for non-JSON cookies — breaks when used as parse-or-passthrough; (5) JSONCookies() mutates input in-place and returns same object reference. 5 scanner concerns queued for detection rule implementation.

multer.omission_reason: memoryStorage() is a pure factory function that returns an in-memory storage engine with no I/O operations and no distinct error surface. Memory allocation failures are OOM conditions handled at process level, not catchable per-upload.
multer.notes: 4 new function entries added. Key insights: (1) fields() throws LIMIT_UNEXPECTED_FILE when a file is received for an unlisted field or beyond maxCount — same error surface as single/array but needs separate entry for completeness; (2) any() is a critical security risk when used without fileFilter — accepts ALL files from ANY field; official docs explicitly warn against using any() as global middleware; postcondition: any-unrestricted-upload-no-filter; (3) none() throws LIMIT_UNEXPECTED_FILE when a file is included in a text-only form — missing error middleware leaves this as unhandled 500; (4) diskStorage() calls fs.mkdirSync synchronously when destination is a string — EACCES or ENOENT throws at startup; function-form destination is safer. Also: write stream errors (ENOSPC, EACCES) from DiskStorage._handleFile propagate to next(err) and require Express error middleware.

functions_intentionally_omitted: ping (health check, no distinct error contract)
setLoggingLevel (admin utility, no distinct error contract)
complete (autocomplete utility, same error profile as protocol layer)
sendLoggingMessage on McpServer (notification wrapper — now contracted as separate entry)
listRoots on Server (same error profile as listTools)
experimental.tasks.listTasks (read-only list, no distinct error contract)
createElicitationCompletionNotifier (sync factory, returns callback - not async)
discoverOAuthMetadata (low-level discovery utility, error profile covered by auth())
SSEClientTransport.finishAuth (deprecated transport, same error profile as StreamableHTTPClientTransport.finishAuth)
StreamableHTTPClientTransport.resumeStream (same error profile as connect — delegates to _startOrAuthSse)
createMcpExpressApp (sync factory, no async throw contract)
mcpAuthRouter (sync Express middleware factory, no async throw contract)
@modelcontextprotocol/sdk.omission_reason: ping/setLoggingLevel/complete/listResourceTemplates/unsubscribeResource/listRoots carry same error profiles as already-contracted methods. Notification methods (sendResourceListChanged, sendToolListChanged, sendPromptListChanged, sendRootsListChanged, sendLoggingMessage notification wrapper, sendResourceUpdated) are fire-and-forget with no distinct error contracts. discoverOAuth* utilities share error profile with auth(). SSEClientTransport.finishAuth is deprecated transport same as StreamableHTTPClientTransport.finishAuth. resumeStream delegates to _startOrAuthSse same profile as connect(). createMcpExpressApp and mcpAuthRouter are sync factories.
@modelcontextprotocol/sdk.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-2 2026-04-16): +7 functions contracted. Key insights: (1) WebSocketClientTransport.start() — new non-deprecated transport, throws AlreadyStarted + WebSocket onerror on connection failure; (2) SSEClientTransport.start() — still common during SSE→StreamableHTTP migration, throws UnauthorizedError("No auth provider") when server requires OAuth but no authProvider configured + SseError on connection failure; (3) SSEServerTransport.handlePostMessage() — deprecated but heavily deployed, throws on wrong Content-Type (non-application/json POSTs), DNS rebinding rejections go to onerror not throw; (4) StdioServerTransport.start() — server-side stdio transport, throws AlreadyStarted on double connect; (5) ProxyOAuthServerProvider.exchangeAuthorizationCode() — throws ServerError on upstream token exchange failure; (6) ProxyOAuthServerProvider.exchangeRefreshToken() — throws ServerError on upstream refresh failure; (7) McpServer.sendLoggingMessage() — fixed schema validation errors (wrong field names code/fix→condition/required_handling, invalid enum values). 6 new scanner concerns queued (concerns 19-24). Pass 4 (deepen-stream-2 2026-06-12): +4 functions enumerated against v1.29.0. Key insights: (1) fetchToken() is the new unified token-fetch helper added in v1.20+ — wraps executeTokenRequest with provider.prepareTokenRequest() hook for client_credentials/private_key_jwt grants; throws synchronously on missing params AND throws OAuthError subclasses on non-2xx responses. (2) discoverOAuthServerInfo() combines RFC 9728 protected-resource-metadata with RFC 8414 authorization-server-metadata discovery; swallows RFC 9728 errors silently but propagates RFC 8414 errors. (3) experimental.tasks.createMessageStream() returns an AsyncGenerator yielding ResponseMessage<CreateMessageResult> with type ∈ {taskCreated, taskStatus, result, error}; consumers must use takeResult() helper OR explicitly branch on message.type === error — a for-await loop that only checks message.type === result silently drops errors. (4) experimental.tasks.elicitInputStream() shares the same iterator contract — same silent-error-drop failure mode. 4 new scanner concerns queued (concerns 25-28): two standard throwing-function patterns (fetchToken, discoverOAuthServerInfo), one new AsyncGenerator-message-type-discrimination pattern, one capability-precheck pattern. Pass 13 (deepen-stream-2 2026-06-18): +3 functions targeting the stream-consumer helpers — takeResult and toArrayAsync from shared/responseMessage, and experimental.tasks.requestStream the generic-request sibling of callToolStream. Three concerns queued (29/30/31). takeResult has THREE throw paths: 'error' ResponseMessage embedded McpError, 'No result in stream.' when generator terminates without yielding result (real for transport drops), and iteration-level propagation. toArrayAsync has a DUAL gap — both the iteration-error rejection AND the post-call array-error inspection (silent failure if caller treats array as success without scanning for type==='error' entries). experimental.tasks.requestStream is the same AsyncGenerator pattern as the other *Stream() methods — detection rule should generalize. Coverage 34/43=0.79 → 37/43=0.86 raw.

jose.omission_reason: flattenedVerify/generalVerify/flattenedDecrypt/generalDecrypt are niche JWS/JWE serialization formats with identical error contracts to the compact variants already covered.
