CompactEncrypt/FlattenedEncrypt/GeneralEncrypt/CompactSign/FlattenedSign/GeneralSign are lower-level builders rarely used directly — SignJWT and EncryptJWT (contracted) are the idiomatic patterns. exportSPKI/exportPKCS8/exportJWK only fail when key is non-extractable (set during generation) — a programming error caught immediately in dev, not a production runtime concern. generateSecret has no documented error cases beyond invalid alg (same contract as generateKeyPair). calculateJwkThumbprint/calculateJwkThumbprintUri are pure computation utilities with no meaningful error contracts beyond invalid key type.
jose.notes: 8 new function entries contracted. Key insights: (1) jwtDecrypt() is the encrypted-JWT equivalent of jwtVerify — throws JWEDecryptionFailed (wrong key/tampered auth tag) + JWTExpired + JWTClaimValidationFailed — commonly used in secure cookie-based session auth; (2) importSPKI/importPKCS8 are high-impact startup-time failures — PEM strings from env vars frequently have truncation/escaping issues that throw TypeError at server boot; (3) compactDecrypt() throws JWEDecryptionFailed on authentication tag failure — critical for any app using JWE for data encryption at rest; (4) compactVerify() is the lower-level JWS verification without JWT claim checks — throws JWSSignatureVerificationFailed on tampered tokens; (5) generateKeyPair() throws JOSENotSupported for unsupported algorithms or sub-2048 RSA keys — affects key rotation flows; (6) EncryptJWT.encrypt() can throw JOSENotSupported/JOSEAlgNotAllowed in sessions that use encrypted tokens. Source confirmed from panva/jose GitHub source: src/jwt/decrypt.ts, src/jwe/compact/decrypt.ts, src/jwe/flattened/decrypt.ts, src/key/import.ts, src/key/generate_key_pair.ts, src/util/errors.ts.

functions_intentionally_omitted: parse (low adoption for direct use; error profile identical to compile)
handlebars.omission_reason: parse() is rarely called directly in application code — it is an AST utility used by handlebars internals and advanced users building custom preprocessing pipelines. Its error profile (Handlebars.Exception on syntax errors) is identical to compile(), which is already contracted. parseWithoutProcessing(), registerHelper, registerPartial, create(), SafeString, and escapeExpression are sync utilities with no meaningful error contracts.
handlebars.notes: First depth pass (2026-04-16, deepen-stream-1 pass 10). 3 new function entries added. Key insights: (1) Template execution (templateFn(context)) is distinct from compile() — it throws Handlebars.Exception when a partial referenced in the template is not registered at execution time; (2) strict: true compile option causes template execution to throw on any undefined variable access — common security hardening pattern without proper error handling; (3) prototype access denial (introduced 4.6.0) is a SILENT failure — not an exception but renders blank values, hard to detect; (4) precompile() shares error profile with compile() but is used in build pipelines where developers incorrectly assume the build tool catches exceptions; (5) template() throws on version mismatch between precompile-time and runtime handlebars versions — critical for microservice deployments.

functions_intentionally_omitted: startChat (sync factory — returns ChatSession, no async throw)
ChatSession.getHistory (read-only, returns cached history — no network call)
GoogleAIFileManager.deleteFile (cleanup path, same error profile as uploadFile)
GoogleAICacheManager.delete (cleanup path, same error profile as create)
@google/generative-ai.omission_reason: startChat() is a synchronous factory returning a ChatSession — no async throw. getHistory() uses cached in-memory history with no network call. listFiles/getFile/list/get are read-only GETs with no distinct error contract beyond the generic GoogleGenerativeAIFetchError already covered by contracted write operations. deleteFile and GoogleAICacheManager.delete are cleanup operations in finally/error paths — failing silently is common accepted behavior and the error profile is identical to the contracted create operations.
@google/generative-ai.notes: First depth pass 2026-04-16 (deepen-stream-1 pass 11). 4 new functions contracted: batchEmbedContents (2 postconditions: batch-network-error for 429/403/400 failures, batch-input-validation for token limit exceeded), GoogleAIFileManager.uploadFile (2 postconditions: upload-network-error for HTTP failures + file size/quota limits, upload-file-processing-failed for missing ACTIVE state polling), GoogleAICacheManager.create (2 postconditions: cache-create-validation-error for missing model/conflicting TTL, cache-create-api-error for FAILED_PRECONDITION when below minimum token count), GoogleAICacheManager.update (1 postcondition: cache-update-not-found for 404 when cache has expired). Key insights: (1) uploadFile() does NOT block until ACTIVE — callers must poll getFile() after upload; (2) GoogleAICacheManager.create() throws synchronously (not via network) when model field is missing or when both ttlSeconds and expireTime are specified; (3) batchEmbedContents failures are silent in RAG pipelines — entire batch fails atomically on 429.

functions_intentionally_omitted: validateExecutionArgs / validateSubscriptionArgs (sync, only throw on invalid schema)
graphql.omission_reason: createSourceEventStream is a lower-level subscription primitive whose error contracts are fully covered by subscribe() (which calls it internally). parseValue/parseType are rarely called directly in app code — they are SDL-building utilities used by tooling. graphqlSync/executeSync are synchronous and have no distinct async error surface. The executeRootSelectionSet / experimentalExecuteRootSelectionSet / legacyExecuteRootSelectionSet helpers take pre-validated args and are internal to graphql-js's executor harness — production code uses the non-RootSelectionSet variants. mapSourceToResponseEvent is a low-level subscription stream helper covered by subscribe's contract. validateExecutionArgs / validateSubscriptionArgs only throw on invalid schemas (deployment bugs caught at startup, not runtime).
graphql.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-18): closed the partial-result-on-abort callout from the 2026-06-15 drift sweep. +2 functions covering v17's incremental delivery executors (experimentalExecuteIncrementally + legacyExecuteIncrementally) and +1 postcondition on execute() for AbortedGraphQLExecutionError. The incremental functions are first-of-kind for discriminated-union return types in nark — 4 new postconditions total (2 discrimination + 2 abort-signal) added across these two functions. Source attribution: graphql@17.0.1 installed at /private/tmp/claude/bc-deepen/graphql/node_modules/graphql; all postconditions cite the package's .d.ts + .js + the GraphQL incremental delivery RFC. Four scanner concerns queued (2026-06-18 concerns 1-4): execute-abort detection, experimental-incremental detection, legacy-incremental detection, and a new cross-cutting discriminated-union pattern. Original pass-2 notes preserved: graphql() never rejects (errors in result.errors[]); subscribe() returns AsyncGenerator | ExecutionResult (discriminated union); subscribe non-GraphQLErrors propagate; buildSchema throws GraphQLError (syntax) OR plain Error (semantic).

ethers.omission_reason: Read-only GETs have no distinct error contract beyond generic SERVER_ERROR/NETWORK_ERROR already captured by the provider.send / generic provider error model. ENS utilities (resolveName/lookupAddress) are narrow-adoption helpers. wallet.signMessage is an async wrapper over sync crypto with no network errors. JsonRpcProvider.listAccounts delegates entirely to provider.send. HDNodeWallet factory methods are synchronous despite being on a class with async methods.
ethers.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-16 (deepen-stream-2 pass 7): +3 functions contracted. Key insights: (1) BrowserProvider.getSigner() triggers eth_requestAccounts and throws ACTION_REJECTED (error.action=requestAccess) when user denies the MetaMask popup -- most common dApp wallet error; (2) Wallet.encrypt() with default scrypt N=131072 requires ~128MB RAM -- crashes Lambda at 128MB; progressCallback not awaited in serverless contexts; (3) JsonRpcProvider.getSigner() throws a plain Error (not EthersError) so isError() does not apply -- critical documentation gap. Newly discovered but skipped: provider.waitForBlock() (NOT_IMPLEMENTED), Contract.queryTransaction() (throws @TODO), Contract.getDeployedCode() (read-only), provider.getTransactionResult() (read-only). 3 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260416-ethers-deepen-5 through -7). Pass 8 (2026-04-17, deepen-stream-2): +2 functions contracted: Contract.method (contract-method-call-exception: CALL_EXCEPTION when contract reverts; contract-method-no-signer: UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION when no Signer attached), provider.send (provider-send-unsupported-method: UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION for unknown RPC methods like trace_transaction; provider-send-network-error: SERVER_ERROR/NETWORK_ERROR on connectivity failure). Coverage 67% -> 75% (18/24). 2 scanner concerns queued. | Pass 4 (2026-06-12, deepen-stream-1): +2 functions: wallet.signTransaction (signtransaction-from-address-mismatch + signtransaction-invalid-transaction-fields — INVALID_ARGUMENT on tx.from mismatch and Transaction.from() field validation, critical for offline signing / relayer / MEV flows) and wallet.authorize (authorize-no-provider-for-auto-fill + authorize-invalid-delegate-address — EIP-7702 / Pectra, new in ethers v6.16.0, UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION when wallet has no provider for chainId/nonce auto-fill, INVALID_ARGUMENT on bad delegate address from API response). Coverage 75% → 77% (20/26 contracted, 11 omitted, 2 new concerns queued: concern-20260612-ethers-deepen-1, -2). | Pass 5 (2026-06-18, deepen-stream-2 pass 4): +1 function — Signer.populateTransaction at ethers@6.17.0. The canonical pre-signing pipeline used by sendTransaction internally and called directly in relayer / MEV / cold-wallet / hardware-wallet / meta-transaction flows. 4 postconditions captured from lib.esm/providers/abstract-signer.js source: populatetransaction-no-provider (UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION when this.provider is null via checkProvider), populatetransaction-chainid-mismatch (INVALID_ARGUMENT when tx.chainId disagrees with provider.getNetwork().chainId), populatetransaction-mixed-fee-fields (INVALID_ARGUMENT when tx mixes pre-EIP-1559 gasPrice with EIP-1559 maxFeePerGas — two distinct assertArgument messages), populatetransaction-network-fee-model-unsupported (UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION with operation=getGasPrice for EIP-1559-only chains rejecting legacy fees, or operation=populateTransaction for legacy chains rejecting EIP-1559 fees). Coverage 77% → 81% (21/26 contracted). 2 scanner concerns queued: concern-20260618-ethers-deepen-1 (detection rule), -2 (chained method resolution).

ws.omission_reason: createWebSocketStream() is a Duplex stream wrapper around an existing WebSocket — its error contracts are identical to the WebSocket error event already contracted in the WebSocket constructor entry. The stream re-emits ws errors so all error handling patterns already documented cover this function.
ws.notes: 3 new functions contracted: ping (2 postconditions: ping-before-open throws synchronously when CONNECTING; ping-callback-receives-error passes error to callback when CLOSING/CLOSED), handleUpgrade (2 postconditions: missing wsClientError handler causes silent drop of malformed requests; calling in non-noServer mode double-handles socket), WebSocketServer.close (2 postconditions: callback receives Error when server already stopped; existing connections not terminated). 3 scanner concerns queued for bc-scanner-upgrade. Key insights: (1) ping() is the most common heartbeat mechanism but throws if called before open event; (2) handleUpgrade noServer pattern is pervasive in Next.js and custom servers; (3) WebSocketServer.close() uses callback-based errors unlike most Node.js server APIs.

@libsql/client.omission_reason: transaction.batch/executeMultiple are variants of client.batch/executeMultiple within tx context — same error profile, covered by transaction.execute. close() and reconnect() are synchronous (close) or effectively synchronous (reconnect) and do not throw expected runtime errors. sync() was previously omitted but promoted to contracted this pass due to SYNC_NOT_SUPPORTED footgun on http/ws clients.
@libsql/client.notes: Pass 6 (2026-06-11, deepen-stream-2 pass 2): Promoted sync() from intentionally-omitted to contracted. v0.17.3 (patch from v0.17.2 — no new API surface, dependency updates only). Key insight: sync() throws LibsqlError(SYNC_NOT_SUPPORTED) on http/ws clients — most common Turso connection type. This is a real DOWNTIME risk for apps that copy sync() calls from embedded-replica tutorials into cloud-connected codebases. 3 postconditions: sync-wrong-client-type (error), sync-network-failure (warning), sync-client-closed (error). Coverage: 8/13 = 0.62 → 9/13 = 0.69 raw; effective contracted/non-omitted = 9/9 = 1.0.

@upstash/qstash.omission_reason: queue.get/list are read-only GETs with no meaningful error contracts beyond generic HTTP. schedules.get/list are read-only GETs. messages.get is a read-only GET. dlq.listMessages is a read-only paginated GET. events() is a deprecated alias for logs() — contractually identical. FlowControl methods (get/getGlobalParallelism/pause/resume/pin/unpin/resetRate) are advanced admin operations with narrow real-world adoption and the same HTTP error profile as other contracted methods. chat() is deprecated as of 2.7.17 and will be removed in 3.0.0.
@upstash/qstash.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass: queue.delete (2 postconditions: api-error + data-loss for pending messages), messages.cancel (2 postconditions: api-error + result-unchecked — empty array returns {cancelled:0} not exception), dlq.delete (2 postconditions: api-error + cursor-not-checked), dlq.retry (2 postconditions: api-error + cursor-not-checked), logs (2 postconditions: api-error + cursor-not-checked). Key insights: (1) queue.delete() is IRREVERSIBLE — pending messages are silently lost without SDK warning; callers must check lag first. (2) messages.cancel() returns {cancelled:0} for empty string[] input — no throw. (3) dlq.delete() and dlq.retry() require cursor loops for complete bulk operations. (4) logs() is paginated — max 1000 per page; cursor loop required for full history. All sources confirmed from qstash-js GitHub source.

functions_intentionally_omitted: basicApi.getPage (read-only list pagination, same errors as getById)
@hubspot/api-client.omission_reason: getPage/batchApi.read are read-only operations with no distinct error contracts beyond the generic HTTP errors already covered by getById. oauth.getAccessToken is a read-only token info GET. oauth.refreshTokens archive is rarely called in SaaS app code. crm.imports is an admin-level file import API used infrequently in production SaaS request handlers.
@hubspot/api-client.notes: Both deepen-stream-1 and deepen-stream-2 worked concurrently on pass 6. Stream-1 added: batchApi.upsert (207 partial-failure silent pattern), basicApi.merge (414 merge history limit), basicApi.purge (GDPR delete, COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION label), crm.getAll (OOM risk from unbounded pagination). Stream-2 added: batchApi.create/update batch-specific postconditions (207 partial failure), timeline.eventsApi.create (OAuth-only endpoint), oauth.tokensApi.create (invalid_grant single-use codes). Critical finding: HubSpot batch operations return HTTP 207 with partial failures without throwing — callers MUST check response.errors[].

posthog-node.omission_reason: reloadFeatureFlags() swallows errors internally via .catch() — never propagates to caller (confirmed from feature-flags.js:340-345). waitForLocalEvaluationReady() returns false on timeout (not throw). captureImmediate/identifyImmediate/aliasImmediate catch errors internally with console.error — fire-and-forget from caller perspective. captureExceptionImmediate is niche error-tracking infrastructure. enable/disable are admin toggles with no unique error contract.
posthog-node.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) shutdown throws a STRING rejection (not Error) on timeout — 'Timeout while shutting down PostHog. Some events may not have been sent.'; (2) getRemoteConfigPayload throws synchronously (not async) if personalApiKey is missing; (3) flush() throws PostHogFetchHttpError/PostHogFetchNetworkError from @posthog/core, not generic Error; (4) reloadFeatureFlags, captureImmediate, identifyImmediate, aliasImmediate all internally swallow errors — do NOT require try-catch; (5) waitForLocalEvaluationReady returns false (not throws) on timeout. Evidence confirmed from posthog-node v5.29.2 source and @posthog/core source.

better-sqlite3.omission_reason: prepare() errors only surface at run/get/all call sites (already contracted). pragma() is read-only. function/aggregate/table are registration methods with no async error contracts. loadExtension() is niche with no standard error contract. close/serialize/defaultSafeIntegers/unsafeMode have no error contracts. Statement config methods (pluck/expand/raw/bind/columns/safeIntegers) are synchronous configuration with no throws.
better-sqlite3.notes: Deepen pass 8. 4 new functions contracted (backup, constructor, transaction, iterate) with 7 postconditions. backup-unhandled-rejection, transaction-async-function-error, iterate-deferred-execution-error are scanner-detected (ground-truth tests pass). database-file-must-exist-error and transaction-inner-exception-rollback require new scanner rules (concerns queued). Effective coverage = 8/8 non-omitted = 1.0.

functions_intentionally_omitted: add (stage files — no distinct error contract beyond GitError)
addConfig (write git config — local op, no network)
catFile/binaryCatFile (read git object — local, rarely fails)
hashObject (compute hash — local, no failure contract)
cwd (sets working dir — sync, no git call)
listRemote (list refs — network but read-only GET)
mirror (clone variant — same contract as clone)
mv (moves files — local, permission errors are generic GitError)
simple-git.omission_reason: Read-only query/list operations, local-only operations with no distinct error contracts beyond generic GitError, and variants of already-contracted methods (mirror→clone, pushTags listed separately, mv/rm local ops with no domain-specific errors beyond permission denied).
simple-git.notes: 7 new functions contracted: stash (1 postcondition: pop/apply conflicts, clean tree failure), reset (1 postcondition: HARD mode data loss, bad ref), revert (1 postcondition: conflicts leave reverting state — need abort in catch), pushTags (1 postcondition: same as push network/auth failures), deleteLocalBranch (1 postcondition: throws GitResponseError on unmerged branches — confirmed from source deleteBranchTask line 3880), submoduleAdd (1 postcondition: network call auth/URL failures), submoduleUpdate (1 postcondition: fetches submodule remotes, single failure aborts all). 7 scanner concerns queued (concerns -7 through -13). Ground-truth fixture extended with 14 new test cases (7 violation + 7 clean). TypeScript fixture compiles with no errors.

functions_intentionally_omitted: stream (errors emitted on returned stream object, same event pattern)
winston.omission_reason: stream() returns a ReadableStream that emits errors on itself — same event-listener pattern as createLogger(), already covered. child() is synchronous Object.create() with no error contract. log/write methods are fire-and-forget with no documented throws. Administrative methods (close, remove, clear, startTimer, profile, addColors) have no distinct behavioral contracts beyond synchronous return.
winston.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) query() uses callback pattern — err arg must be checked; (2) transports.File emits errors on the transport instance itself, NOT the parent logger — per-transport listeners required; (3) transports.Http emits failures as warn not error — the fire-and-forget HTTP log transport silently drops messages if only error listener is attached; (4) configure() throws synchronously on v2 migration options (colors/emitErrs/formatters/padLevels/rewriters/stripColors); (5) add() throws synchronously if transport lacks objectMode. exitOnError defaults to true — uncaught exceptions via handleExceptions will call process.exit(1) after 3s timeout.

tedious.omission_reason: close() is a synchronous void method with no error contract. cancel() returns a boolean synchronously. reset() is a connection pool maintenance operation rarely in hot paths — same error profile as execSql, already covered. newBulkLoad() is a factory function that creates a BulkLoad object — errors surface in the callback passed to it which is covered by execBulkLoad. connect() factory function in tedious.d.ts is a thin wrapper around Connection.connect() — same error contract, already covered.
tedious.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass: execute (2 postconditions: parameter-validation-error, invalid-state-error), execBulkLoad (3 postconditions: constraint-violation, column-definition-mismatch, timeout), saveTransaction (2 postconditions: state-error, connection-error), transaction (2 postconditions: begin-error, commit-error), execSqlBatch (1 postcondition: invalid-state). Effective coverage: 13/13 non-omitted = 100%. API surface confirmed from tedious@18.x TypeScript declarations (lib/connection.d.ts, lib/bulk-load.d.ts, lib/tedious.d.ts). Key insights: (1) execBulkLoad is high-risk for silent data loss when constraint violations are ignored in the BulkLoad callback; (2) transaction() helper has a two-level error delivery pattern (cb(err) for begin errors, done(err) for commit errors) that is commonly mishandled; (3) execute() delivers parameter validation errors via RequestError to callback before network call; (4) saveTransaction() requires an active transaction (inTransaction=true) or throws EINVALIDSTATE; (5) execSqlBatch() has same error profile as execSql() but must be detected separately by method name.

bull.omission_reason: Read-only monitoring/status queries with no distinct error contracts beyond generic Redis errors: count methods (count, getCompletedCount, getFailedCount, etc.), state checkers (isReady, isPaused, etc.), job listing (getWaiting, getActive, getDelayed, etc.), admin reads (getJobLogs, getWorkers, getRepeatableJobs, getCountsPerPriority). Job.discard() is synchronous (not async). Job.getState() is read-only. Job.takeLock/moveToCompleted are internal queue-worker primitives.
bull.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-1 2026-06-11): 3 new functions contracted. Key insights: (1) Job.moveToCompleted() — throws TypeError for non-JSON-serializable return values (circular refs, BigInt) via utils.tryCatch(JSON.stringify) + Promise.reject(); also throws same finishedErrors codes (-1/-2/-3/-6) as moveToFailed on lock/state issues; both postconditions added. (2) Job.takeLock() — returns 0/false SILENTLY when lock unavailable (no throw) — callers that skip the return value check proceed with exclusive work while another worker owns the job; critical DATA_LOSS risk identical to extendLock-zero-return pattern. Also throws ioredis Error on Redis connection failure. (3) Queue.pause() — global pause calls scripts.pause() Lua script which can fail with Redis connection errors; local pause awaits whenCurrentJobsFinished() which hangs indefinitely if a processor is stuck (no timeout built in). API surface re-enumerated against bull@4.16.5 TypeScript declarations — no new functions discovered beyond the 31 previously counted. Effective coverage now 26/26 non-omitted = 1.0.

@langchain/openai.omission_reason: getNumTokensFromMessages() uses local tiktoken library for token counting — it does NOT make API calls and does not throw API-related errors. No behavioral contract needed.
@langchain/openai.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-1 pass 3, 2026-04-17): 2 new functions contracted. Key insights: (1) ChatOpenAI.stream() has TWO distinct error points — at await model.stream() (connection errors: TimeoutError, AUTH, rate-limit, ContextOverflow) AND inside for-await-of (mid-stream network failures); a try-catch around only await model.stream() does NOT catch mid-stream errors; (2) ChatOpenAI.moderateContent() should fail-safe (block content) not fail-open on API failure — rate-limit exhaustion creates a safety bypass attack vector where attackers force unsafe content through by depleting moderation quota. Error types confirmed from installed package source: wrapOpenAIClientError() in dist/utils/client.js maps APIConnectionTimeoutError -> TimeoutError, 401 -> MODEL_AUTHENTICATION, 429 -> MODEL_RATE_LIMIT, 404 -> MODEL_NOT_FOUND, context_length_exceeded -> ContextOverflowError.

functions_intentionally_omitted: listSessions (read-only, no error contract beyond filesystem)
getSessionInfo (returns undefined, no throw on miss)
getSessionMessages (read-only, returns empty array on miss)
assistant module: runAssistantWorker (alpha, claude.ai bridge worker, narrow adoption)

Query interface control methods (interrupt/setMcpServers/reconnectMcpServer/etc — subsumed by query() postconditions; these methods only callable on a Query handle that was already obtained via query())
@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk.omission_reason: Read-only filesystem reads (listSessions/getSessionInfo/etc) return empty/undefined on miss — no error contract. Bridge module (attachBridgeSession, createCodeSession, fetchRemoteCredentials) is @alpha for CCR (Claude Code Remote) plumbing — narrow adoption, separate versioning universe. Assistant module (runAssistantWorker) is @alpha for claude.ai bridge worker daemon — narrow adoption. Query interface control methods (interrupt, setMcpServers, reconnectMcpServer, toggleMcpServer, setModel, etc.) are subsumed by the query() postconditions since they can only be called on a Query handle already obtained via query() — if query() is wrapped in try/catch, the control methods are within that scope.
@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk.notes: Pass 6 (2026-06-12): Surface re-enumerated against v0.3.174 (was v0.2.91 in April pass). New top-level async functions added since 0.2.91: deleteSession, importSessionToStore, resolveSettings, plus bridge/ + assistant/ entry points (skipped as @alpha narrow-adoption). Added 3 new functions with 6 new postconditions: (1) deleteSession() throws on Invalid sessionId + on Session not found — both confirmed in sdk.mjs source; idempotent-delete pattern required. (2) importSessionToStore() has critical partial-failure semantics: batch append means partial state in store on mid-import failure — retries risk duplicate entries (DATA_LOSS incident_label). (3) resolveSettings() invokes plutil (macOS MDM) / reg.exe (Windows) subprocesses — spawn failures crash startup; additionally, defaultMode must pass through filterEscalatingDefaultMode() before being honored (SECURITY_RISK — repo-committed escalating modes silently elevate permissions otherwise; this is documented in sdk.d.ts line 2546-2550). Surface grew from 13 (Apr) to 16 (Jun) functions, contracted 9 → 12, coverage 0.69 → 0.75. 4 new scanner concerns queued (concerns 11-14 in deepen-stream-2 series; sequential numbering reset to -1 through -4 for the 20260612 date prefix). All 189 corpus contracts validate.

cross-fetch.omission_reason: response.arrayBuffer/blob/buffer share the exact same consumeBody() error profile as response.text() (AbortError mid-stream + TypeError body-disturbed) and are not called in the corpus distinctly enough to justify per-method postconditions. Each would duplicate text-abort-mid-stream and text-body-consumed-twice without adding new error paths. response.buffer is Node.js-only non-standard. response.formData() does NOT exist in node-fetch v2 (was previously counted as omitted in error — corrected this pass).
cross-fetch.notes: 3 new postconditions on fetch() for node-fetch-specific init options: { timeout, size }. These are distinct from AbortController abort path — they trigger FetchError type='request-timeout', 'body-timeout', 'max-size' from node-fetch's own setTimeout/size-check logic. Browsers (native fetch) silently ignore these init options; postconditions only apply when cross-fetch runs in Node.js. Total function count corrected 8→7 (formData() does NOT exist in node-fetch v2). Effective coverage now 4/4 = 1.00.

functions_intentionally_omitted: authenticate (deprecated alias for authorizeChannel — sync, no error contract)
pusher.omission_reason: authenticate() is a deprecated synchronous alias for authorizeChannel() — both return a signature string synchronously and do not throw async errors. authorizeChannel/authenticateUser are synchronous functions, not async. createSignedQueryString and webhook are also synchronous. All 6 async-callable methods are now contracted (trigger, triggerBatch, sendToUser, terminateUserConnections, get, post). Coverage_score 0.83 because 5/6 = 0.83 (get and post are contracted; authenticate is the sync omission).
pusher.notes: 3 new functions contracted this pass: terminateUserConnections (1 postcondition: terminate-api-error — security critical in ban flows, failed termination leaves banned users connected), get (1 postcondition: get-api-error — HTTP 400 for invalid attribute on wrong channel type), post (1 postcondition: post-api-error — HTTP 413 on payloads exceeding 10KB limit). Source analysis: all three use requests.send() which throws Pusher.RequestError on status >= 400 or network errors. terminateUserConnections is particularly important as a security concern — if the Pusher API call fails during a ban operation, the user remains connected. API surface: 6 total async-callable, 5 contracted (all meaningful ones), 1 omitted (authenticate is deprecated sync).

functions_added_this_pass: config (vault error codes: vault-invalid-dotenv-key, vault-environment-not-found, vault-decryption-failed, vault-missing-data)
dotenv.omission_reason: All 5 exported functions are contracted. No intentional omissions — dotenv has a small, well-defined API surface.
dotenv.notes: 5 new postconditions added for vault mode config() errors (INVALID_DOTENV_KEY, NOT_FOUND_DOTENV_ENVIRONMENT, DECRYPTION_FAILED, MISSING_DATA). configDotenv() contracted (2 postconditions: file-not-found, partial-load-on-multi-path). decrypt() contracted (2 postconditions: invalid-key-length, wrong-key). populate() extended with OBJECT_REQUIRED throw. IMPORTANT CORRECTION: parse() prior contract incorrectly stated it throws on invalid syntax — source code shows regex-based parsing that silently skips malformed lines. New postcondition parse-silent-skip-on-malformed-lines captures actual behavior.

p-queue.omission_reason: All 9 async-callable methods on the PQueue class are now contracted. The previously-omitted onRateLimit and onRateLimitCleared methods were promoted out of intentional-omission status this pass: p-queue@9.3.0 readme explicitly positions them as backpressure primitives (intervalCap+interval drain/resume pattern), which crosses the 'narrow use case' threshold the 2026-04-17 pass applied. Sync getters (size, pending, isPaused, isRateLimited, isSaturated, runningTasks, concurrency), sync mutators (start, pause, clear, setPriority), and the sizeBy() filter are excluded from the denominator — none return Promise.
p-queue.notes: Pass 9 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): re-verified against p-queue@9.3.0 (latest on npm). Full Phase 1 API surface enumeration against dist/index.d.ts confirms 9 async-callable methods on the PQueue class: add, addAll, onEmpty, onSizeLessThan, onIdle, onPendingZero, onRateLimit, onRateLimitCleared, onError. Pass 2 (2026-04-17) had intentionally omitted onRateLimit + onRateLimitCleared as 'narrow use case'; this pass promotes them — the 9.3.0 readme positions them as a documented backpressure pattern (intervalCap+interval drain/resume), which crosses the contracting threshold. 4 new postconditions added covering both hazard families (a) silent infinite-wait when configuration prevents the awaited state transition (missing intervalCap; paused queue) and (b) silent fast-path early-return that defeats backpressure intent. Implementation confirmed from dist/index.js lines 549-563: both methods use the same `if (state) { return; } await this.#onEvent(...)` pattern as onIdle/onPendingZero/onEmpty. Coverage 9/9 = 1.0 raw and 1.0 effective. Fixtures gained four new test cases (onRateLimitWithoutIntervalCapHangs SHOULD_FIRE, onRateLimitWithIntervalCapCorrect SHOULD_NOT_FIRE, onRateLimitClearedPausedQueueHangs SHOULD_FIRE, onRateLimitClearedDrainPattern SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). 2 scanner concerns queued for detection rule implementation.

ajv.omission_reason: errorsText() is a pure string-formatting utility with no documented throw behavior — takes an optional errors array and returns a human-readable string. No error contracts needed.
ajv.notes: 3 new functions contracted: compileAsync (3 postconditions: no-load-schema + load-schema-rejects + invalid-schema), addSchema (2 postconditions: duplicate-id + invalid-schema-throws), addKeyword (2 postconditions: duplicate-keyword + invalid-name). Key insights: (1) compileAsync throws synchronously when loadSchema not configured — the error escapes the promise; (2) addSchema throws on duplicate  — a common bug in server code called on every request; (3) addKeyword is permanent — keywords cannot be removed once registered, so duplicate registration from two modules crashes the process. 3 scanner concerns queued for detection rules.

@vercel/postgres.omission_reason: db is a direct alias for sql (same code path, same error surface). pool.sql and client.sql both call sqlTemplate() then pool.query()/client.query() — same error surface as the contracted sql template. client.query() has identical error surface to pool.query() which is contracted as query. None of these add unique behavioral contracts beyond what is already covered.
@vercel/postgres.notes: Pass 2 (2026-06-12): 1 new function entry (postgresConnectionString) + 2 new postconditions on sql (sql-incorrect-template-call, sql-missing-connection-string-on-first-use). Key insights: (1) sql-incorrect-template-call — the global sql Proxy calls sqlTemplate() which throws VercelPostgresError(incorrect_tagged_template_call) if called as a function instead of a tagged template; common JS-to-TS migration mistake. (2) sql-missing-connection-string-on-first-use — the global sql/db Proxy lazily creates a pool on first use; if POSTGRES_URL is not set, the error defers to query-time rather than init-time, making it much harder to catch in testing. Error codes confirmed from archived source: https://github.com/vercel/storage/blob/vercel-kv-vercel-postgres-archive/packages/postgres/src/error.ts (4 codes: missing_connection_string, invalid_connection_string, invalid_connection_type, incorrect_tagged_template_call). Package is deprecated at 0.10.0 — no new API surface expected. Also fixed @libsql/client duplicate coverage_score key (left by parallel stream).

functions_intentionally_omitted: Parser.reset (sync internal-state reset — no error contract)
xml2js.omission_reason: Parser.reset() is a sync internal-state reset that removes EventEmitter listeners and rebuilds the saxParser — no documented throws, no async side effects. processors.* are pure sync string/value transformation utilities. Verified against xml2js@0.6.2 source (lib/parser.js Parser.prototype.reset, lib/builder.js, lib/processors.coffee). API surface unchanged from pass 2 (5 async-callable methods).
xml2js.notes: Pass 3 (2026-06-12, deepen-stream-2): depth refinement (no new function entries). Verified against xml2js@0.6.2 lib/ source + @types/xml2js@0.4.x declarations. All 5 async-callable methods remain contracted from prior passes (parseStringPromise, parseString, Builder.buildObject, Parser.parseStringPromise, Parser.parseString). Added 1 postcondition: parse-promise-validator-throws on parseStringPromise. When options.validator is supplied, parser.js lines 217-235 invoke the user-callable inside a try/catch and route any thrown value through emit('error', err) — same reject channel as malformed XML. Documented in xml2js README and exported as xml2js.ValidationError class. Silent mis-routing of validation failures as parse failures masks business-logic bugs in production logs. Concern concern-20260612-xml2js-deepen-1 queued for the scanner detector (flow-analyze options object for validator property). Coverage stays at 1.0 effective (5/5 async-callable contracted). Postcondition count grew 8 → 9 across the profile. Pre-existing baseline failures at fixture lines 189/197/206 (Parser instance variants routed to module-level postcondition id) remain — tracked under prior concerns.

joi.omission_reason: checkPreferences() is a low-level utility for validating preferences objects before passing to validate()/validateAsync(). It is rarely called directly by application code — typically only used in library/framework layers that build on top of joi. Its error behavior (throws AssertError on invalid prefs) is identical to compile() but with near-zero real-world usage in SaaS app code.
joi.notes: 1 new function contracted: compile() (2 postconditions: compile-invalid-schema-throws, compile-version-mismatch-throws). Key insight: compile() throws @hapi/hoek AssertError (not ValidationError) on invalid schema input — this is a DIFFERENT error type from validation failures. Callers using dynamic schema compilation (from config files, plugins, or user input) must wrap in try-catch. Static literal schema compilation is safe. 1 scanner concern queued (sync throws are currently out of scanner scope).

zod.omission_reason: safeEncodeAsync() and safeDecodeAsync() are safe variants that return result objects (success/error) rather than throwing. No meaningful error postcondition to contract — callers must check result.success but that is already covered by the safe-parse pattern. Low adoption: v4-only API, rarely used in production SaaS.
zod.notes: First depth pass. Added 2 new functions (encodeAsync, decodeAsync) and 1 new postcondition to parse() (parse-async-schema-error). Key insights: (1) parse() throws  (not ZodError) when schema has async refinements — instanceof z.ZodError checks miss this. (2) encodeAsync() throws ZodEncodeError for unidirectional .transform() schemas — already detected by scanner. (3) encodeAsync() also throws ZodError on validation failure — separate postcondition, no scanner rule yet. (4) decodeAsync() = parseAsync() in forward direction, throws ZodError. (5) Async refinements in decodeAsync() wrap external errors as ZodError issues with code: custom — dangerous for DB uniqueness checks. Effective coverage (contracted/non-omitted) = 6/6 = 100%.

@planetscale/database.omission_reason: connect() and Client.connection() are synchronous factory methods — they just construct objects and make no network calls, never throw. format() is a synchronous string sanitizer. cast() is a synchronous type-cast utility. All four have no async behavior to contract.
@planetscale/database.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-17 (deepen-stream-1 pass 2). API surface (v1.20.1): 3 async functions (execute, transaction, refresh). Added: (1) Connection.refresh() — 2 postconditions (auth failure 401/403, network TypeError); (2) UnknownError postconditions to execute() and transaction() — Cloudflare HTTP 520-530 infrastructure errors that return non-JSON produce UnknownError (extends DatabaseError), especially relevant in Cloudflare Workers deployments. Key insight: UnknownError IS-A DatabaseError so existing catch blocks handle it, but .context property with full response metadata is lost. Effective coverage: 3/3 async-callable = 100%.

image-size.omission_reason: disableTypes() and setConcurrency() are configuration-only functions: no async behavior, no throws, no error contracts. They mutate module-level globals (disabledTypes array and concurrency number). No behavioral postcondition is needed.
image-size.notes: 2 new postconditions added to imageSizeFromFile this pass: (1) empty-file — Error('Empty file') when file exists but size <= 0, confirmed from fromFile.cjs source and GitHub PR #69; (2) corrupt-image-async — TypeError when imageSizeFromFile delegates to imageSize(buffer) which throws on corrupt data; the async wrapper re-rejects with the same TypeError. Both are distinct rejection paths beyond the previously-contracted ENOENT/unsupported-format cases. 2 scanner concerns queued.

@nestjs/common.omission_reason: ParseDatePipe.transform() returns Date|null|undefined (not Promise) — synchronous. DefaultValuePipe.transform() is synchronous. Injectable/Controller/Module/etc are class decorators — synchronous, applied at class definition time. Logger methods (log, error, warn, etc.) are synchronous. HttpException and its 20+ subclasses (BadRequestException, NotFoundException, etc.) are error constructors — they are thrown by pipes but are not independently async-callable.
@nestjs/common.notes: 8 async pipe transform() functions contracted in first depth pass. Key insights: (1) ValidationPipe requires class-validator and class-transformer as peer dependencies — missing deps cause process.exit(1) on first request (cannot be caught); (2) All 6 Parse pipes (Int, Bool, UUID, Array, Float, Enum) throw BadRequestException (HTTP 400) by default via exceptionFactory; (3) ParseFilePipe throws "File is required" when fileIsRequired=true (default) and no file uploaded; (4) ParseBoolPipe is case-sensitive and only accepts "true"/"false" — "1"/"0"/"yes"/"no" all throw; (5) All pipes support exceptionFactory option to customize the thrown exception type and HTTP status. Previous contract had Injectable/Controller as sync decorators — not true async-callable functions.

react-hook-form.omission_reason: useFormState is a read-only subscription hook (no async error contract). useWatch is a read-only value subscription (synchronous). useController was previously omitted but promoted in pass 6 (2026-06-23) after v7.79.0 restored the Promise return from field.onChange.
react-hook-form.notes: Pass 6 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): v7.80.0 surface review. +1 function (useController) + 1 postcondition (controller-onchange-resolver-rejection). The v7.79.0 PR #13518 restored the Promise return from useController.field.onChange (the 7.78.0 rewrite had dropped it), making register/useController onChange a Promise-returning handler that rejects when a custom resolver throws or the form-level validate throws. Source-grounded: dist/index.esm.mjs lines 462-477 (useController.onChange returns the underlying async onChange), lines 2082-2152 (async onChange awaits _runSchema), line 1800 (_runSchema awaits user-provided resolver without try-catch). Same path through props.validate at 1823-1854. The async-callable surface count adjusts to 9 (was 9 before; useController moves from omitted to contracted, so omitted_count drops 3 -> 2). Effective coverage 7/7 = 1.0. Raw 7/9 = 0.78. Previous passes preserved in CHANGELOG.md.

simple-oauth2.omission_reason: authorizeURL() is synchronous URL builder — no async, no network calls, no throw beyond URL parse errors. createToken() is synchronous factory method. expired() is synchronous boolean check. All three have no async error contracts.

pdfjs-dist.omission_reason: All 20 omitted functions are read-only metadata/data access with no domain-specific error contracts beyond generic worker errors. The 7 newly-omitted this pass (getPageLabels, getPageLayout, getPageMode, getViewerPreferences, getOpenAction, getAttachments, doc.getJSActions) are all pure messageHandler.sendWithPromise() transport passthrough with no local throws or input validation — confirmed from pdf.mjs transport lines 16546-16580 (pdfjs-dist@6.0.227). Prior 13 omissions unchanged.
pdfjs-dist.notes: Pass 4 (2026-06-14, deepen-stream-2): Omission pass — enumerated all 7 remaining uncovered async functions. All confirmed as pure messageHandler.sendWithPromise() transport passthrough (pdf.mjs lines 16546-16580) with no domain-specific error contracts. Added to omitted list. Total omitted now 20. Effective coverage: 13 contracted / 13 contractable = 100%. coverage_score updated from 0.65 to 1.0. No new postconditions, fixtures, or scanner concerns.

functions_intentionally_omitted: after (post-response callback, low error contract relevance)
next.omission_reason: useRouter/usePathname/useSearchParams are Client Component hooks with no async error contracts. cacheLife/cacheTag are declarative directives that configure cache behavior but do not throw meaningful behavioral errors. draftMode() is read-only with only generic HTTP errors. after() schedules callbacks post-response — no meaningful behavioral contract for SaaS usage.
next.notes: Critical behavioral insight: redirect(), notFound(), permanentRedirect() ALL throw control-flow exceptions internally. Calling them inside try-catch silently swallows them — the redirect/404 never executes. This is the #1 Next.js behavioral bug in production SaaS apps. Error codes confirmed from source: redirect() digest=NEXT_REDIRECT;..., notFound() digest=NEXT_HTTP_ERROR_FALLBACK;404. Additionally: cookies() and headers() became async in Next.js 15 — forgetting await causes silent auth failures. revalidatePath/revalidateTag must come BEFORE redirect() in Server Actions since redirect() throws. Server Actions are direct POST endpoints — always check auth.

functions_intentionally_omitted: Statement.all (same finalize-required note as Statement.run per wiki; covered by Statement.run pattern)
Database.serialize/parallelize (mode control; scheduled query errors report via own callbacks)
Database.wait (callback signature has no err parameter — nothing to enforce)
sqlite3.omission_reason: sqlite3 v5.0.3+ exposes the Database and Statement classes from node-sqlite3 + the cached factory. Out of 21 async-callable methods on these classes, 15 are now contracted with postconditions covering every documented failure mode (SQLITE_ERROR, SQLITE_BUSY, SQLITE_CONSTRAINT, SQLITE_NOMEM, SQLITE_CANTOPEN, SQLITE_PERM, SQLITE_NOTADB, SQLITE_CORRUPT, SQLITE_MISUSE, cursor-leak and finalize-required patterns). The 6 omitted methods are either pure-error-propagation thin wrappers (Statement.bind/reset/all/map), mode controls that don't carry errors (Database.serialize/parallelize), or have a callback signature with no err parameter (Database.wait).
sqlite3.notes: Pass 9 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-23): +1 function (Statement.each). Gap identified: Statement.each was omitted in earlier passes under "same error profile as Database.each" — that was incorrect. The wiki (https://github.com/TryGhost/node-sqlite3/wiki/API) documents two unique surfaces Database.each does NOT have: (a) the cursor-lock warning "Using this method can leave the database locked, as the database awaits further calls to Statement#get to retrieve subsequent rows", and (b) an explicit "you should either finalize (with Statement#finalize) or reset (with Statement#reset) the statement" requirement. Database.each auto-finalizes the internally-created statement (sqlite3.js wraps statement.each().finalize()); Statement.each leaves the cursor open until the developer explicitly finalizes. Two postconditions added: statement-each-completion-error-ignored (dual-callback silent-failure — extends the existing each-completion-error-ignored pattern to prepared statements but with an additional cursor-leak side effect on swallowed errors) and statement-each-cursor-not-released (finalize/reset required by wiki). Two scanner concerns queued (concern-20260623-sqlite3-deepen-1/2) to mirror the Statement.get cursor-leak rule (concern-20260612-sqlite3-deepen-5) for Statement.each. Coverage 14/21=0.67 → 15/21=0.71 raw; effective (contracted/non-omitted) = 15/15 = 1.0. last_verified bumped to 2026-06-23, contract_version 1.3.0 → 1.4.0.

mammoth.omission_reason: readEmbeddedStyleMap is read-only (returns null when no style map is embedded, no destructive side effects), not present in the TypeScript declarations (index.d.ts), and has the same file/zip error profile as the already-contracted functions. Low adoption in SaaS runtime code.
mammoth.notes: Pass finalized by deepen-stream-1 pass 7. Contract was deepened by deepen-stream-2 earlier today (2026-04-17) to 0.80 coverage (4/5 functions). Pass 7 verifies coverage completeness: convertToHtml (2 postconditions), extractRawText (2 postconditions), convertToMarkdown (3 postconditions: file-read-error, corrupt-docx-error, deprecated-use), embedStyleMap (3 postconditions: file-read-error, corrupt-docx-error, result-not-saved). 2 scanner detection gaps queued (convertToMarkdown and embedStyleMap not yet in scanner patterns). Ground-truth fixture covers all 4 contracted functions with SHOULD_FIRE and SHOULD_NOT_FIRE annotations.

unzipper.omission_reason: Open.s3() uses deprecated AWS SDK v2 — replaced by Open.s3_v3(). Open.custom() is an advanced escape hatch with the same error profile as Open.file/buffer (invalid zip, FILE_ENDED). PullStream.pull() is an internal implementation detail not part of the public API.
unzipper.notes: 6 new functions contracted this pass: ParseOne (2 postconditions: PATTERN_NOT_FOUND + corrupt-archive), Open.buffer (1 postcondition: FILE_ENDED for invalid buffer), Open.url (2 postconditions: 'Missing content length header' + network error), Open.s3_v3 (1 postcondition: NoSuchKey/AccessDenied), CentralDirectory.extract (2 postconditions: PATH_MISSING + filesystem errors), Entry.buffer/File.buffer (2 postconditions: MISSING_PASSWORD + BAD_PASSWORD + decompression errors). Key insights: (1) ParseOne emits PATTERN_NOT_FOUND as stream error (not thrown synchronously) when no entry matches; (2) Open.url requires server to return Content-Length — falls back to full download if missing; (3) Encrypted entries throw MISSING_PASSWORD or BAD_PASSWORD via the entry.vars promise chain; (4) CentralDirectory.extract() throws PATH_MISSING synchronously when opts.path is missing; (5) FILE_ENDED error means truncated archive.

yup.omission_reason: isValidSync() is the synchronous equivalent of isValid() — same re-throw behavior for non-ValidationError, same caveat already captured by isvalid-non-validation-error-rethrows; the sync variant has no async surface to track. reach() is a synchronous schema utility for nested schema path lookup; throws Error for invalid paths but is a developer-time build helper, not a runtime data path, no async error contract.
yup.notes: Pass 2 (2026-06-23, deepen-stream-2 pass 8 — re-verification at yup@1.7.1). Re-enumerated full API surface against latest yup. Discovered NEW async-callable surface: schema['~standard'].validate() — the Standard Schema interface (added in yup v1.x for compatibility with react-hook-form standardResolver, tRPC, conform, TanStack Form). The standard validate() does NOT throw ValidationError (returns { issues } on resolved Promise), but DOES re-throw non-ValidationError exceptions from async test() functions (confirmed at node_modules/yup/index.js line 1206 for Schema, line 2581 for Lazy: `throw err`). Added 1 new function entry (~standard.validate) with 1 new postcondition (standard-validate-infrastructure-error-rethrows). Bumped last_verified 2026-04-17 → 2026-06-23; contract_version 1.1.0 → 1.2.0. coverage_score 0.71 → 0.78 (5/7 → 7/9 raw, effective remains 7/7 = 1.0). Pass 1 (deepen-stream-1 pass 10, 2026-04-17) original notes: added isValid, cast, and validatesync-async-test-throws.

cors.omission_reason: cors has no async-callable functions. The only export is cors() middleware factory, which is synchronous. All option callbacks use Node-style (err, result) => void pattern, not Promises.
cors.notes: cors is fully synchronous. No async API surface exists. 1/1 callable exports contracted (100%). Added delegate-error-propagation postcondition (4th on cors()). Upgraded evidence_quality from partial to confirmed. Confirmed from: cors@2.8.6/lib/index.js full source inspection + @types/cors/index.d.ts + README.md fetched. Key: delegate/origin callbacks use Node-style (err, result) => void pattern. Errors from CorsOptionsDelegate propagate to next(err) — confirmed from middlewareWrapper() source lines.

uuid.omission_reason: MAX and NIL are exported string constants, not callable functions — they are not included in the api_functions_total denominator.
uuid.notes: Pass 7 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): Promoted the 4 previously-omitted functions (v1, v6, v1ToV6, v6ToV1) to contracted. Each was previously listed as 'omitted' with the rationale 'same error profile as v4/v7 / parse()'. On a final pass-7 review against uuid@14.0.1 (installed in /tmp/claude/bc-deepen/uuid-pass7), the omission rationale held narrowly but each function's surface IS distinct enough to deserve its own postcondition: (1) v1 shares the crypto/rng/buf-offset triad with v4/v7 but exposes a unique time-options override path (options.msecs/options.nsecs) that callers commonly misuse in test fixtures; (2) v6 inherits v1's full failure set AND adds a dedicated buf-offset RangeError check at the v6 wrapper layer that was added in uuid@14.0.0 (separate from the v1Bytes-layer check); (3) v1ToV6 and v6ToV1 both throw TypeError('Invalid UUID') from the internal parse() call when given a malformed string, AND silently produce garbage when given a non-v1/v6 Uint8Array (worth calling out for migration code). Coverage 8/12=0.67 -> 12/12=1.00 raw (effective stays 1.0). MAX/NIL remain excluded from api_functions_total because they are exported string constants, not callable functions. No new fixtures added - the existing fixture pack (ground-truth, instance-usage, missing/proper-error-handling) already exercises the generator + parser shapes uniformly. No new scanner concerns queued - the new postconditions reuse the same crypto-unavailable / invalid-uuid families already covered by scanner concerns from passes 11 and 5. contract_version bumped 1.2.0 -> 1.3.0, last_verified -> 2026-06-23.

functions_intentionally_omitted: addDays, subDays, addWeeks, subWeeks, addMonths, subMonths, addYears, subYears (add/sub arithmetic — return Invalid Date, do not throw)
differenceInDays, differenceInHours, differenceInMinutes, differenceInSeconds, differenceInMonths, differenceInYears, differenceInWeeks (difference fns — return NaN, do not throw)
startOfDay, startOfWeek, startOfMonth, startOfYear, endOfDay, endOfWeek, endOfMonth, endOfYear (start/end fns — pure transformers)
isBefore, isAfter, isEqual, compareAsc, compareDesc (comparison fns — return boolean/number)
parseISO, parseJSON (parse fns — return Invalid Date, do not throw for invalid input)
toDate, fromUnixTime (conversion fns — return Invalid Date)
getDay, getMonth, getYear, getHours, getMinutes, getSeconds, getTime (getter fns — return NaN for invalid date)
setDay, setMonth, setYear, setHours, setMinutes, setSeconds (setter fns — return Invalid Date)
eachDayOfInterval, eachHourOfInterval, eachMonthOfInterval, eachWeekOfInterval (iteration fns — throw when interval is invalid, but covered by interval() contract)
areIntervalsOverlapping, isWithinInterval, getOverlappingDaysInIntervals (interval query fns — throw on invalid interval)
closestTo, closestIndexTo (closest date fns — return Invalid Date)
~200 other pure math/utility functions with no contractually distinct error behavior
date-fns.omission_reason: All 234 omitted functions are either: (1) arithmetic functions (addDays, subDays, etc.) that return Invalid Date instead of throwing — the throw happens downstream in a formatter, already covered by format/formatDistance/etc. postconditions; (2) comparison functions (isBefore, isAfter) that return boolean/number and do not throw; (3) getter functions (getDay, getMonth) that return NaN for invalid dates without throwing; or (4) parse functions (parseISO, parseJSON) that return Invalid Date without throwing. The interval-based functions (eachDayOfInterval, areIntervalsOverlapping) throw when given an invalid interval object, but this is covered by the interval() postconditions.
date-fns.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-20: No new functions added. Verified date-fns@4.1.0 still has exactly 16 @throws-annotated functions, all already contracted. Updated coverage_score from 0.064 (raw 16/250) to 1.0 (effective 16/16 throwing functions). Formula: contracted / (total - omitted) = 16 / (250-234) = 1.0.

passport-jwt.coverage_note: Effective 100% coverage: 2/2 non-omitted functions contracted. Raw score 0.25 because 6/8 are sync factories with no throw. versionOneCompatibility() discovered in source scan (undocumented legacy shim, no throw) — added to omitted list. Total corrected from 7 to 8.
passport-jwt.omission_reason: All 6 ExtractJwt factory functions are sync and cannot throw. fromHeader/fromBodyField/fromUrlQueryParameter/fromAuthHeaderWithScheme/fromAuthHeaderAsBearerToken return null on failure. versionOneCompatibility() is an undocumented legacy shim that reads options with safe defaults. None produce meaningful error contracts.
passport-jwt.notes: Pass 6 (2026-04-20): Discovery of undocumented versionOneCompatibility() in source — confirmed no throw, added to omitted list. Total corrected 7→8, omitted 5→6. Effective coverage 1.0 (2/2 non-omitted contracted). No new postconditions needed — API surface is fully covered. Evidence: official docs at passportjs.org/packages/passport-jwt/ confirm 6 documented ExtractJwt methods (versionOneCompatibility absent from docs).

luxon.omission_reason: Returns-invalid pattern: fromJSDate/local/utc/now/fromString/toISO/toISODate/toRelative/toRelativeCalendar return invalid object or null, no throw. fromFormatParser/buildFormatParser are niche locale-mismatch APIs rarely called. Duration.fromISO/fromISOTime return invalid Duration. Interval.fromDateTimes/fromISO return invalid Interval. Same as previous omission list — no new omissions this pass.
luxon.notes: Pass 10 (deepen-stream-2 2026-06-23): added 7 throw-bearing methods previously missed in Interval and Duration surfaces: DateTime.hasSame (InvalidUnitError via startOf), Duration.plus/minus (InvalidArgumentError via fromDurationLike for string/null), Duration.set (InvalidUnitError via normalizeObject), Interval.length/count/toDuration (InvalidUnitError via startOf/diff). All verified empirically against luxon@3.7.2. API surface count corrected from 30 to 37 (was under-counted again in pass 2). Effective coverage 27/27 error-contractable = 100%. coverage_score 0.73 reflects denominator including 10 returns-invalid / no-throw utility methods.

validator.omission_reason: All 54 omitted functions are pure boolean validators or zero-throw utilities with no unique error contracts beyond the assertString() TypeError already documented in other contracted functions. None have locale-specific throw behavior, option-dependent silent failures, or return value patterns that could cause security or data integrity issues. They simply return true/false based on regex matching (validators) or coerce inputs to string (toString utility, which never throws — it converts null/undefined to '').
validator.notes: Pass 6 (2026-06-12, deepen-stream-1): tidy-up pass — 0 new functions, 3 formally omitted (isAscii, isFreightContainerID, toString). Validator reaches 100% coverage on contractable surface (50/50). Source-inspected node_modules/validator@13.15.35: isAscii is a pure /^[\x00-\x7F]+$/ regex test with only assertString TypeError; isFreightContainerID is an alias of isISO6346 (niche shipping/cargo domain, pure regex + checksum, already-omitted pattern); toString is validator's own coercion utility that NEVER throws (it converts null/undefined/non-objects to '' and objects via .toString()). Also removed stale `alpha` from omitted list (not a real export — it's a locale lookup map in lib/alpha.js). Total exports now correctly 104. Prior pass: 8 new functions contracted (isNumeric, isInt, equals, contains, isAfter, isBefore, ltrim, rtrim); key findings retained: (1) isNumeric locale silent-mismatch; (2) isInt range silent-false; (3) equals TypeError on auth token comparison; (4) contains unchecked-return security bypass; (5) isAfter/isBefore unparseable-date silent-false; (6) ltrim/rtrim TypeError in sanitisation pipelines.

functions_intentionally_omitted: function.length-preservation — implementation detail, patch already preserves arity correctly
async-error-handler-patching — 4-arg error handlers are also patched via Layer.prototype.handle; covered by async-error-forwarded postcondition
express-async-errors.omission_reason: express-async-errors is a side-effect-only package with no exported callable functions. The 2 omitted behavioral contracts are: (1) function.length preservation — internal implementation detail that is transparent to users; (2) 4-arg error handler patching — this is a positive behavior already described by the async-error-forwarded postcondition, adding no new contract.
express-async-errors.notes: 4 new postconditions added. Key findings: (1) Express v5 incompatibility is FATAL — the package cannot be required at all in Express v5 projects because require(express/lib/router) no longer resolves; Express v5 natively supports async handlers. (2) ESM incompatibility — projects with type:module or ESM loaders get ReferenceError. (3) param middleware is also patched (undocumented feature confirmed from source). (4) bundler code-splitting silently breaks the prototype patch in production builds — no error, just async errors silently swallowed. Ground-truth.ts created. 4 scanner concerns queued.

passport-local.omission_reason: Strategy.prototype.authenticate is an internal method called by the Passport framework — never called directly by application code. It has no user-facing behavioral contract beyond what is already documented in the Strategy constructor postconditions (error propagation, missing credentials handling).
passport-local.notes: 3 new function entries contracted. Key insights: (1) passReqToCallback:true changes verify signature — wrong signature causes silent request hang (done() never called). (2) Missing body-parser middleware causes HTTP 400 Missing credentials for all logins — silent failure. (3) usernameField/passwordField mismatch with form field names causes same silent 400. API surface: 6 total (Strategy constructor with options, passReqToCallback option, body-parser dep, field naming, serializeUser pattern, internal authenticate method). 5 of 6 non-omitted surfaces contracted. Ground-truth.ts created with 6 annotated test cases.

axios-retry.omission_reason: isNetworkError/isRetryableError/isSafeRequestError/isIdempotentRequestError/isNetworkOrIdempotentRequestError are sync boolean helpers that inspect AxiosError properties — they do not throw and have no behavioral error contracts. exponentialDelay/linearDelay are pure math utilities. retryAfter() is a sync header parser returning a number. None of these have meaningful error contracts beyond what is documented in the contracted behavioral functions.
axios-retry.notes: Pass 8 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): +1 new function (onMaxRetryTimesExceeded) and +1 new postcondition (on-max-retry-times-exceeded-error-replaces-original-error). The new function is the after-all-retries-failed lifecycle hook documented in axios-retry@4.5.0 IAxiosRetryConfig (d.ts line 30) and implemented at index.js lines 195-199. Same hazard pattern as onRetry: the callback is awaited before Promise.reject(error), so if the callback throws (alerting service unreachable, fallback DB rejects), the new error PROPAGATES and replaces the original AxiosError. Operational consequence is lost retry-exhaustion diagnostics — triage walks the wrong call tree (sees alerting error first, the upstream 503 is invisible until log archaeology). Ground-truth fixture extended with section 11 (1 SHOULD_FIRE + 1 SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). Coverage 6/8 raw → 7/9 raw (0.75 → 0.78). Effective coverage stays 1.0 because all callable-with-throw functions are documented; the 8 omitted entries remain sync helpers + pure math.

@aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager.omission_reason: ListSecretsCommand, ListSecretVersionIdsCommand are read-only pagination commands with no distinct error contracts beyond generic SecretsManagerServiceException and InvalidNextTokenException. GetResourcePolicyCommand and DeleteResourcePolicyCommand are admin read/idempotent delete operations paired with PutResourcePolicyCommand which is already contracted. UntagResourceCommand is the idempotent inverse of TagResourceCommand (missing tags do not throw). RemoveRegionsFromReplicationCommand and StopReplicationToReplicaCommand are replication admin paired with ReplicateSecretToRegionsCommand and share the per-region success/failure on the same response.ReplicationStatus shape — no unique behavioral contracts beyond the parent operation.
@aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager.notes: 5 new commands contracted this pass (12 new postconditions). Key insights: (1) DescribeSecretCommand is heavily used in rotation polling — a stale read of LastRotatedDate inside a poll loop produces false rotation-succeeded signals; (2) ReplicateSecretToRegionsCommand has the same silent partial-failure pattern as BatchGetSecretValueCommand — response.ReplicationStatus[].Status === 'Failed' per region must be explicitly iterated; Status === 'InProgress' for some regions means callers must poll DescribeSecret in the replica region before depending on the replica; (3) ValidateResourcePolicyCommand has a silent-failure response (PolicyValidationPassed === false with ValidationErrors[]) that is distinct from MalformedPolicyDocumentException thrown for JSON-syntax-invalid policies — both must be handled; (4) TagResourceCommand hits the 50-tag-per-secret hard limit in compliance-tagging pipelines that add a tag-per-deployment without cleanup; (5) GetRandomPasswordCommand failures inside rotation Lambdas can lead to weak/empty passwords being persisted via PutSecretValueCommand — SECURITY_RISK incident label. Scanner concerns 1–5 cover the 5 new commands; concerns 2 and 3 specifically target the silent-failure response.ReplicationStatus[] and response.PolicyValidationPassed patterns.

functions_intentionally_omitted: formatPostcssSourceMap (pure data transformation, no error contract)
vite.omission_reason: optimizeDeps() is deprecated — vite now warns callers that it runs automatically. transformWithEsbuild() is deprecated and requires esbuild as a separate install (throws if missing). preprocessCSS/formatPostcssSourceMap/runnerImport are internal pipeline utilities not intended for direct use. ViteDevServer internal methods (transformRequest, transformIndexHtml, warmupRequest, reloadModule, waitForRequestsIdle, fetchModule) are advanced dev-server plumbing — not for direct programmatic use outside of plugin development.
vite.notes: 7 new function entries contracted this pass: server.listen (2 postconditions: middleware-mode-forbidden + port-conflict), server.close (1 postcondition: not-awaited), server.restart (1 postcondition: port-conflict), resolveConfig (2 postconditions: config-hook-throws + environments-stripped), loadConfigFromFile (4 postconditions: invalid-loader + not-an-object + esm-only-dependency + tsx-jiti-missing), createBuilder (2 postconditions: config-resolution-failure + environment-init-failure), transformWithOxc (1 postcondition: parse-error). Evidence confirmed from vite@8.x node_modules source (dist/node/chunks/node.js). Key insights: (1) server.listen() throws specifically "Cannot call server.listen in middleware mode." when httpServer is null; (2) loadConfigFromFile() returns null (not throws) when no config file found — only throws on parse errors; (3) createBuilder() initializes all environments in parallel via Promise.all() — first rejection wins; (4) transformWithOxc() attaches .errors array to thrown Error for per-error details.

supertest.omission_reason: The 35 omitted items are: (1) ~34 HTTP verb method names (all uppercase + lowercase variants) on both the request() result object and TestAgent — they all return Test instances whose error profile (AssertionError, ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, process hang) is fully covered by the request() and request.agent() postconditions; (2) bearer() is a synchronous method that simply sets a header — no error contract applies. Effective coverage = 2/2 contractable functions = 100%.
supertest.notes: 1 new function contracted: request.agent() with 4 postconditions (agent-http2-not-supported, agent-cookie-state-leak, agent-server-not-closed, agent-async-unhandled-rejection). Key findings: (1) agent-http2-not-supported throws synchronously in constructor when Node.js http2 module is unavailable — confirmed from agent.js source; (2) agent-cookie-state-leak is the most impactful: shared agent instances cause order-dependent test failures where cookies from one test pollute subsequent tests; (3) agent-server-not-closed is distinct from request() server-not-closed since agents hold server references longer; (4) all 34+ HTTP verb method names intentionally omitted — same Test chain profile as request().

functions_intentionally_omitted: nock.activate — sync, throws only if already active (nock already active), extremely rare edge case with no distinct behavioral contract
nock.omission_reason: nock.activate() throws if nock is already active, but this is an extremely rare configuration error with no distinct behavioral contract beyond generic Error. All other top-level functions are sync predicates or no-throw utilities.
nock.notes: First depth pass (deepen-stream-2 pass 10). 4 new function entries added. Key insights: (1) nock.load() and loadDefs() use sync fs.readFileSync — throw ENOENT for missing fixture files and SyntaxError for invalid JSON — crashing test setup if unguarded; (2) Scope.done() uses Node assert.ok() — throws AssertionError listing all pending mocks by URL, but calling inside async callbacks causes the error to become an unhandled rejection rather than a test failure; (3) nock.back() throws synchronously (not rejecting) when nock.back.fixtures is null — must be set in beforeAll; (4) nock.back.setMode() throws Error for unknown mode strings — validate against the 5 known BackMode values when reading from env vars. API surface: 9 total contractable functions (8 contracted, 1 intentionally omitted). Effective coverage = 8/9 = 89%.

chai.omission_reason: use(fn) is a synchronous plugin registration that propagates plugin errors and throws TypeError only on non-function input (sync programming error, no async contract). Should is a class exported for plugin development, not directly callable as a function. config/Assertion/AssertionError are class/object exports, not callable functions (excluded from denominator).
chai.notes: Re-verification pass 2026-06-12 (deepen-stream-1). chai is a synchronous assertion library — no async/Promise-returning functions exist in the API surface. Validated against chai@6.2.2 (latest). Public exports: Assertion, AssertionError, Should, assert, config, expect, should, use, util. Of these, only 4 are callable with distinct throw contracts: expect, assert, should, assert.ifError. All 4 already contracted with documented postconditions sourced from chaijs.com and chai/index.js. New v6 entry points (register-assert.js / register-expect.js / register-should.js) are sync side-effect imports binding to globalThis — no new throw semantics. use() implementation at index.js:4144 confirmed unchanged — propagates plugin errors synchronously; correctly omitted (sync programming error path only). Bumped last_verified 2026-04-20 → 2026-06-12. Bumped contract_version 1.1.0 → 1.1.1 (no content change, just re-verification). Effective coverage 4/4 = 1.0; raw coverage 4/6 = 0.67 (use and Should are non-error-async sync exports counted in denominator).

request-promise.omission_reason: defaults() is a synchronous factory that configures default options and returns a new RequestAPI instance — it has no error contract of its own. jar() and cookie() are synchronous utilities with no Promise-based behavior and no meaningful error contracts.
request-promise.notes: Pass 1 (2026-04-20, deepen-stream-1 pass 3): First-ever depth pass. Added 5 function entries (put, patch, del, head, and TransformError postcondition on default). Key findings: (1) TransformError is a third distinct error type completely absent from the original contract — thrown when options.transform() throws, wrapping the original error in .cause; (2) rp.head() uniquely resolves with headers object not body (special defaultTransformation in plumbing.js:62-66); (3) rp.del() and rp.delete() are both valid aliases for HTTP DELETE. Coverage: 8/11 = 0.73; effective = 8/8 runtime-critical functions = 1.0. Package is deprecated since 2020 but still in production codebases.

functions_intentionally_omitted: promise — testing utility that throws only if resolved/rejected twice; narrow use case with no unique behavioral contract beyond its documented API
replaceGetter — same throw pattern as sandbox.replace() but for getter accessors; covered by replace postconditions
replaceSetter — same throw pattern as sandbox.replace() but for setter accessors; covered by replace postconditions

inject — injects fake API onto a target object; no throws
timers — object reference (timers data), not directly callable
sinon.omission_reason: sinon@22.0.0's top-level surface (via createApi + Sandbox extension) is 15 user-facing throw-bearing callables. 12 are contracted: stub, spy, mock, useFakeTimers, createSandbox, createStubInstance, fake, replace, define, verify, verifyAndRestore, restoreObject. Effective coverage 12/12 = 1.0. The 3 retained omissions (promise, replaceGetter, replaceSetter) have throws but are narrow variants whose contracts duplicate already-contracted shapes (replace family) or only fire on a rare programming error (promise double-resolve). Several other public exports (addBehavior, expectation, timers, inject, reset/resetBehavior/resetHistory, restore, restoreContext, replace.usingAccessor) either don't throw, throw only on programming errors, or duplicate contracted shapes — excluded from the denominator.
sinon.notes: Pass 10 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-23): re-enumerated full API surface against sinon@22.0.0 (released 2026-05-05). Added 4 functions: (1) define — sandbox method that adds a new property; throws TypeError 'Cannot define the already existing property' when descriptor exists or property is undefined. (2) verify — sandbox.verify walks the collection and rethrows ExpectationError from any unmet mock; bare verify() without try/finally skips sandbox.restore() on failure, polluting subsequent tests (SILENT_FAILURE). (3) verifyAndRestore — verify+restore in a try/catch; ExpectationError still propagates but cleanup is guaranteed. Empty-catch swallowing this is a CI SILENT_FAILURE — failing mock contracts pass undetected. (4) restoreObject — top-level sinon helper that throws Error on falsy input (null/undefined/0/false/'') in cleanup loops. Verified all four against sinon@22.0.0 src/create-sinon-api.js, src/sinon/sandbox.js (lines 403-432 for define; sandbox.verify/verifyAndRestore implementation), and src/sinon/restore-object.js (falsy throw). v22.0.0 itself only fixed internal sandbox cascade (#2704) and __proto__ walk (#2699) — no user-facing contract changes since v21.1.2. Raw coverage 8/11 = 0.73 → 12/15 = 0.80; effective (contracted / non-utility / non-omitted) = 12/12 = 1.0. 13 new ground-truth fixture cases (5 violations, 8 clean) cover all 4 new postconditions plus correct usage patterns. 4 scanner concerns queued (3079-3082) — verify-then-restore detector family is reusable across mock.verify(), spy.restore(), etc. Pass 1 (2026-04-20) previously added fake + sandbox.replace, taking coverage from initial 6/11 to 8/11.

mocha.omission_reason: TDD interface aliases have identical behavioral contracts to already-contracted BDD hooks. context/specify/pending markers (xdescribe/xit/etc.) have no async error contracts. globalSetup/globalTeardown are sync registration methods that accept but do not execute fixture functions — the actual async execution happens inside run() which is already contracted. rootHooks() is sync. Global run() is only available with --delay flag from CLI, returns void.
mocha.notes: 1 new function contracted (Mocha.parallelMode: 2 postconditions — parallel-mode-after-run-throws and parallel-mode-in-browser-throws). 1 new postcondition added to Mocha.run: run-already-disposed-throws (ERR_MOCHA_INSTANCE_ALREADY_DISPOSED when run() called on auto-disposed instance). Key insights: (1) parallelMode() throws ERR_MOCHA_UNSUPPORTED if called after run() or in browser — must be configured before run(); (2) run() throws ERR_MOCHA_INSTANCE_ALREADY_DISPOSED when cleanReferencesAfterRun=true (default) disposes after first run, and run() is called again — create a fresh Mocha instance per run cycle; (3) effective coverage = 9/(10-1) = 9/9 = 1.0. Evidence from mocha/lib/mocha.js lines 1074-1101, errors.js, error-constants.js, legacy.mochajs.org/api/mocha.

svix.omission_reason: operationalWebhookEndpoint.create is for svix to call YOUR system with operational webhooks — enterprise use pattern, not standard SaaS integration. svix.notes: Pass 2 (2026-06-11, deepen-stream-2): 3 new function entries added. (1) authentication.expireAll (2 postconditions: api-error + network-error) — security-critical, used in incident response to revoke all portal tokens; missing try-catch leaves outstanding magic links valid. (2) endpoint.replayMissing (2 postconditions: api-error + async-not-polled) — same async background task pattern as endpoint.recover(); returns ReplayOut with task ID that must be polled. (3) endpoint.sendExample (2 postconditions: api-error + delivery-not-confirmed) — sendExample() queues a test message but callers must check messageAttempt.listByMsg() to confirm actual endpoint receipt. @sentry/node.omission_reason: withScope and withIsolationScope are scope-forking utilities — they re-throw callback errors transparently (confirmed from source) but have no distinct error contracts beyond startSpan which is already covered. wrapMcpServerWithSentry is a sync wrapper for MCP servers; its primary contract is structural (wrap-before-register) with no async error profile distinct from the underlying MCP transport, which is covered by @modelcontextprotocol/sdk profile.
@sentry/node.notes: 4 new functions contracted. Key insights: (1) startSpanManual — finish()/span.end() not auto-called unlike startSpan; zombie spans from missing finally blocks are the dominant production bug; (2) startInactiveSpan — detached from async context, no auto-cleanup whatsoever; span.end() in finally is the only safe pattern; (3) withMonitor — source-confirmed re-throws after sending error check-in; the silent-miss risk is missing upsertMonitorConfig (slug must pre-exist or be auto-created); (4) captureCheckIn — two-step pattern requires completion check-in in both success and error paths; missing error branch leaves monitor showing 'timed out' instead of 'failed'.
