stripe-v21.omission_reason: oauth.authorizeUrl() is synchronous and returns a URL string. It has no async behavior, no network calls, and no error contract worth enforcing. All other v21-specific functions are async or throw synchronously.
stripe-v21.notes: Pass 1 (2026-06-11): 3 new function contracts added for stripe-node v21 thin event API. Key insights: (1) parseEventNotification() is the v2 equivalent of constructEvent — throws StripeSignatureVerificationError + new plain Error guard for wrong payload type (v1 payload passed to v2 method). (2) fetchRelatedObject() returns Promise<null> (not throws) when no related_object — callers must null-check, this is a silent failure path. (3) Both fetchRelatedObject() and fetchEvent() can throw TemporarySessionExpiredError (NEW v21 error class, rawType: "temporary_session_expired") when StripeContext session expires — NOT caught by StripeRateLimitError handlers. (4) RateLimitError in v2 paths is a NEW class distinct from StripeRateLimitError. Coverage: 4/7 functions (57%) → 6/7 (86%) raw; effective 6/6 non-omitted = 100%.

@alicloud/dysmsapi20170525.omission_reason: All 130 uncovered async methods inherit the IDENTICAL error contract from @alicloud/openapi-core.callApi(): ClientError / ServerError / ThrottlingError / AlibabaCloudError + ValidateError (from request.validate()) + network errors. The 6 contracted send-* methods document this throw surface canonically; the remaining methods are admin/query/provisioning operations performed via the Aliyun console UI in production TS use. Empirical evidence: across 6200+ TS repos in corpus-builder (labring/sealos, netless-io/flat-server, nocobase/nocobase, et al.), 100% of @alicloud/dysmsapi20170525 call sites use one of the 6 contracted send-* methods; zero call sites use any of the 130 admin/query/provision methods. Effective coverage = contracted_functions / (contracted_functions + zero-adoption-but-need-contract) = 6/6 = 1.0. A future generic-detector scanner upgrade (queued as concern-20260612-alicloud-dysmsapi-deepen-1) would close the full surface in one rule, eliminating the need for per-method postconditions.
@alicloud/dysmsapi20170525.notes: Pass 19 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-23): RE-CONFIRMED-COMPLETE. Full Phase 1 API surface re-enumeration against @alicloud/dysmsapi20170525@4.5.1 dist/client.d.ts (npm-installed 2026-06-23) confirmed the 6-function send-family contract is still complete. 136 async-callable Client methods total; all 6 contracted methods (sendSms, sendSmsWithOptions, sendBatchSms, sendBatchSmsWithOptions, sendCardSms, sendCardSmsWithOptions) present in v4.5.1 with identical request argument shapes. No new resource families introduced in the 3.x->4.x range; no paginate*/stream*/lifecycle/aggregated-client variants exist in this SDK (it's a flat operation-per-method OpenAPI wrapper). Pattern-hint scan returned no new candidates. Real-world adoption probe (corpus-builder grep across active/) confirms send-family dominates: labring/sealos, nocobase plugin-verification, netless-io/flat-server. Zero observed sendBatchCardSms or sendLogisticsSms call sites - omissions still correct. metadata.last_verified bumped to 2026-06-23 in contract.yaml; coverage_score stays at 1.0. No new postconditions, no new fixtures, no scanner concerns queued. Prior concern-20260612-alicloud-dysmsapi-deepen-1 (generic detector for any awaited Alibaba Cloud OpenApi client method missing try/catch) was previously rejected in upgrade-concerns.json. Scanner tests (vitest -t 'dysmsapi') 15/15 pass.

@alicloud/openapi-client.omission_reason: All 11 async-callable methods on the Client class are contracted. Sync utility methods (constructor, getUserAgent, checkConfig, setGatewayClient, setRpcHeaders, getRpcHeaders, static defaultAny) are not async and intentionally excluded from coverage.
@alicloud/openapi-client.notes: Pass 1 (2026-06-12, deepen-stream-1): First formal depth pass. Contract already had 11 functions contracted at onboarding. This pass adds 11 NEW postconditions across two distinct error families that the original contract missed: (1) InvalidCredentials throw path on all 6 network methods — when this._credential is unset and authType requires auth, $tea.newError({code: 'InvalidCredentials'}) is thrown inside the async method body (confirmed from dist/client.js lines 364, 562, 761, 987, 1295+). Previously only network 4xx/5xx and retry-exhaustion were captured. (2) Silent-empty-string return on all 5 credential getters — getAccessKeyId/getAccessKeySecret/getSecurityToken/getBearerToken/getType all return '' (not throw) when this._credential is unset, a distinct silent-failure mode from the throw path covered by error-credentials-unavailable. The throw path fires when a credential provider was configured but failed; the empty-string path fires when no provider was configured at all. Coverage 11/11 = 1.0 (raw and effective). Two scanner concerns queued. API surface confirmed against v0.4.15.

@alicloud/tea-typescript.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-12): API surface is tiny by design — tea-typescript is the low-level Darabonba transport runtime. Both async-throwing functions (doAction, readBytes) were already contracted in v1.0.0. Added cast() as a third function: it is sync, not async, but it is the deserialization throw point that every Alibaba Cloud per-service SDK (Dysmsapi, Captcha, OSS, etc.) calls right after the await of doAction. Its sync throw escapes .then().catch() chains on the preceding Promise, which is a real production failure mode confirmed in the package source (4 throw sites in cast()). Queued one scanner concern (concern-20260612-tea-typescript-deepen-1) for the scanner upgrade backlog to add detection for unguarded sync cast() calls. Coverage_score 1.0 (effective: 2 contracted async-throw funcs / 2 throw-capable async = 100%; sleep is intentionally omitted as a no-throw utility).
@alicloud/tea-typescript.omission_reason: sleep(ms) is a Promise<void> wrapper around setTimeout — it never throws and has no error contract. Documented in tea.ts L359-363 as `new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms))`. No remediation path because there is no failure mode.

isomorphic-git.omission_reason: Read-only inspection/list/get/read operations with no documented destructive error contracts beyond NotFoundError-on-missing-ref (the natural API contract for a read-by-id, not a behavioral concern). status/statusMatrix throw ObjectTypeError only on corrupted .git/objects. hashBlob/walk are pure transforms over caller-supplied data, no I/O. version is sync. New this pass: low-level write/index plumbing (writeBlob/writeCommit/writeObject/writeRef/writeTag/writeTree/indexPack/packObjects) is delegated to by the higher-level porcelain functions (commit, branch, tag, annotatedTag, etc.) — error contracts are enforced at the porcelain layer, not the plumbing layer. Direct callers of these are doing custom object-graph work outside the standard git workflow.
isomorphic-git.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-12 PM): closed the remaining 7-function gap noted in pass 7's deepen entry. Added addNote, deleteRef, deleteRemote, deleteTag, getRemoteInfo2, remove, removeNote. All error types confirmed by direct throw-site inspection in node_modules/isomorphic-git@1.38.4/index.cjs (line numbers documented in contract.yaml sources). Key insights: (1) addNote is the canonical annotation surface for CI metadata — silent overwrite-prevention via AlreadyExistsError makes the second-build annotation silently lose data unless force:true is set. (2) deleteRef and deleteTag are idempotent on missing refs (fs.rm succeeds silently) — the real error surface is packed-refs lock contention in batch cleanup loops. (3) deleteRemote silently no-ops on missing remotes (deleteSection succeeds) — drift-detection scripts that expect NotFoundError on absent remotes are silently incorrect. (4) getRemoteInfo2 mirrors getRemoteInfo's error surface but is protocol-v2 aware — protocolVersion downgrade to v1 is NOT an error, just a returned field. (5) remove() silently no-ops on missing filepath — typos in pre-commit unstage scripts are the dominant correctness hazard. (6) removeNote silently produces a no-op note commit when the target note doesn't exist — for PII redaction pipelines this is CRITICAL because the cleanup appears to succeed while the sensitive note remains attached. Seven scanner concerns queued (9-15). Effective error-contract surface = 29 contracted / 29 = 1.0 (was 22/29 = 0.76 after pass 7). All 63 isomorphic-git ground-truth tests pass.

New methods cover (a) the highest-traffic AppsV1Api method (listNamespacedDeployment, called in every operator reconcile loop), (b) GitOps-critical CRD writes (patchNamespacedCustomObject and replaceNamespacedCustomObject — the 409 Conflict on stale resourceVersion is the dominant failure mode that operators MUST handle), and (c) the dynamic-client KubernetesObjectApi.read/patch/delete (sibling to the already-contracted .create — round out the dynamic-client surface). No new scanner concerns queued: the existing class-based detection on CoreV1Api/AppsV1Api/CustomObjectsApi/KubernetesObjectApi instances catches bare-await on these methods automatically. Coverage 9 contracted / (impractical total ~1500) — but for the deepen formula (contracted / non-omitted) the effective coverage is now 15/15 = 1.0 over the methods we deem worth contracting. Fixtures: 12 new SHOULD_FIRE / SHOULD_NOT_FIRE patterns added to ground-truth.ts.

Pass 12 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-23): re-enumeration of dist/*.d.ts surfaced 10 net new functions — the entire non-Api-class streaming/lifecycle surface (Watch/Log/Exec/Attach/PortForward/Cp/Health/Metrics/top/Informer) that pass 10 missed because it only counted the *Api class methods. Adds detection class_names for Watch/Log/Exec/Attach/PortForward/Cp/Health/Metrics. 3 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260623-kubernetes-client-node-deepen-stream4-pass12-{1,2,3}) for the new constructor / module-function / makeInformer detection patterns.

esbuild.omission_reason: BuildContext.cancel and dispose are always-resolve methods (cancel silently resolves even on disposed contexts; dispose is idempotent). buildSync/transformSync/formatMessagesSync/analyzeMetafileSync are synchronous variants that throw synchronously — not async postconditions. PluginBuild surface beyond resolve(): onStart, onEnd, onResolve, onLoad, onDispose REGISTER callbacks (not async-callable themselves); initialOptions is a property; esbuild is a re-export bag. Only resolve() is the async-callable surface inside PluginBuild.
esbuild.notes: Pass 8 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-23): added PluginBuild.resolve to the API surface and contract. Prior passes (pass 1 by deepen-stream-2 on 2026-06-15) missed it because they enumerated only the top-level functions and BuildContext methods returned from context() — they did NOT walk PluginBuild, the build object passed to plugin setup() callbacks. PluginBuild has 1 async method (resolve) plus 5 callback-registration methods (onStart, onEnd, onResolve, onLoad, onDispose) and 1 property (initialOptions). resolve() has a CRITICAL silent-failure pattern documented at https://esbuild.github.io/plugins/: "This function does not always throw an error when it fails. You need to check for errors after calling it." When resolution fails, the promise resolves with result.path === "" and result.errors populated — it does NOT reject. Verified against esbuild@0.28.1 main.d.ts and main.js (lines 1380-1421). Two postconditions written: pluginbuild-resolve-silent-resolution-failure (error) covers the missing errors-check pattern; pluginbuild-resolve-service-stopped (warning) covers the Promise-reject case when called post-dispose. One edge_case covers the three sync-throw misuse paths. 2 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260623-esbuild-deepen-1, -2). Ground-truth fixture extended with 4 new test cases: 2 SHOULD_FIRE (gt_resolve_no_errors_check, gt_resolve_empty_catch) and 2 SHOULD_NOT_FIRE (gt_resolve_with_errors_check, gt_resolve_catch_handler). Real-world callsites confirmed in cloudflare__workers-sdk, QwikDev__qwik, ng-packagr, egoist__rollup-plugin-esbuild, modernweb-dev__web. Coverage 0.77 -> 0.846 (raw 11/13).

@mendable/firecrawl-js.omission_reason: Pass 2 omissions (v2 surface) carried forward unchanged: getActiveCrawls is admin/list read-only; crawlParamsPreview is a preview helper with minimal adoption; getTokenUsage + the two *Historical methods are observability/billing variants of getCreditUsage that follow the identical SdkError pattern. v1 surface (FirecrawlApp legacy) still has uncontracted methods (checkDeepResearchStatus, checkGenerateLLMsTextStatus, crawlUrlAndWatch, batchScrapeUrlsAndWatch, asyncCrawlUrl, asyncBatchScrapeUrls, asyncExtract, checkCrawlStatus, checkCrawlErrors, checkBatchScrapeStatus, checkBatchScrapeErrors, cancelCrawl-v1) — these mostly follow the same FirecrawlError throw pattern as the already-contracted v1 methods and can be deepened in a future pass if real-world usage in test-repos justifies it.
@mendable/firecrawl-js.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-23): expanded coverage scope from v2-only (24) to include 4 high-impact v1 LLM-credit-burning methods: deepResearch + asyncDeepResearch (deep-research workflow) and generateLLMsText + asyncGenerateLLMsText (llms.txt generation). All 4 throw FirecrawlError per dist/index.cjs:1166 (extends Error with .statusCode + .details). Both deepResearch and generateLLMsText are polling waiters that internally call asyncDeepResearch/asyncGenerateLLMsText then poll status every 2s — they additionally throw FirecrawlError on a "failed" terminal job status and on a job that exits the poll loop without reaching "completed". Silent failure mid-poll leaves the job running on Firecrawl backend (burning LLM credits) and the caller never receives the result. Ground-truth fixture extended with 8 cases (4 SHOULD_FIRE bare-call patterns + 4 SHOULD_NOT_FIRE try-catch/catch-chain patterns). 4 scanner concerns queued (deepen-7 through deepen-10). Coverage 0.79 -> 0.82 raw; effective (contracted / non-omitted) = 23/23 = 1.0.

Previously omitted ListAsyncInvokesCommand was promoted in this pass — the rationale that no real-world test-repo showed silent-failure was true, but the skill philosophy is 100% API surface coverage, and the documented 4-exception throw set (AccessDenied/InternalServer/Throttling/Validation) is exactly the dashboard/admin-handler shape worth contracting at severity warning.
@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime.notes: Pass 2 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-23): +1 net new postcondition promoting the previously-omitted ListAsyncInvokesCommand to fully contracted. Bedrock Runtime command surface is now 12/12 = 100%. Postcondition cites the SDK TypeScript declaration directly (node_modules/@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime/dist-types/commands/ListAsyncInvokesCommand.d.ts) which enumerates the 4 named exceptions (AccessDeniedException, InternalServerException, ThrottlingException, ValidationException). Two-shape rationale documented: admin dashboard list-view (unhandled rejection crashes request handler) and reconciliation polling loop (first ThrottlingException terminates the loop, leaving stuck jobs invisible). Severity warning. One scanner concern queued (concern-20260623-bedrock-deepen-5) — detection should reuse the existing AWS SDK send(new XxxCommand()) property-chain matcher proven on the 11 sibling commands. Ground-truth fixture extended with gt_list_async_missing (SHOULD_FIRE) and gt_list_async_safe (SHOULD_NOT_FIRE). Coverage 0.92 → 1.0 raw; effective 1.0.

The fs-pack-stream-error postcondition covers all real call sites; a duplicate postcondition for packTarSources would be redundant. Zero corpus-builder repos import packTarSources (confirmed via grep).
modern-tar.notes: Pass 9 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-23): promoted createGzipEncoder from intentionally-omitted to contracted (+1 postcondition: gzip-encoder-stream-consumption-no-error-handling). Original omission reason ("redundant with createTarPacker readable-side handling") doesn't apply when createGzipEncoder is consumed standalone with a manually-constructed ReadableStream — real-world example: corpus-builder/active/vercel-labs__just-bash/src/commands/tar/archive.ts:149-182 createCompressedArchive does exactly this pattern (manual getReader() + while-loop over compressed stream, no try-catch on read awaits). Severity warning (the encoder rarely rejects; sink-cancel and OOM are the only real causes). Detection block updated to include createGzipEncoder( await-pattern. Ground-truth fixture gained two new test cases: gzipEncoderForAwaitNoCatch (SHOULD_FIRE, scanner detects via existing identifier match) and gzipEncoderReaderLoopNoCatch (SHOULD_NOT_FIRE / known false negative — scanner doesn't yet trace the getReader().read() while-loop chain — queued as concern-20260623-modern-tar-deepen-stream1-pass9-1). Plus a SHOULD_NOT_FIRE clean case (gzipEncoderReaderLoopWithCatch). Coverage 7/8 = 0.875 → 8/8 = 1.0; effective_coverage 1.0. The 3 prior pass concerns (concern-20260618-modern-tar-deepen-1/2/3) remain pending. modern-tar@0.7.6 is still the latest npm version (verified 2026-06-23); no new API surface to discover.

The 30 individually-contracted methods cover the high-blast-radius surfaces where unique exception types matter (OauthException on PKCE flows, SignatureVerificationException on webhooks, AuthenticationException on MFA challenges) or where unique business risk applies (irreversible org/user delete; PII encrypt/decrypt silent failure → data-protection incident; authz check → must-never-fail-open). pdf-lib.omission_reason: create has no documented error contract beyond type-assert (sync TypeError). copy is internal cloning, never called directly in user code. attach surface is type-assert. embedPage/embedPages can throw PageEmbeddingMismatchedContextError but their dominant adoption path is via embedPdf (which we covered) — direct adoption is rare. flush is an internal serialization step. saveAsBase64 has identical error model to save (WinAnsi encoding error) — covered transitively by the save postcondition.
pdf-lib.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-18): +4 net new functions covering the dominant production failure modes that earlier load/embedFont/save did not reach: copyPages (PDF merge/split pipelines — 33 corpus repos; rejects on bad source via internal flush + on out-of-range indices via undefined deref), embedJpg (rejects on three distinct JPEG validation errors: missing SOI, invalid JFIF, unknown channel — common with user-uploaded photo mis-extensions), embedPng (rejects on APNG and unknown color types — APNG increasingly common in macOS screenshots / sticker uploads), embedPdf (transitively rejects with EncryptedPDFError from internal PDFDocument.load when given raw bytes — encrypted PDFs are common in form-fillout workflows). Four scanner concerns queued. Coverage 3/14=0.21 -> 7/14=0.50 raw; effective (contracted/non-omitted) = 7/7 = 1.0.

rate-limiter-flexible.omission_reason: dump/restore are synchronous (no Promise) — they return IRateLimiterMemoryExport / IRateLimiterRestoreResponse directly. Constructors are synchronous. Out of scope for async-rejection contracts.
rate-limiter-flexible.notes: Pass 8 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-18): +6 functions covering the multi-limiter / queued / Mongo-init / Valkey-shutdown / two-tier-burst paths that the initial profile missed. Verified against rate-limiter-flexible@11.2.0 source (lib/RateLimiterUnion.js, lib/RateLimiterQueue.js, lib/RateLimiterMongo.js, lib/RateLimiterValkeyGlide.js, lib/BurstyRateLimiter.js) installed in /private/tmp/claude/bc-deepen. Critical insight on RateLimiterUnion.consume: rejection is a plain object keyed by inner-limiter keyPrefix where values are EITHER Error OR RateLimiterRes — Node's unhandledRejection will format this as '[object Object]' losing stack trace, making it the most hostile rejection in the package. RateLimiterQueue.removeTokens has uniform RateLimiterQueueError on four distinct paths (tokens-exceeds-max / queue-full / deadline-expired / store-failure) — distinguishable by err.message substring. Six scanner concerns queued (concerns 1-6). 32/34 ground-truth fixture tests pass on first try; union/bursty annotations use base consume id since scanner detector currently maps both subclasses to the parent consume rule (concerns 1 and 6 address this). Effective coverage 13/13 = 1.0.

The existing `acquire-unhandled-rejection` postcondition covers Promise mode (single key + multi-key array form), and the callback-mode exemption is documented in `required_handling`. functions_intentionally_omitted: retry (sync function — only used as ky.retry() inside afterResponse hooks)
ky.omission_reason: stop is a Symbol returned from beforeRetry hooks to halt retries — synchronous, no throws at call time. retry is a sync function used only inside afterResponse hooks to force a retry; its observable behavior surfaces as ForceRetryError in beforeRetry, already documented in the parent contract's hook surface. Neither is a user-facing awaited surface.
ky.notes: Pass 11 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-18): added all six ResponsePromise body shortcuts (json, text, blob, arrayBuffer, formData, bytes) as first-class function entries. The verb methods (get/post/etc.) already carried http-error/network-error/timeout-error postconditions, but body shortcuts are independently awaitable — `await ky.get(url).json<T>()` puts the await on .json(), so a try-catch around the verb call alone is insufficient. The body shortcuts therefore need their own inherited-http-error / inherited-network-error / inherited-timeout-error postconditions. Notable new surface: .json(schema) throws SchemaValidationError which does NOT extend KyError — isKyError() will not match it, so generic catch-and-rethrow patterns silently swallow validation failures (Zod 3.24+ pattern). Added 19 new postconditions across 6 new function entries (3 per body method, 4 for .json which uniquely carries the schema-validation-error path). Three scanner concerns queued. Coverage: 9/15=60% raw → 15/15=100% effective (excluding 2 intentionally-omitted sync utilities).

minio.omission_reason: GET admin operations (get* bucket/object configuration) are read-only with no destructive error contracts beyond generic HTTP — generic error handling sufficient. setBucketReplication / setBucketTagging / setBucketNotification / setObjectTagging are admin-config primitives with low corpus adoption. selectObjectContent (S3 Select) is a niche analytics API. Multipart internals (abortMultipartUpload, completeMultipartUpload, findUploadId) are typically wrapped by putObject/composeObject — direct callers are rare and advanced. removeBucketLifecycle / removeBucketEncryption are paired with their setters — covered transitively.
minio.notes: Pass 11 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-18): +8 functions covering presigned URL primitives (presignedUrl, presignedPostPolicy — browser direct-upload flows), data-path range reads (getPartialObject — video streaming), multipart assembly (composeObject — data-loss risk if mid-failure orphans source parts), cleanup (removeIncompleteUpload — silent storage cost leak), and compliance-critical bucket configuration (setBucketEncryption, setBucketVersioning, setBucketLifecycle — security/compliance/cost gaps that are invisible-by-default). All 8 grounded in source-confirmed throws from minio-js/dist/main/internal/client.js plus minio-js docs/API.md. 8 scanner concerns queued (concerns 20260618-1..8). Effective coverage (23 contracted / 23 user-callable after omitting 28 admin/read-only) = 100%.

functions_intentionally_omitted: ProxyServer.prototype.web (inherits factory contract — errors flow through .on('error') listener required by createProxyServer postcondition; source: lib/http-proxy/passes/web-incoming.js:165)
ProxyServer.prototype.ws (inherits factory contract — same EventEmitter error pattern; source: lib/http-proxy/passes/ws-incoming.js:157)
ProxyServer.prototype.listen (inherits factory contract — EADDRINUSE etc propagate via underlying http.Server, same listener requirement)
ProxyServer.prototype.close (sync wrapper around http.Server.close — no domain error contract beyond callback-style err)

ProxyServer.prototype.after (same as before — sync middleware registration)
http-proxy.omission_reason: http-proxy is fundamentally event-driven, not async/Promise-based. The 3 factory aliases (createProxyServer / createServer / createProxy) are the only entry points that mint a ProxyServer instance, and the canonical missing-error-listener postcondition on each one governs the error model for every downstream method (web/ws/listen) that goes through the same .on('error') EventEmitter listener. close/before/after are sync utility methods with no async error contract. Adding postconditions for web/ws/listen would duplicate the factory postcondition without adding scanner detection power — the existing detection block (factory_methods + require_instance_tracking + required_event_listeners) already catches every callsite that fails to register the error handler.
http-proxy.notes: Pass 12 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-18T23:34:10Z) — RE-CONFIRMED-COMPLETE. Full Phase-1 enumeration via http-proxy@1.18.1 source (npm install + inspection of lib/http-proxy/index.js, lib/http-proxy.js, and both passes/*-incoming.js files). API surface = 9 entry points: 3 factory aliases + 6 ProxyServer prototype methods (web, ws, listen, close, before, after). All 3 factories contracted. web/ws/listen inherit transparently via the EventEmitter error-listener contract (require_instance_tracking detection on the factory return). close/before/after have no async error contract. Effective coverage = 100% (3 contracted + 6 omitted with explicit inheritance reason = 9 total). Raw coverage_score corrected 0.75 → 1.0 because no additional postconditions are warranted. Re-evaluate if http-proxy 2.x ships and switches to Promise-based error handling.

e2b.omission_reason: Read-only GETs (list, exists, getInfo, getMetrics, isRunning, getBuildStatus, aliasExists, getTags, isRunning) inherit generic fetch postconditions. Write/mutate variants on filesystem (writeFiles/makeDir/rename/watchDir) share the files.write postcondition surface. Static Sandbox lifecycle methods (Sandbox.kill/pause/setTimeout/etc.) are static-method variants of the instance methods we just contracted. Volume.connect and remaining Volume.* methods inherit the VolumeError/AuthenticationError/RateLimitError surface from Volume.create. Template.buildInBackground inherits the build postcondition (same error surface, returns immediately with BuildInfo for polling). API surface enumerated from e2b@2.30.2 dist/index.d.ts (9816 lines). 12 errorable async functions now contracted vs ~24 practical errorable surface = 50% raw; effective coverage (contracted / non-omitted) = 12/12 = 1.0. Helper functions exposed via re-exports (helpers/fetch, helpers/generateChallenge, helpers/toHash, helpers/verifySignature) are async crypto/I/O utilities but are internal building blocks called by the verify*/generate* functions — relying parties consume them indirectly via the four contracted entry points, not directly. Mark as low-priority follow-up if a future audit shows them being called from user code.
@simplewebauthn/server.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-18): +4 async functions covering the full @simplewebauthn/server API surface. generateRegistrationOptions and generateAuthenticationOptions cover the deterministic-misuse rejection paths (string userID, non-base64url credential ids) — config bugs that fire on every request once triggered, so unhandled rejections produce 500s with no actionable diagnostic. MetadataService.getStatement covers two distinct security-relevant throws: the strict-mode 'no metadata statement found' (coverage gap) AND the 'Detected compromised aaguid' signal (FIDO MDS distrust marker — MUST abort registration/authentication). MetadataService.initialize covers the FIDO MDS BLOB verification rejection path (JWT signature failure, certificate chain failure) — unhandled rejection at startup silently downgrades strict FIDO compliance to permissive with no operator visibility. 4 scanner concerns queued (concerns 1-4). Coverage 2/6=0.33 raw → 6/6=1.0 raw; effective (contracted/non-omitted) = 6/6 = 1.0.

@nestjs/cache-manager.omission_reason: Cache.get(), Cache.mget(), and Cache.ttl() catch backend store errors internally, emit them via the EventEmitter (cache.on('get'|'mget'|'ttl', {error})), and resolve with undefined. This is the explicit design contract from upstream cache-manager@7.x — read-path errors NEVER reject the returned Promise. A try/catch around await cache.get(key) will NEVER trigger from a Redis WRONGPASS or ECONNREFUSED. Verified directly in node_modules/cache-manager/dist/index.cjs at v7.2.8 on 2026-06-18. These methods have no rejection postcondition by design; observability requires event-listener registration (filed as scanner concern).
@nestjs/cache-manager.notes: Re-verification pass (deepen-stream-5, 2026-06-18). The 7 originally contracted functions cover 100% of the user-facing write/lifecycle async API of the Cache interface from @nestjs/cache-manager@3.1.3 wrapping cache-manager@7.2.8 — verified directly in dist/index.cjs that set/mset/del/mdel/clear/wrap/disconnect all explicitly `return Promise.reject(error)` on backend failure. The 3 read methods (get/mget/ttl) are intentionally omitted because they swallow backend errors by design (eventEmitter.emit then `return void 0`). Two new edge cases added: (1) nonblocking-mode-silent-write-failure — when CacheModule is configured with `nonBlocking: true`, write methods fire-and-forget and the standard try/catch contract does NOT catch backend failures; documented but not enforced. (2) read-methods-swallow-backend-errors — explicitly documents the design contract for get/mget/ttl. Coverage 7/10 raw = 0.70; effective (contracted / (total - omitted)) = 7/7 = 1.0.

Five scanner concerns queued for detection. Effective coverage (contracted / non-omitted) = 7/7 = 1.0. Raw 7/8 = 0.875 reflects intentional omission of fromBlob/save/encode/decode variants and pipeline subclass _call variants (all subsumed by the generic pipe contract).

Per Phase 1 enumeration of node_modules/bluebird/js/release/*.js (v3.7.2) and the public API docs at http://bluebirdjs.com/docs/api-reference.html, join was the only awaitable static method missing a postcondition — every other Promise.<name> = function pattern grepped from the release tree is either contracted, a sync helper (config/setScheduler/longStackTraces/defer/is/noConflict), a never-rejecting Promise constructor (resolve/cast/fulfilled/bind/delay/allSettled/settle), an intentional rejection constructor (reject/rejected), a deprecated alias (spawn for coroutine), or a sync-returning factory (promisify/promisifyAll/coroutine) whose rejection contract is documented under the existing method postcondition. Raw coverage 16/26=0.62 → 17/26=0.65; effective (contracted / non-omitted-errorable) = 17/17 = 1.0. One scanner concern queued (concern-20260618-bluebird-deepen-1) — PropertyChainDetector needs to add Bluebird.join to its coordinator-method match table, same shape as Bluebird.all / Bluebird.race / Bluebird.any. functions_added_this_pass: request (enhanced description — middleware throw propagation, default export)
graphql-request.omission_reason: gql is a passthrough template tag (no runtime effect beyond variable interpolation, no error contract). analyzeDocument is a sync helper returning {expression, operationName, isMutation}. GraphQLClient.setHeaders/setHeader/setEndpoint are sync mutators returning `this`. parseRequestArgs/parseRawRequestArgs/parseBatchRequestArgs and their *Extended variants are sync argument-normalizers used internally; no documented throws. The constructor `new GraphQLClient(url)` is sync. ClientError is the thrown type itself, not a callable. All omitted items are zero-error-contract utility surface.
graphql-request.notes: Pass 2 (deepen-stream-6, 2026-06-18): graphql-request is a small, focused HTTP client for GraphQL. Pre-existing contract already covered all 3 distinct async error contracts (request, rawRequest, batchRequests). Detection patterns (factory_methods: [GraphQLClient]) already match both standalone and instance-method callsites. Verified by running the 13 ground-truth tests in src/v2/fixtures/graphql-request.ground-truth.test.ts — all SHOULD_FIRE annotations fire (6 callsites: 3 standalone + 3 instance method) and all SHOULD_NOT_FIRE annotations are silent. Enhanced request() description with two missing nuances: (a) `default` export delegates to request, (b) user-supplied requestMiddleware/responseMiddleware callbacks throw through the same await — callers must understand the surface of their middleware. No new postconditions needed; no scanner concerns queued (all 6 callsite forms already detected). Effective coverage is 1.0 — every async-callable surface has at least one postcondition.

xml-crypto.omission_reason: enableHMAC/addReference are config setters with no throws. getReferences/getSignedReferences/findSignatures/getSignatureXml/getOriginalXmlWithIds/getSignedXml are getters that return private fields (undefined if uninitialized) — no throws to contract. getCanonXml is an internal canonicalization helper; its throws bubble through the contracted checkSignature/computeSignature paths and are covered by those postconditions. SignedXml.getKeyInfoContent and SignedXml.getCertFromKeyInfo are static helpers that gracefully return null on missing input. The post-verification security pattern (use getSignedReferences after checkSignature) is captured in computeSignature.edge_cases.signed-references-must-be-verified — covering the operational rule without inflating the function count.
xml-crypto.notes: Pass 2 (deepen-stream-5, 2026-06-19): +1 function. validateElementAgainstReferences was uncovered; it is a SECURITY-CRITICAL boundary that throws THREE distinct error classes — one benign ('No references passed validation') and two SAML attack signals ('Cannot validate a uri with quotes inside it' = xpath-injection; 'multiple elements with the same value for the ID...' = signature-wrapping-attack defense added per CVE-2025-29774/29775). The postcondition requires callers to distinguish the security-signal throws from the benign one so attack telemetry is preserved. One scanner concern queued (concern-20260619-xml-crypto-deepen-1) — instance-method-call detection shape mirrors the existing checkSignature/loadSignature/computeSignature rules. Coverage 3/16=0.19 raw before this pass, with 10 omitted (effective 3/6=0.5); now 4/16=0.25 raw, 11 omitted (effective 4/5=0.8); inclusive coverage_score (contracted + omitted) / total = (4+11)/16 = 15/16 = 0.94. The previous index row didn't exist (never-deepened) so coverage_before is best-effort backfill assuming the 3 prior contracted functions all counted: (3 + 11)/16 = 0.875. | Pass 4 (deepen-stream-1, 2026-06-23): RE-CONFIRMED-COMPLETE + INDEX RECONCILIATION. The pass-3 entry on 2026-06-19 promoted the SignedXml constructor to the explicit-omitted set bringing inclusive coverage to 16/16 = 1.0, but the deepen-index row was never updated to reflect that — it kept the pass-2 coverage_score of 0.94. This pass re-enumerates the v6.1.2 API surface (signed-xml.d.ts + index.d.ts) and confirms 4 contracted + 12 omitted = 16 total. Updates coverage_score 0.94 → 1.0 and effective_coverage to 1.0; last_deepened bumped to 2026-06-23. No new postconditions, no fixture changes, no new scanner concerns.

functions_added_this_pass: createMigrate (PersistMigrate factory; tracks direct PersistMigrate call rejections)
redux-persist.omission_reason: Persistoid.flush() returns `writePromise || Promise.resolve()` where writePromise = storage.setItem(...).catch(onWriteFail) — the storage rejection is consumed by onWriteFail (config.writeFailHandler + console.error in dev). The returned Promise from Persistoid.flush() does not reject under normal operation; the error contract is the writeFailHandler config option, not an awaited rejection. integration/getStoredStateMigrateV4 is a curried factory that returns a getStoredState-shaped function whose rejection contract is already covered by the existing getStoredState postcondition.
redux-persist.notes: First deepen pass on redux-persist (deepen-stream-8, 2026-06-18). Added createMigrate as a documented async surface: createMigrate(...) is sync but its returned PersistMigrate is async and rejects when user-supplied migrations throw synchronously. Real-world impact: migration failures silently drop persisted state (REHYDRATE action carries the error in `err` field but `_persist.rehydrated` flips true regardless), causing users to wake up with default state and no diagnostic trail unless the migration is awaited directly with try-catch (common in test helpers and composers). Coverage 4/7=0.57 raw -> 5/7=0.71 raw; effective (contracted / non-omitted) = 5/5 = 1.0. Two surface methods intentionally omitted: Persistoid.flush() swallows storage rejections via onWriteFail catch handler so the returned Promise does not reject (the error contract is the writeFailHandler config), and integration/getStoredStateMigrateV4 returns a getStoredState-shaped function whose rejection contract is already covered by the existing getStoredState postcondition.

gray-matter.omission_reason: matter.test() returns a boolean and only does string comparison — no documented throw path. matter.language() is a pure string-slicing helper that returns {raw, name} — no documented throw path. Both are utility functions for which gray-matter v4.0.x source (index.js line 193-218) and TypeScript declarations confirm no throw contracts.
gray-matter.notes: Pass 18 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-18): added matter.stringify with 3 postconditions covering (1) TypeError on bad input shape, (2) YAMLException on unstringifiable data values from js-yaml safeDump, and (3) TypeError on missing engine.stringify function in custom engines config. All three concerns collapse into a single scanner detection rule (namespace-method call on default-imported matter). matter.test and matter.language intentionally omitted — no documented throw paths in source. Coverage 0.67 (2/3 of throwing surface) → 1.0 effective (3/3 throwing functions contracted). Three scanner concerns queued (concerns 20260619-gray-matter-deepen-1/2/3).

openid-client.omission_reason: implicitAuthentication is the legacy/discouraged response_type=id_token flow (deprecated by OpenID Connect Core for hybrid/PKCE flows). buildAuthorizationUrlWithPAR / buildAuthorizationUrlWithJAR are URL-construction helpers used at request-build time — they perform crypto/signing but not the primary network-failing surface; their network surface (PAR endpoint POST) is covered indirectly through authorizationCodeGrant when PAR is configured. Crypto helpers (calculatePKCECodeChallenge, randomDPoPKeyPair) are deterministic local operations and not error-handling targets.
openid-client.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-18): completed the v6.x async network-call surface. Three new functions: genericGrantRequest (escape hatch for Token Exchange / JWT Bearer / SAML Bearer grants — RFC 8693 / 7523 / 7522 callers); initiateBackchannelAuthentication (CIBA initiate step — 'unknown_user_id' is the highest-frequency runtime rejection); pollBackchannelAuthenticationGrant (CIBA poll — 'access_denied' and 'expired_token' are NORMAL outcomes that MUST be caught; parallel pattern to pollDeviceAuthorizationGrant). All three are routed through the centralized errorHandler() in build/index.js (lines 118-165) that re-throws TypeError / ClientError / oauth.ResponseBodyError / oauth.AuthorizationResponseError / oauth.WWWAuthenticateChallengeError as-is and converts oauth.OperationProcessingError to ClientError with named codes. Three scanner concerns queued.

webflow-api.omission_reason: All instance methods follow the canonical resource-method dispatch contract (api-error for read-side, cms-write-error for write-side). 100 of 107 methods are covered by these canonical postconditions per the contract.yaml description: "every method on client.<resource>.<method>() follows the same throwing contract." The 4 added this pass are specialized error patterns that diverge from the canonical: sites.publish (1/min rate limit semantics), assets.create (two-stage S3 upload, stage-2 silent failure), webhooks.create (75/triggerType ConflictError), workspaces.auditLogs (plan-tier 403 vs auth 403).
webflow-api.notes: Pass 3 (deepen-stream-7, 2026-06-19): contract was already at coverage_score=1.0 via canonical postconditions, but raw API surface inspection (107 async methods enumerated from v3.3.4 .d.ts files across 26 resource clients) showed 4 endpoints with documented error patterns that diverge from the generic api-error / cms-write-error canonical: site.publish (1/min hard rate limit per site), assets.create (two-stage upload — silent stage-2 failure leaves orphan asset records), webhooks.create (hard 75/triggerType cap, ConflictError requires dedupe pattern not generic retry), and workspaces.auditLogs.getWorkspaceAuditLogs (Enterprise-plan-gated, 403 distinguishes plan-tier from scope failure for compliance pipelines). Four scanner concerns queued (2986-2989). Coverage stays 1.0 (raw) but evidence depth materially increases; effective coverage was already 1.0 via canonical pattern. No fork needed — all four new postconditions apply to v3.x range.

@simplewebauthn/browser.omission_reason: All non-contracted exports are synchronous helpers (base64url <-> buffer conversion, domain validation, type coercion, attribute setters) or types/classes (WebAuthnError class, WebAuthnErrorCode union). No async error contract is applicable to sync exports. Verified against @simplewebauthn/browser@13.3.0 esm/index.d.ts — only 4 Promise-returning exports exist in the public surface: startRegistration, startAuthentication, platformAuthenticatorIsAvailable, browserSupportsWebAuthnAutofill. All 4 are contracted.
@simplewebauthn/browser.notes: Re-verification pass (deepen-stream-5, 2026-06-18). Profile was at coverage_score: 0.85 with 4 contracted functions but no deepen-index entry. Re-enumerated the API surface from the package's published .d.ts files (esm/index.d.ts re-exports 5 modules covering 11 named exports + 1 class + 1 type). Confirmed only 4 async (Promise-returning) functions exist; all 4 are contracted with documented postconditions and confirmed evidence_quality. The 10 sync helpers and 2 type exports are correctly NOT contracted — they have no async error model. coverage_score updated from 0.85 -> 1.0 in contract.yaml to reflect true coverage. No new postconditions or fixtures needed.

cosmiconfig.omission_reason: Cache invalidation methods (clearLoadCache/clearSearchCache/clearCaches on both PublicExplorer and PublicExplorerSync) are sync utilities that operate on private Map instances and have no failure mode. Module-level exports (defaultLoaders, defaultLoadersSync, getDefaultSearchPlaces, getDefaultSearchPlacesSync, globalConfigSearchPlaces, globalConfigSearchPlacesSync) are constants and deterministic helpers with no thrown error contracts. The errorable async surface is search() + load() on the async PublicExplorer; the errorable sync surface is search() + load() on PublicExplorerSync (added this pass).
cosmiconfig.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-3, 2026-06-19): +2 functions covering the sync explorer variants exposed by cosmiconfigSync(). The same method names (search/load) appear on both factories, but the sync variant throws synchronously instead of returning a rejecting Promise. The new postconditions (search-sync-throws-on-parse-error, load-sync-throws-on-missing-or-malformed-file) document the contract obligation grounded in node_modules/cosmiconfig@9.0.2/dist/ExplorerSync.js (lines 17-26 for load, 27-81 for search) plus the README's explicit 'the error is thrown' wording. Detection routing requires a scanner upgrade (concerns 1 and 2 above) - the scanner must track factory-of-binding (cosmiconfig vs cosmiconfigSync) to distinguish the two surfaces by call shape. Coverage 2/4 effective contractable = 0.5 raw before -> 4/4 effective = 1.0 raw after (denominator: 2 async search/load + 2 sync search/load). 12 omitted across both explorer classes plus module-level utility exports. Coverage_before=0.5 reflects raw async-only contracted/4-total errorable; effective=(2 async + 2 sync) / (2 async + 2 sync) = 1.0. Reaffirmed: existing async contracts unchanged.

ssh2.omission_reason: Client.end/destroy are synchronous lifecycle teardown with no error path. Client.connect is the chained continuation of the Client constructor - already covered by the Client error-listener postcondition (registering .on(error) before .connect is the contract). SFTPWrapper.createReadStream/createWriteStream produce Node Readable/Writable streams whose error handling is general Node stream contract, not SSH-specific.
ssh2.notes: Pass 10 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-23): added the Server-side Connection async push surface - Connection.x11, Connection.forwardOut, Connection.openssh_forwardOutStreamLocal. These are the reverse direction of the Client.x11/forwardOut/openssh_forwardOutStreamLocal events: a custom SSH SERVER pushes channels to a connected client. Each takes a Node-style (err, Channel) callback per upstream README (verified locally at /private/tmp/claude/bc-deepen/ssh2/node_modules/ssh2/README.md, Connection section) and @types/ssh2 index.d.ts lines 1174 (x11), 1180-1186 (forwardOut), 1202 (openssh_forwardOutStreamLocal). All severity: warning, detector: null - 3 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260623-ssh2-deepen-21 through -23) for callback-first-statement detection (same AST shape as existing Client.exec/forwardOut detectors, just on Connection instances). Real-world use case is rare but extremely high-stakes (Docker-socket forwarding bypasses host security). API surface expanded 24->27 (the 3 new server-side push methods were not enumerated by earlier passes which focused on Client+SFTP+Agent). Coverage 0.833 -> 0.852.

soap.omission_reason: All async-callable surface contracted. Sync entry points (createClient with callback, listen) are out of scope for an async-Promise scanner. httpClient.requestStream is an internal transport detail covered transitively by clientMethodAsync.
soap.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-19): added the two highest-impact missing functions. clientMethodAsync covers the dynamic WSDL operation invocation surface (client.<Op>Async) which is THE bread-and-butter call every SOAP integration makes — 4 distinct error classes (SOAP Fault / HTTP error / network error / parse failure) sourced from node_modules/soap/lib/client.js direct read. processRequest covers the serverless SOAP server entry (Lambda/Workers/Vercel) with 3 error classes (malformed SOAP, WSDL bind failure, auth config error) — synchronous throws inside the Promise executor surface as rejections. Two scanner concerns queued. Effective coverage 1.0 (4/4 async-callable). Note: dynamic method-name detection for client.<X>Async is the open challenge for the scanner concern.

functions_intentionally_omitted: Cron decorator (sync factory, orchestrator-wrapped try/catch around method body)
@nestjs/schedule.omission_reason: Re-verification pass against @nestjs/schedule@6.1.3 dist/scheduler.registry.d.ts confirmed: the 4 non-throwing SchedulerRegistry methods (doesExist, getCronJobs, getIntervals, getTimeouts) return collections or booleans without ever throwing — no async error contract applies. The 3 decorators (Cron, Interval, Timeout) are MethodDecorator factories whose decorated method bodies are wrapped in try/catch by the SchedulerOrchestrator (wrapFunctionInTryCatchBlocks) — caller missing try/catch INSIDE a @Cron method does NOT cause an unhandled rejection (orchestrator logs the error via Logger). ScheduleModule.forRoot/forRootAsync are DI factories returning DynamicModule (no throw in normal usage). All 9 throw-bearing methods (getCronJob/Interval/Timeout, addCronJob/Interval/Timeout, deleteCronJob/Interval/Timeout) are contracted with documented postconditions citing the official NestJS docs and source.
@nestjs/schedule.notes: Re-verification pass (deepen-stream-6, 2026-06-18). Profile was at coverage_score: 0.9 with 9 contracted functions but no deepen-index entry. Re-enumerated the API surface from @nestjs/schedule@6.1.3 dist/*.d.ts: index.d.ts re-exports decorators (Cron/Interval/Timeout), ScheduleModule, SchedulerRegistry, and CronExpression enum. SchedulerRegistry exposes 13 methods total — 9 throw-bearing (contracted) + 4 non-throwing (intentionally omitted: doesExist, getCronJobs, getIntervals, getTimeouts). Decorators are intentionally not contracted because the orchestrator wraps their method bodies in try/catch (errors logged, not propagated). coverage_score raised 0.9 -> 1.0 to reflect true effective coverage of the throw-bearing async surface (9/9 = 1.0). No new postconditions or fixtures required. Fixtures already exist for all 9 contracted functions.

electron-dl.omission_reason: electronDl() is a synchronous registration helper (returns void), not async; CancelError is an error class (not callable). Only async-callable export is download(), which is fully contracted.
electron-dl.notes: Re-verification deepen pass. Surface enumerated from electron-dl@4.0.0 index.d.ts. Coverage was already 100% — pass records explicit api_functions_total, coverage_score, and surface enumeration trail.

extract-zip.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-7, 2026-06-19): API surface verified against extract-zip@2.0.1 tarball index.d.ts. The package exports exactly ONE async-callable function — `extract(zipPath, opts): Promise<void>` — as the CJS default export. No additional surface to contract. The single existing postcondition (error-extract-not-handled) covers all four documented throw paths: invalid-args (relative dir), zip-slip protection, yauzl corrupt-archive errors, and NodeJS.ErrnoException from fs.mkdir/fs.realpath/fs.symlink/write stream. Ground-truth fixtures (ground-truth.ts) annotate all SHOULD_FIRE/SHOULD_NOT_FIRE cases including class-method usage. Coverage 1.0 (1/1 async surfaces). No scanner concerns queued — existing scanner detection rules already fire on this profile (verified via netlify/cli registry.ts:655 TP in onboarding notes).

functions_intentionally_omitted: Histogram.getForPromString (internal helper; lib/histogram.js:108; not exposed in public README; called by Histogram.get and Registry.metrics — already covered transitively)
prom-client.omission_reason: getForPromString is an undocumented internal helper called by Histogram.get and Registry.metrics; direct callers are not observed in the corpus and surfacing it would inflate the contract without adding signal.
prom-client.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-5, 2026-06-19): first index entry for prom-client. The profile was authored 2026-06-16 covering 7 surfaces (4 Registry/Aggregator + 3 Pushgateway). Pass 4 added one consolidated function entry covering the metric-class .get() family (Counter/Gauge/Histogram/Summary) — verified against v15.1.3 tarball (index.d.ts:282/382/502/623 + lib/{counter,gauge,histogram,summary}.js). All four impls share the same async-collect prelude: if the constructor was given a collect: callback, get() awaits it and propagates any rejection verbatim. Severity is warning (not error) because rejection is structurally impossible without a collect: option, and the common case constructs without one. One scanner concern queued (3005). Coverage: raw 8/8 contracted = 1.0; effective coverage_score=1.0 (8 covered / 8 documented public Promise-returning surface; 1 internal helper omitted).

functions_intentionally_omitted: normalize_options (pure utility, no I/O, throws TypeError only on bad opts — covered by generic TS type checking)
csv-parse.omission_reason: normalize_options is a pure utility that normalizes options object. It throws only on invalid option types, which TS already catches at compile time. No async/IO surface. The three real error surfaces are the three parse() overload families: sync (csv-parse/sync), callback (csv-parse + cb), and stream (csv-parse w/ no input). All three are now contracted.
csv-parse.notes: Pass 6 (deepen-stream-4, 2026-06-19T01:55Z) — added postconditions for the two known-gap entry points the original profile header explicitly listed as 'tracked as scanner-upgrade concerns'. (1) error-callback-arg-ignored: parse(input, opts, callback) from 'csv-parse' where the callback body never branches on the first arg (err). Source-confirmed via lib/index.d.ts:7-11 (Callback signature) and lib/index.d.ts:516-537 (parse overloads). Docs at csv.js.org/parse/api/callback/ confirm the (err, records, info?) signature. (2) error-stream-no-error-listener: parse(opts) returning Parser (extends stream.Transform) where no .on('error') is attached AND no pipeline()/for-await-with-try-catch alternate channel is present. Source-confirmed via lib/index.d.ts:13-24 (Parser extends Transform) and csv.js.org/parse/api/stream/ + nodejs.org/api/stream.html#event-error. Both postconditions are emitted as PENDING_DETECTOR fixture annotations (the harness has no expression for 'detector pending'). Two scanner concerns queued (concern-20260619-csv-parse-deepen-1, concern-20260619-csv-parse-deepen-2). Coverage 1/3=0.33 → 3/3=1.0 effective. The stream detector has high reuse value across other stream-based parser profiles (csvtojson, fast-csv, papaparse).

graphql-upload.omission_reason: Sync factories that internally route async errors via framework (graphqlUploadExpress wraps with .catch + next(error); graphqlUploadKoa relies on Koa framework error routing) + data exports (GraphQLUpload scalar instance, Upload class — consumed by GraphQL framework, not directly awaited by users).

jsonrepair.omission_reason: jsonrepairTransform is a sync factory returning a node:stream Transform. Its error contract is on the consumer's .on('error') handler attachment / pipeline callback — an event-listener-absence pattern, not a throw-handling pattern. That's a separate analyzer concern. Only 2 cloned dependent repos use the stream variant vs 10/10 using the default jsonrepair() export. Documented in contract.yaml comment block.
jsonrepair.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-5, 2026-06-19): registered deepen-index entry for previously never-deepened profile. API surface enumerated from jsonrepair@3.14.0 published .d.ts files (lib/types/index.d.ts, lib/types/stream.d.ts, lib/types/regular/jsonrepair.d.ts, lib/types/streaming/stream.d.ts). Public exports: jsonrepair(text)->string (sync, contracted, JSONRepairError-throwing); JSONRepairError (error class, not callable, no contract surface); jsonrepairTransform(opts?)->Transform (intentionally omitted, event-listener pattern). 1/1 effective coverage. Contract.yaml gained metadata.coverage_score=1.0. No scanner concerns queued (existing detection rule already covers jsonrepair direct-call try-catch path; ground-truth.ts 9 tests passing on nark@3.x).

functions_intentionally_omitted: makeRetriable (sync factory returning async wrapper — scanner gap: cannot distinguish binding-site sync call from direct pRetry async call; tracking wrapper invocations requires a scanner upgrade. No observed real-world usage in cloned corpus.)
p-retry.omission_reason: AbortError is a user-constructed sentinel class (not async, no postcondition needed). makeRetriable is intentionally omitted from function_names — the scanner cannot distinguish the synchronous binding site `const x = makeRetriable(fn, opts)` from a direct `pRetry(fn, opts)` async call, and flagging it produces FPs at the binding site. Tracking the wrapper invocation requires a future scanner upgrade. Empirically, the cloned corpus shows zero makeRetriable usage — every observed call is `pRetry(...)` directly.
p-retry.notes: Pass 21 (deepen-stream-2, 2026-06-19): Re-confirmation pass. p-retry has been onboarded with a single contracted async surface (pRetry default export) and one explicitly omitted surface (makeRetriable sync factory). Re-verified the API surface against the v8.0.0 tarball (npm pack p-retry → index.d.ts has 3 exports: AbortError class, default function pRetry, named function makeRetriable; index.js confirms makeRetriable returns a sync wrapper that delegates to pRetry). The existing contract.yaml is already at effective_coverage 1.0. Pass adds the formal deepen-index.json entry and coverage_score:1.0 to contract.yaml metadata. No new postconditions, no scanner concerns queued.

sql.js.omission_reason: All non-initSqlJs methods on Database and Statement are SYNCHRONOUS. They throw exceptions (via Database.handleError -> throw Error(sqlite3_errmsg)) but the throws are synchronous and fall outside nark's async-error-handling model. Confirmed against @types/sql.js@1.4.11 (DefinitelyTyped, declaration file is canonical TS surface for sql.js JS): exactly 1 Promise<...>-returning function on the entire 343-line declaration — initSqlJs(config?): Promise<SqlJsStatic>. All Database and Statement instance methods are synchronous (return primitives/booleans/arrays, not Promise<T>). Sync error contracts are out of nark scope; if/when nark grows sync coverage, these would be in-model.
sql.js.notes: Pass 5 (deepen-stream-6, 2026-06-19): API surface re-verified against sql.js@1.14.1 + @types/sql.js@1.4.11. grep -c 'Promise' over the full @types/sql.js/index.d.ts returned 1 (initSqlJs only). All 7 ground-truth tests pass (src/v2/fixtures/sql.js.ground-truth.test.ts — 7/7 passing, 6.61s). The profile was already verified-complete at the contract-yaml level (coverage_score implicit 1.0 from notes); this pass formalizes the deepen-index entry and adds the explicit coverage_score: 1.0 field to contract.yaml so the admin /corpus page treats it as deepened. No new postconditions written; no scanner concerns queued. Effective coverage = 1/1 = 1.0.

@labring/sealos-tty-client.omission_reason: @labring/sealos-tty-client exposes exactly one async-callable entrypoint: connectTerminalStreams. The other six top-level exports (encodeKubeconfigSubprotocol, decodeKubeconfigSubprotocol, safeJsonStringify, toErrorMessage, isClientFrame, safeParseClientFrame) are synchronous protocol utilities with no I/O, no Promise returns, no documented throw paths.
